model.dw.core_fact__member_events.name: core_fact__member_events
model.dw.core_fact__member_events.description:
One row per `MemberEvent` — a collaborator was added (or had their permission level changed) on a repository.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#memberevent)

- Grain: one event.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'member_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

`user_*` columns describe the **actor** (the user/admin who performed the change); `member_*` columns
describe the **target collaborator** (the account being added or modified). `changes_permission_from` / `_to`
are only populated on the `edited` action — typically null on `added`.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

member_id.name: member_id
member_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the **target collaborator** (`payload.member.id`).
The user being added to / modified on the repo — distinct from the actor in `user_id` (who performed the change).

member_node_id.name: member_node_id
member_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the target collaborator (`payload.member.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

member_name.name: member_name
member_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the target collaborator (`payload.member.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

member_type.name: member_type
member_type.description: Lowercased account type of the target collaborator (`payload.member.type`) — typically `user`.

member_user_view_type.name: member_user_view_type
member_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the target collaborator (`payload.member.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

member_gravatar_id.name: member_gravatar_id
member_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the target collaborator (`payload.member.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty for modern accounts.

member_site_admin.name: member_site_admin
member_site_admin.description: Whether the target collaborator is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.member.site_admin`). Boolean.

member_avatar_url.name: member_avatar_url
member_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the target collaborator (`payload.member.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

member_object_url.name: member_object_url
member_object_url.description: REST API URL for the target collaborator user object (`payload.member.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

member_html_url.name: member_html_url
member_html_url.description: Web URL for the target collaborator profile (`payload.member.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

member_events_url.name: member_events_url
member_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the target collaborator. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_followers_url.name: member_followers_url
member_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the target collaborator's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_following_url.name: member_following_url
member_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the target collaborator is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_gists_url.name: member_gists_url
member_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the target collaborator's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_organizations_url.name: member_organizations_url
member_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the target collaborator belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_received_events_url.name: member_received_events_url
member_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the target collaborator. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_repos_url.name: member_repos_url
member_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the target collaborator's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_starred_url.name: member_starred_url
member_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the target collaborator. Case-sensitive reference link.

member_subscriptions_url.name: member_subscriptions_url
member_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the target collaborator's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

changes_permission_from.name: changes_permission_from
changes_permission_from.description:
Previous permission level when `action = 'edited'` on a `member_event`
(`payload.changes.permission.from`, case-sensitive — e.g. `read`, `write`, `admin`).
Null on `added` and on `edited` rows where permission did not change.

changes_permission_to.name: changes_permission_to
changes_permission_to.description:
New permission level when `action = 'edited'` on a `member_event` (`payload.changes.permission.to`, case-sensitive).
Null on `added` and on `edited` rows where permission did not change.

model.dw.core_fact__member_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- member
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.id')), '') as int64) as member_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.node_id')), '') as member_node_id,                             -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.login')), '')) as member_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.type')), '')) as member_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.user_view_type')), '')) as member_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.gravatar_id')), '') as member_gravatar_id,                     -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.site_admin')), '') as bool) as member_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.avatar_url')), '') as member_avatar_url,                        -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.url')), '') as member_object_url,                              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.html_url')), '') as member_html_url,                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.events_url')), '') as member_events_url,                       -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.followers_url')), '') as member_followers_url,                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.following_url')), '') as member_following_url,                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.gists_url')), '') as member_gists_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.organizations_url')), '') as member_organizations_url,         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.received_events_url')), '') as member_received_events_url,     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.repos_url')), '') as member_repos_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.starred_url')), '') as member_starred_url,                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.member.subscriptions_url')), '') as member_subscriptions_url,         -- case-sensitive
    -- changes
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.changes.permission.from')), '') as changes_permission_from,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.changes.permission.to')), '') as changes_permission_to,               -- case-sensitive
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'member_event'

model.dw.core_fact__push_events.name: core_fact__push_events
model.dw.core_fact__push_events.description:
One row per `PushEvent` — one or more commits were pushed to a branch or tag.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#pushevent)

- Grain: one event = one push (which may bundle multiple commits — but commit-level detail is no longer
  in the public feed; see note below).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'push_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

**Payload note**: GitHub stripped `commits[]`, `size`, and `distinct_size` from the public Events feed in 2024
(per GH Archive's release notes). What remains is the minimal push metadata: `push_id`, `ref`, `head`, `before`,
`repository_id`. Commit-level analytics now require the GitHub REST API or webhook archive — not this feed.

Largest event type by volume — on a typical day, push events are ~85% of all rows in `stg_fact__events`.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

push_id.name: push_id
push_id.description:
GitHub's numeric ID for the push itself (`payload.push_id`). Distinct from `event_id`.
Globally unique. A single push that touches multiple refs may produce multiple events sharing one push context,
though in practice 1:1 with `event_id`.

ref.name: ref
ref.description:
Short git ref involved in the event (case-sensitive).
  - `create_event` / `delete_event`: the branch or tag name (e.g. `feat/login`, `v1.2.0`) — without the `refs/heads/` or `refs/tags/` prefix.
  - `push_event`: the full ref path `refs/heads/<branch>` or `refs/tags/<tag>` — note the prefix is present here, unlike create/delete.

Nullable on `create_event` when `ref_type = 'repository'` (the repo itself was created and no specific ref applies).

head.name: head
head.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the branch after the push (`payload.head`).
Lowercased for case-insensitive comparison.

before.name: before
before.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the branch before the push (`payload.before`).
Equals `0000000000000000000000000000000000000000` when the push created a new ref.

repository_id.name: repository_id
repository_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID echoed inside the push payload (`payload.repository_id`).
Should equal the event-level `repo_id` — surfaced separately for fidelity to the upstream shape.

model.dw.core_fact__push_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- push
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.push_id')), '') as int64) as push_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref')), '') as ref,         -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.head')), '')) as head,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.before')), '')) as before,
    -- repository
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.repository_id')), '') as int64) as repository_id,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'push_event'
model.dw.core_fact__push_events.compiled_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- push
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.push_id')), '') as int64) as push_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref')), '') as ref,         -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.head')), '')) as head,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.before')), '')) as before,
    -- repository
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.repository_id')), '') as int64) as repository_id,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'push_event'

model.dw.core_fact__release_events.name: core_fact__release_events
model.dw.core_fact__release_events.description:
One row per `ReleaseEvent` — a release was published in a repository.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#releaseevent)

- Grain: one event (one published release).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'release_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

Only the `published` action is emitted to the public feed (other release actions like `created` / `edited` / `deleted`
exist on the webhooks contract but are filtered out of the public events feed).
Binary release assets (e.g. compiled artifacts) are kept as a JSON ARRAY in `release_assets` rather than flattened —
release sizes vary from 0 to hundreds of assets, so flattening would distort the per-event grain.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

release_id.name: release_id
release_id.description: GitHub numeric release ID (`payload.release.id`). Globally unique, stable.

release_node_id.name: release_node_id
release_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the release (`payload.release.node_id`, case-sensitive).

release_tag_name.name: release_tag_name
release_tag_name.description: Git tag name the release is built on (`payload.release.tag_name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `v1.2.0`).

release_target_commitish.name: release_target_commitish
release_target_commitish.description:
Branch or commit SHA the release was cut from (`payload.release.target_commitish`, case-sensitive).
Typically a branch name like `main`; can be a full SHA for releases targeting a specific commit.

release_name.name: release_name
release_name.description:
Display name of the release (`payload.release.name`, case-sensitive).
Often null when the tag name is used as the display label.

release_body.name: release_body
release_body.description: Release notes markdown (`payload.release.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.

release_short_description_html.name: release_short_description_html
release_short_description_html.description:
Pre-rendered HTML snippet of the release notes (`payload.release.short_description_html`, case-sensitive).
Truncated; see `release_is_short_description_html_truncated`.

release_draft.name: release_draft
release_draft.description: Whether the release is a draft (`payload.release.draft`). Boolean — drafts rarely appear in the public feed since `published` is the only action emitted.

release_prerelease.name: release_prerelease
release_prerelease.description: Whether the release is marked as pre-release (`payload.release.prerelease`). Boolean.

release_immutable.name: release_immutable
release_immutable.description: Whether the release is marked immutable (`payload.release.immutable`). Boolean.

release_created_at.name: release_created_at
release_created_at.description: UTC datetime the release object was created (`payload.release.created_at`).

release_updated_at.name: release_updated_at
release_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the release was last updated (`payload.release.updated_at`).

release_published_at.name: release_published_at
release_published_at.description: UTC datetime the release was published (`payload.release.published_at`). The canonical "release time" for analytics.

release_object_url.name: release_object_url
release_object_url.description: REST API URL for the release object (`payload.release.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_html_url.name: release_html_url
release_html_url.description: Web URL for the release page (`payload.release.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_assets_url.name: release_assets_url
release_assets_url.description: REST API URL for the release's asset collection (`payload.release.assets_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_upload_url.name: release_upload_url
release_upload_url.description: REST API URL template for uploading new assets to the release (`payload.release.upload_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_tarball_url.name: release_tarball_url
release_tarball_url.description: URL to download the source code tarball at the release tag (`payload.release.tarball_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_zipball_url.name: release_zipball_url
release_zipball_url.description: URL to download the source code zip at the release tag (`payload.release.zipball_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_discussion_url.name: release_discussion_url
release_discussion_url.description:
Web URL for the linked Discussions thread (`payload.release.discussion_url`).
Nullable — only set when discussions are enabled and linked.

release_author_id.name: release_author_id
release_author_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the release author (`payload.release.author.id`).

release_author_node_id.name: release_author_node_id
release_author_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the release author (`payload.release.author.node_id`, case-sensitive).

release_author_name.name: release_author_name
release_author_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the release author (`payload.release.author.login`, lowered).

release_author_type.name: release_author_type
release_author_type.description: Lowercased account type of the release author (`payload.release.author.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

release_author_user_view_type.name: release_author_user_view_type
release_author_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the release author (`payload.release.author.user_view_type`).

release_author_gravatar_id.name: release_author_gravatar_id
release_author_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the release author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

release_author_site_admin.name: release_author_site_admin
release_author_site_admin.description: Whether the release author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

release_author_avatar_url.name: release_author_avatar_url
release_author_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the release author (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

release_author_object_url.name: release_author_object_url
release_author_object_url.description: REST API URL for the release author user object (`payload.release.author.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_html_url.name: release_author_html_url
release_author_html_url.description: Web URL for the release author's profile (`payload.release.author.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_events_url.name: release_author_events_url
release_author_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the release author. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_followers_url.name: release_author_followers_url
release_author_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the release author's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_following_url.name: release_author_following_url
release_author_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the release author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_gists_url.name: release_author_gists_url
release_author_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the release author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_organizations_url.name: release_author_organizations_url
release_author_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the release author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_received_events_url.name: release_author_received_events_url
release_author_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the release author. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_repos_url.name: release_author_repos_url
release_author_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the release author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_starred_url.name: release_author_starred_url
release_author_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the release author. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_author_subscriptions_url.name: release_author_subscriptions_url
release_author_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the release author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

release_assets.name: release_assets
release_assets.description:
JSON array of release asset objects (`payload.release.assets`, `ARRAY<JSON>`).
Each element describes a binary attached to the release (name, content type, size, download_count, urls, uploader, ...).
Asset count varies 0 → hundreds per release; kept as `ARRAY<JSON>` to preserve the per-event grain.
Unnest with `unnest(release_assets)` and project per-element fields via `json_value`.

model.dw.core_fact__release_events.raw_code:

    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.login')), '')) as release_author_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.type')), '')) as release_author_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.user_view_type')), '')) as release_author_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.gravatar_id')), '') as release_author_gravatar_id,                                                  -- case-sensitive

    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.avatar_url')), '') as release_author_avatar_url,                                                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.url')), '') as release_author_object_url,                                                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.html_url')), '') as release_author_html_url,                                                        -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.events_url')), '') as release_author_events_url,                                                    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.followers_url')), '') as release_author_followers_url,                                              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.following_url')), '') as release_author_following_url,                                              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.gists_url')), '') as release_author_gists_url,                                                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.organizations_url')), '') as release_author_organizations_url,                                      -- case-sensitive

where true

    and event_name = 'release_event'

    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.repos_url')), '') as release_author_repos_url,                                                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.starred_url')), '') as release_author_starred_url,                                                  -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.release.author.subscriptions_url')), '') as release_author_subscriptions_url,                                      -- case-sensitive
    -- release.assets
    json_query_array(payload, '$.release.assets') as release_assets,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'release_event'

model.dw.core_fact__watch_events.name: core_fact__watch_events
model.dw.core_fact__watch_events.description:
One row per `WatchEvent` — a user starred a repository.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#watchevent)

- Grain: one event (one star action by one user on one repo).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'watch_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

Despite the legacy "watch" name, this event represents a **star** — GitHub renamed the concept
to "starring" years ago but kept the event name for API back-compat.
The only observed `action` value is `started`. There is no `unstarred` event in the public feed.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

model.dw.core_fact__watch_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'watch_event'
model.dw.core_fact__watch_events.compiled_code:

    * except (payload),
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'watch_event'

model.dw.core_fact__commit_comment_event.name: core_fact__commit_comment_events
model.dw.core_fact__commit_comment_event.description:
One row per `CommitCommentEvent` — a comment was made on a commit (outside of any PR review).
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#commitcommentevent)

- Grain: one event = one comment created.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'commit_comment_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

Distinct from `pull_request_review_comment_event` (which is a comment on a PR diff inside a review).
The comment can be attached to a specific line+path of the commit (line-level review) or to the commit as a whole.

event_id.description:

Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.description:

(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)

Not null.

  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:

Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).

is_public.description:

cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

comment_id.name: comment_id
comment_id.description: GitHub numeric ID of the comment (`payload.comment.id`). Globally unique across all comments of this kind.

comment_node_id.name: comment_node_id
comment_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the comment (`payload.comment.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

commit_id.name: commit_id
commit_id.description:
Lowercased 40-char SHA the comment is associated with (`payload.comment.commit_id`).
Lowercased for case-insensitive join. The join key into commit-level data.
For `commit_comment_event`: SHA of the commit being commented on (stable).
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: SHA the comment was rebased onto in current state —
the original SHA the comment was first written against is in `original_commit_id`.

comment_body.name: comment_body
comment_body.description: Comment markdown body (`payload.comment.body`, case-sensitive). Can be long.

comment__path.name: comment__path
comment__path.description:
File path the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.path`, case-sensitive).
For `commit_comment_event`: null when the comment is on the commit as a whole (not a specific file).
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: always populated — the file in the PR diff the comment targets.
Note the double-underscore in the column name distinguishes it from the comment object itself.

comment_line.name: comment_line
comment_line.description:
Line number in the file the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.line`).
Used by `commit_comment_event` only. Null when commenting on a non-line target.

comment_position.name: comment_position
comment_position.description:
Diff-relative position the comment is anchored to (`payload.comment.position`).
For `commit_comment_event`: position in the commit's diff.
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: current position in the PR diff — updated when the PR head moves;
null if the comment is now outdated (the original anchor is in `comment_original_position`).

comment_created_at.name: comment_created_at
comment_created_at.description: UTC datetime the comment was created (`payload.comment.created_at`).

comment_updated_at.name: comment_updated_at
comment_updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the comment was last updated (`payload.comment.updated_at`).
Refreshed on edits and on reaction count changes.

comment_object_url.name: comment_object_url
comment_object_url.description: REST API URL for the comment object (`payload.comment.url`). Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

comment_html_url.name: comment_html_url
comment_html_url.description: Web URL for the comment in the GitHub UI (`payload.comment.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

reactions_total_count.name: reactions_total_count
reactions_total_count.description:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

reactions_plus_one_count.name: reactions_plus_one_count
reactions_plus_one_count.description: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_minus_one_count.name: reactions_minus_one_count
reactions_minus_one_count.description: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_laugh_count.name: reactions_laugh_count
reactions_laugh_count.description: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_confused_count.name: reactions_confused_count
reactions_confused_count.description: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_heart_count.name: reactions_heart_count
reactions_heart_count.description: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_hooray_count.name: reactions_hooray_count
reactions_hooray_count.description: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_rocket_count.name: reactions_rocket_count
reactions_rocket_count.description: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_eyes_count.name: reactions_eyes_count
reactions_eyes_count.description: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

model.dw.core_fact__commit_comment_event.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- comment
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.id')), '') as int64) as comment_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.node_id')), '') as comment_node_id,   -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.commit_id')), '')) as commit_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.body')), '') as comment_body,         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.path')), '') as comment__path,        -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.line')), '') as int64) as comment_line,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.position')), '') as int64) as comment_position,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.created_at')), '') as timestamp)) as comment_created_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.updated_at')), '') as timestamp)) as comment_updated_at,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.url')), '') as comment_object_url,    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.html_url')), '') as comment_html_url, -- case-sensitive
    -- comment.reactions
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.total_count')), '') as int64) as reactions_total_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."+1"')), '') as int64) as reactions_plus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."-1"')), '') as int64) as reactions_minus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.laugh')), '') as int64) as reactions_laugh_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.confused')), '') as int64) as reactions_confused_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.heart')), '') as int64) as reactions_heart_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.hooray')), '') as int64) as reactions_hooray_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.rocket')), '') as int64) as reactions_rocket_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.eyes')), '') as int64) as reactions_eyes_count,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'commit_comment_event'
