    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."+1"')), '') as int64) as reactions_plus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."-1"')), '') as int64) as reactions_minus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.laugh')), '') as int64) as reactions_laugh_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.confused')), '') as int64) as reactions_confused_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.heart')), '') as int64) as reactions_heart_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.hooray')), '') as int64) as reactions_hooray_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.rocket')), '') as int64) as reactions_rocket_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.eyes')), '') as int64) as reactions_eyes_count,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'commit_comment_event'

model.dw.core_fact__issue_comment_events.name: core_fact__issue_comment_events
model.dw.core_fact__issue_comment_events.description:
One row per `IssueCommentEvent` — a comment was added to an issue or pull request.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#issuecommentevent)

- Grain: one event = one comment.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'issue_comment_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

The full issue snapshot is included under `issue_*` — same shape as `core_fact__issues_events`.
To disambiguate "comment on an issue" vs "comment on a PR", check whether `issue_pull_request_object_url IS NOT NULL`
(PR-style issues). Only the `created` action is meaningfully observed in the GH Archive public feed —
`edited` / `deleted` are filtered out upstream.

`changes_body_from` is populated only on `action = 'edited'`.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.description:
GitHub numeric issue ID (`payload.issue.id`).
Globally unique across all repositories, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into issue-level dimensions.

issue_node_id.name: issue_node_id
issue_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the issue (`payload.issue.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

issue_number.name: issue_number
issue_number.description:
Issue number within its repository (`payload.issue.number`). NOT globally unique — only unique per repo.
Use `(repo_id, issue_number)` together, or `issue_id` alone for global uniqueness.

issue_body.name: issue_body
issue_body.description:
Issue body markdown (`payload.issue.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.
Nullable when the issue was opened without a description.

issue_state.name: issue_state
issue_state.description:
Lowercased open/closed state of the issue (`payload.issue.state`).
Observed values: `open`, `closed`.

issue_state_reason.name: issue_state_reason
issue_state_reason.description:
Lowercased reason the issue is in its current state (`payload.issue.state_reason`).
Observed values: `completed`, `not_planned`, `reopened`, `duplicate`. Null when not applicable.

issue_author_association.name: issue_author_association
issue_author_association.description:
Lowercased relationship of the issue author to the repository (`payload.issue.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`, `first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`.
Typically null in GH Archive's public feed — included for parity with the upstream schema.

issue_active_lock_reason.name: issue_active_lock_reason
issue_active_lock_reason.description:
Lowercased reason this issue is locked from comments (`payload.issue.active_lock_reason`).
Observed values: `resolved`, `spam`, `off-topic`, `too heated`. Null when the issue is not locked.

issue_locked.name: issue_locked
issue_locked.description: Whether the issue is currently locked from new comments (`payload.issue.locked`). Boolean.

issue_draft.name: issue_draft
issue_draft.description:
Whether the issue is a draft (`payload.issue.draft`). Boolean.
Only meaningful when the row represents a pull-request-style issue
(see `issue_pull_request_object_url` for the discriminator).

issue_comments.name: issue_comments
issue_comments.description: Count of comments currently on the issue (`payload.issue.comments`). Snapshot at event time.

issue_labels.name: issue_labels
issue_labels.description:
JSON array of label objects attached to the issue (`payload.issue.labels`).
Each element follows the label-object shape (`id`, `name`, `color`, `description`, ...).
Stored as `array<json>` — unnest with `unnest(issue_labels)` and project per-element fields via `json_value`.

issue_assignees.name: issue_assignees
issue_assignees.description:
JSON array of assignee user objects on the issue (`payload.issue.assignees`).
Each element follows the user-object shape.
Stored as `array<json>` — unnest and project as needed.

issue_created_at.name: issue_created_at
issue_created_at.description: UTC datetime the issue was created (`payload.issue.created_at`). DATETIME, not TIMESTAMP.

issue_updated_at.name: issue_updated_at
issue_updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the issue was last updated (`payload.issue.updated_at`).
Refreshed on any state/labels/comments change.

issue_closed_at.name: issue_closed_at
issue_closed_at.description:
UTC datetime the issue was most recently closed (`payload.issue.closed_at`).
Null when `issue_state = 'open'`.

issue_object_url.name: issue_object_url
issue_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the issue (`payload.issue.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/issues/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_html_url.name: issue_html_url
issue_html_url.description: GitHub web URL for the issue page (`payload.issue.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_repository_url.name: issue_repository_url
issue_repository_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the parent repository (`payload.issue.repository_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_labels_url.name: issue_labels_url
issue_labels_url.description: REST API URL template for the issue's labels (`payload.issue.labels_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_comments_url.name: issue_comments_url
issue_comments_url.description: REST API URL for the issue's comments collection (`payload.issue.comments_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_events_url.name: issue_events_url
issue_events_url.description: REST API URL for the issue's timeline events (`payload.issue.events_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_timeline_url.name: issue_timeline_url
issue_timeline_url.description: REST API URL for the issue's full timeline (`payload.issue.timeline_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_id.name: issue_user_id
issue_user_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.id`). Stable across login renames.

issue_user_node_id.name: issue_user_node_id
issue_user_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_user_name.name: issue_user_name
issue_user_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

issue_user_type.name: issue_user_type
issue_user_type.description:
Lowercased account type of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`, `organization`, `mannequin`.

issue_user_user_view_type.name: issue_user_user_view_type
issue_user_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility for the issue author (`payload.issue.user.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

issue_user_gravatar_id.name: issue_user_gravatar_id
issue_user_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier (`payload.issue.user.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

issue_user_site_admin.name: issue_user_site_admin
issue_user_site_admin.description: Whether the issue author is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.user.site_admin`). Boolean.

issue_user_avatar_url.name: issue_user_avatar_url
issue_user_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the issue author (`payload.issue.user.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

issue_user_object_url.name: issue_user_object_url
issue_user_object_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author user object (`payload.issue.user.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_html_url.name: issue_user_html_url
issue_user_html_url.description: Web URL for the issue author's profile (`payload.issue.user.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_events_url.name: issue_user_events_url
issue_user_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_followers_url.name: issue_user_followers_url
issue_user_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_following_url.name: issue_user_following_url
issue_user_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the issue author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_gists_url.name: issue_user_gists_url
issue_user_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the issue author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_organizations_url.name: issue_user_organizations_url
issue_user_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the issue author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_received_events_url.name: issue_user_received_events_url
issue_user_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_repos_url.name: issue_user_repos_url
issue_user_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_starred_url.name: issue_user_starred_url
issue_user_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_subscriptions_url.name: issue_user_subscriptions_url
issue_user_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_assignee_id.name: issue_assignee_id
issue_assignee_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the issue's primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.id`).
Null when the issue has no assignee. For the full list of assignees see `issue_assignees`.

issue_assignee_node_id.name: issue_assignee_node_id
issue_assignee_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_assignee_name.name: issue_assignee_name
issue_assignee_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.login`, lowered).

issue_assignee_type.name: issue_assignee_type
issue_assignee_type.description:
Lowercased account type of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`.

issue_assignee_user_view_type.name: issue_assignee_user_view_type
issue_assignee_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility for the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.user_view_type`).

issue_assignee_site_admin.name: issue_assignee_site_admin
issue_assignee_site_admin.description: Whether the primary assignee is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.assignee.site_admin`). Boolean.

issue_assignee_avatar_url.name: issue_assignee_avatar_url
issue_assignee_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.avatar_url`, case-sensitive).

issue_assignee_object_url.name: issue_assignee_object_url
issue_assignee_object_url.description: REST API URL for the primary assignee user object (`payload.issue.assignee.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_assignee_html_url.name: issue_assignee_html_url
issue_assignee_html_url.description: Web URL for the primary assignee's profile page (`payload.issue.assignee.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_id.name: issue_milestone_id
issue_milestone_id.description: GitHub numeric milestone ID attached to the issue (`payload.issue.milestone.id`). Null when no milestone is set.

issue_milestone_node_id.name: issue_milestone_node_id
issue_milestone_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_milestone_number.name: issue_milestone_number
issue_milestone_number.description: Milestone number within its repository (`payload.issue.milestone.number`). NOT globally unique — `(repo_id, milestone_number)` is the natural key.

issue_milestone_open_issues.name: issue_milestone_open_issues
issue_milestone_open_issues.description: Count of open issues currently tied to this milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.open_issues`). Snapshot at event time.

issue_milestone_closed_issues.name: issue_milestone_closed_issues
issue_milestone_closed_issues.description: Count of closed issues currently tied to this milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.closed_issues`). Snapshot at event time.

issue_milestone_due_on.name: issue_milestone_due_on
issue_milestone_due_on.description: UTC datetime the milestone is due (`payload.issue.milestone.due_on`). Nullable.

issue_milestone_created_at.name: issue_milestone_created_at
issue_milestone_created_at.description: UTC datetime the milestone was created (`payload.issue.milestone.created_at`).

issue_milestone_updated_at.name: issue_milestone_updated_at
issue_milestone_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the milestone was last updated (`payload.issue.milestone.updated_at`).

issue_milestone_closed_at.name: issue_milestone_closed_at
issue_milestone_closed_at.description: UTC datetime the milestone was most recently closed (`payload.issue.milestone.closed_at`). Null while open.

issue_milestone_object_url.name: issue_milestone_object_url
issue_milestone_object_url.description: REST API URL for the milestone object (`payload.issue.milestone.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_html_url.name: issue_milestone_html_url
issue_milestone_html_url.description: Web URL for the milestone page (`payload.issue.milestone.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_labels_url.name: issue_milestone_labels_url
issue_milestone_labels_url.description: REST API URL for the milestone's associated labels (`payload.issue.milestone.labels_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_creator_id.name: issue_milestone_creator_id
issue_milestone_creator_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the user who created the milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.id`).

issue_milestone_creator_node_id.name: issue_milestone_creator_node_id
issue_milestone_creator_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_milestone_creator_name.name: issue_milestone_creator_name
issue_milestone_creator_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.login`, lowered).

issue_milestone_creator_type.name: issue_milestone_creator_type
issue_milestone_creator_type.description: Lowercased account type of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.type`).

issue_milestone_creator_site_admin.name: issue_milestone_creator_site_admin
issue_milestone_creator_site_admin.description: Whether the milestone creator is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.site_admin`). Boolean.

issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url.name: issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url
issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.avatar_url`, case-sensitive).

issue_milestone_creator_object_url.name: issue_milestone_creator_object_url
issue_milestone_creator_object_url.description: REST API URL for the milestone creator user object (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_creator_html_url.name: issue_milestone_creator_html_url
issue_milestone_creator_html_url.description: Web URL for the milestone creator's profile page (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_object_url.name: issue_pull_request_object_url
issue_pull_request_object_url.description:
REST API URL of the linked pull request (`payload.issue.pull_request.url`, case-sensitive).
**Presence (non-null) of this field is the canonical discriminator that the issue row actually represents a pull request** — GitHub models PRs as a specialization of issues.

issue_pull_request_html_url.name: issue_pull_request_html_url
issue_pull_request_html_url.description: Web URL of the linked pull request (`payload.issue.pull_request.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_diff_url.name: issue_pull_request_diff_url
issue_pull_request_diff_url.description: URL to the PR's diff view (`payload.issue.pull_request.diff_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_patch_url.name: issue_pull_request_patch_url
issue_pull_request_patch_url.description: URL to the PR's patch file (`payload.issue.pull_request.patch_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_merged_at.name: issue_pull_request_merged_at
issue_pull_request_merged_at.description:
UTC datetime the linked pull request was merged (`payload.issue.pull_request.merged_at`).
Null while the PR is unmerged. Only populated on PR-style issues.

issue_reactions_total_count.name: issue_reactions_total_count
issue_reactions_total_count.description:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

issue_reactions_plus_one_count.name: issue_reactions_plus_one_count
issue_reactions_plus_one_count.description: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_minus_one_count.name: issue_reactions_minus_one_count
issue_reactions_minus_one_count.description: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_laugh_count.name: issue_reactions_laugh_count
issue_reactions_laugh_count.description: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_confused_count.name: issue_reactions_confused_count
issue_reactions_confused_count.description: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_heart_count.name: issue_reactions_heart_count
issue_reactions_heart_count.description: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_hooray_count.name: issue_reactions_hooray_count
issue_reactions_hooray_count.description: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_rocket_count.name: issue_reactions_rocket_count
issue_reactions_rocket_count.description: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_eyes_count.name: issue_reactions_eyes_count
issue_reactions_eyes_count.description: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_id.name: comment_id
comment_id.description: GitHub numeric ID of the comment (`payload.comment.id`). Globally unique across all comments of this kind.

comment_node_id.name: comment_node_id
comment_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the comment (`payload.comment.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

comment_body.name: comment_body
comment_body.description: Comment markdown body (`payload.comment.body`, case-sensitive). Can be long.

comment_author_association.name: comment_author_association
comment_author_association.description:
Lowercased relationship of the comment author to the repo (`payload.comment.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`,
`first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`. Typically null in the GH Archive public feed.

comment_created_at.name: comment_created_at
comment_created_at.description: UTC datetime the comment was created (`payload.comment.created_at`).

comment_updated_at.name: comment_updated_at
comment_updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the comment was last updated (`payload.comment.updated_at`).
Refreshed on edits and on reaction count changes.

comment_object_url.name: comment_object_url
comment_object_url.description: REST API URL for the comment object (`payload.comment.url`). Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

comment_html_url.name: comment_html_url
comment_html_url.description: Web URL for the comment in the GitHub UI (`payload.comment.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_issue_url.name: comment_issue_url
comment_issue_url.description: REST API URL of the parent issue/PR the comment lives on (`payload.comment.issue_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_user_id.name: comment_user_id
comment_user_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.id`). Should equal event-level `user_id`.

comment_user_node_id.name: comment_user_node_id
comment_user_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

comment_user_name.name: comment_user_name
comment_user_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.login`, lowered).

comment_user_type.name: comment_user_type
comment_user_type.description: Lowercased account type of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

comment_user_user_view_type.name: comment_user_user_view_type
comment_user_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

comment_user_gravatar_id.name: comment_user_gravatar_id
comment_user_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the comment author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

comment_user_site_admin.name: comment_user_site_admin
comment_user_site_admin.description: Whether the comment author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

comment_user_avatar_url.name: comment_user_avatar_url
comment_user_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the comment author (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

comment_user_object_url.name: comment_user_object_url
comment_user_object_url.description: REST API URL for the comment author user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_user_html_url.name: comment_user_html_url
comment_user_html_url.description: Web URL for the comment author's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_reactions_total_count.name: comment_reactions_total_count
comment_reactions_total_count.description:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

comment_reactions_plus_one_count.name: comment_reactions_plus_one_count
comment_reactions_plus_one_count.description: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_minus_one_count.name: comment_reactions_minus_one_count
comment_reactions_minus_one_count.description: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_laugh_count.name: comment_reactions_laugh_count
comment_reactions_laugh_count.description: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_confused_count.name: comment_reactions_confused_count
comment_reactions_confused_count.description: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_heart_count.name: comment_reactions_heart_count
comment_reactions_heart_count.description: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_hooray_count.name: comment_reactions_hooray_count
comment_reactions_hooray_count.description: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_rocket_count.name: comment_reactions_rocket_count
comment_reactions_rocket_count.description: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

comment_reactions_eyes_count.name: comment_reactions_eyes_count
comment_reactions_eyes_count.description: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

changes_body_from.name: changes_body_from
changes_body_from.description:
Previous body markdown when `action = 'edited'` and the body changed (`payload.changes.body.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event`, `issue_comment_event`, and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'issue_comment_event'

    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.starred_url')), '') as issue_user_starred_url,                                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.subscriptions_url')), '') as issue_user_subscriptions_url,                         -- case-sensitive

    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.html_url')), '') as issue_milestone_html_url,                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.labels_url')), '') as issue_milestone_labels_url,                             -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.milestone.creator
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.id')), '') as int64) as issue_milestone_creator_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.node_id')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_node_id,                   -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.login')), '')) as issue_milestone_creator_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.type')), '')) as issue_milestone_creator_type,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.site_admin')), '') as bool) as issue_milestone_creator_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.avatar_url')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url,              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.url')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_object_url,                    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.html_url')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_html_url,                 -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.pull_request
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.url')), '') as issue_pull_request_object_url,                              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.html_url')), '') as issue_pull_request_html_url,                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.diff_url')), '') as issue_pull_request_diff_url,                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.patch_url')), '') as issue_pull_request_patch_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.merged_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_pull_request_merged_at,
    -- issue.reactions
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.total_count')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_total_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions."+1"')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_plus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions."-1"')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_minus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.laugh')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_laugh_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.confused')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_confused_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.heart')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_heart_count,

    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.rocket')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_rocket_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.eyes')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_eyes_count,
    -- comment

    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.node_id')), '') as comment_node_id,                                                   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.body')), '') as comment_body,                                                         -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.author_association')), '')) as comment_author_association,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.created_at')), '') as timestamp)) as comment_created_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.updated_at')), '') as timestamp)) as comment_updated_at,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.url')), '') as comment_object_url,                                                    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.html_url')), '') as comment_html_url,                                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.issue_url')), '') as comment_issue_url,                                               -- case-sensitive
    -- comment.user
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.id')), '') as int64) as comment_user_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.node_id')), '') as comment_user_node_id,                                         -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.login')), '')) as comment_user_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.type')), '')) as comment_user_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.user_view_type')), '')) as comment_user_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.gravatar_id')), '') as comment_user_gravatar_id,                                 -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.site_admin')), '') as bool) as comment_user_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.avatar_url')), '') as comment_user_avatar_url,                                    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.url')), '') as comment_user_object_url,                                          -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.html_url')), '') as comment_user_html_url,                                       -- case-sensitive
    -- comment.reactions
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.total_count')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_total_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."+1"')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_plus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."-1"')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_minus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.laugh')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_laugh_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.confused')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_confused_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.heart')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_heart_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.hooray')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_hooray_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.rocket')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_rocket_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.eyes')), '') as int64) as comment_reactions_eyes_count,
