model.dw.core_fact__issues_events.name: core_fact__issues_events
model.dw.core_fact__issues_events.description:
One row per `IssuesEvent` — an action was taken on an issue.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#issuesevent)

- Grain: one event (one action on one issue).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'issues_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

The issue snapshot under `issue_*` is captured at event time — `issue_state`, `issue_comments`, `issue_updated_at`
etc. reflect state as-of when the event fired.
To identify PR-style issues, check whether `issue_pull_request_object_url IS NOT NULL` — GitHub models PRs as
a specialization of issues, so PR activity also flows through `IssuesEvent` for actions like `assigned` / `labeled`.

`label_*` / `labels` columns are populated only on `labeled` / `unlabeled` actions;
`assignee_*` columns only on `assigned` / `unassigned`.
`changes_*` columns only on `edited`.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.description:
GitHub numeric issue ID (`payload.issue.id`).
Globally unique across all repositories, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into issue-level dimensions.

issue_node_id.name: issue_node_id
issue_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the issue (`payload.issue.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

issue_number.name: issue_number
issue_number.description:
Issue number within its repository (`payload.issue.number`). NOT globally unique — only unique per repo.
Use `(repo_id, issue_number)` together, or `issue_id` alone for global uniqueness.

issue_body.name: issue_body
issue_body.description:
Issue body markdown (`payload.issue.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.
Nullable when the issue was opened without a description.

issue_state.name: issue_state
issue_state.description:
Lowercased open/closed state of the issue (`payload.issue.state`).
Observed values: `open`, `closed`.

issue_state_reason.name: issue_state_reason
issue_state_reason.description:
Lowercased reason the issue is in its current state (`payload.issue.state_reason`).
Observed values: `completed`, `not_planned`, `reopened`, `duplicate`. Null when not applicable.

issue_author_association.name: issue_author_association
issue_author_association.description:
Lowercased relationship of the issue author to the repository (`payload.issue.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`, `first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`.
Typically null in GH Archive's public feed — included for parity with the upstream schema.

issue_active_lock_reason.name: issue_active_lock_reason
issue_active_lock_reason.description:
Lowercased reason this issue is locked from comments (`payload.issue.active_lock_reason`).
Observed values: `resolved`, `spam`, `off-topic`, `too heated`. Null when the issue is not locked.

issue_locked.name: issue_locked
issue_locked.description: Whether the issue is currently locked from new comments (`payload.issue.locked`). Boolean.

issue_draft.name: issue_draft
issue_draft.description:
Whether the issue is a draft (`payload.issue.draft`). Boolean.
Only meaningful when the row represents a pull-request-style issue
(see `issue_pull_request_object_url` for the discriminator).

issue_comments.name: issue_comments
issue_comments.description: Count of comments currently on the issue (`payload.issue.comments`). Snapshot at event time.

issue_labels.name: issue_labels
issue_labels.description:
JSON array of label objects attached to the issue (`payload.issue.labels`).
Each element follows the label-object shape (`id`, `name`, `color`, `description`, ...).
Stored as `array<json>` — unnest with `unnest(issue_labels)` and project per-element fields via `json_value`.

issue_assignees.name: issue_assignees
issue_assignees.description:
JSON array of assignee user objects on the issue (`payload.issue.assignees`).
Each element follows the user-object shape.
Stored as `array<json>` — unnest and project as needed.

issue_created_at.name: issue_created_at
issue_created_at.description: UTC datetime the issue was created (`payload.issue.created_at`). DATETIME, not TIMESTAMP.

issue_updated_at.name: issue_updated_at
issue_updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the issue was last updated (`payload.issue.updated_at`).
Refreshed on any state/labels/comments change.

issue_closed_at.name: issue_closed_at
issue_closed_at.description:
UTC datetime the issue was most recently closed (`payload.issue.closed_at`).
Null when `issue_state = 'open'`.

issue_object_url.name: issue_object_url
issue_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the issue (`payload.issue.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/issues/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_html_url.name: issue_html_url
issue_html_url.description: GitHub web URL for the issue page (`payload.issue.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_repository_url.name: issue_repository_url
issue_repository_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the parent repository (`payload.issue.repository_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_labels_url.name: issue_labels_url
issue_labels_url.description: REST API URL template for the issue's labels (`payload.issue.labels_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_comments_url.name: issue_comments_url
issue_comments_url.description: REST API URL for the issue's comments collection (`payload.issue.comments_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_events_url.name: issue_events_url
issue_events_url.description: REST API URL for the issue's timeline events (`payload.issue.events_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_timeline_url.name: issue_timeline_url
issue_timeline_url.description: REST API URL for the issue's full timeline (`payload.issue.timeline_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_id.name: issue_user_id
issue_user_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.id`). Stable across login renames.

issue_user_node_id.name: issue_user_node_id
issue_user_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_user_name.name: issue_user_name
issue_user_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

issue_user_type.name: issue_user_type
issue_user_type.description:
Lowercased account type of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`, `organization`, `mannequin`.

issue_user_user_view_type.name: issue_user_user_view_type
issue_user_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility for the issue author (`payload.issue.user.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

issue_user_gravatar_id.name: issue_user_gravatar_id
issue_user_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier (`payload.issue.user.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

issue_user_site_admin.name: issue_user_site_admin
issue_user_site_admin.description: Whether the issue author is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.user.site_admin`). Boolean.

issue_user_avatar_url.name: issue_user_avatar_url
issue_user_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the issue author (`payload.issue.user.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

issue_user_object_url.name: issue_user_object_url
issue_user_object_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author user object (`payload.issue.user.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_html_url.name: issue_user_html_url
issue_user_html_url.description: Web URL for the issue author's profile (`payload.issue.user.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_events_url.name: issue_user_events_url
issue_user_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_followers_url.name: issue_user_followers_url
issue_user_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_following_url.name: issue_user_following_url
issue_user_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the issue author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_gists_url.name: issue_user_gists_url
issue_user_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the issue author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_organizations_url.name: issue_user_organizations_url
issue_user_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the issue author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_received_events_url.name: issue_user_received_events_url
issue_user_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_repos_url.name: issue_user_repos_url
issue_user_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_starred_url.name: issue_user_starred_url
issue_user_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_user_subscriptions_url.name: issue_user_subscriptions_url
issue_user_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the issue author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_assignee_id.name: issue_assignee_id
issue_assignee_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the issue's primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.id`).
Null when the issue has no assignee. For the full list of assignees see `issue_assignees`.

issue_assignee_node_id.name: issue_assignee_node_id
issue_assignee_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_assignee_name.name: issue_assignee_name
issue_assignee_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.login`, lowered).

issue_assignee_type.name: issue_assignee_type
issue_assignee_type.description:
Lowercased account type of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`.

issue_assignee_user_view_type.name: issue_assignee_user_view_type
issue_assignee_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility for the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.user_view_type`).

issue_assignee_site_admin.name: issue_assignee_site_admin
issue_assignee_site_admin.description: Whether the primary assignee is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.assignee.site_admin`). Boolean.

issue_assignee_avatar_url.name: issue_assignee_avatar_url
issue_assignee_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.avatar_url`, case-sensitive).

issue_assignee_object_url.name: issue_assignee_object_url
issue_assignee_object_url.description: REST API URL for the primary assignee user object (`payload.issue.assignee.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_assignee_html_url.name: issue_assignee_html_url
issue_assignee_html_url.description: Web URL for the primary assignee's profile page (`payload.issue.assignee.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_id.name: issue_milestone_id
issue_milestone_id.description: GitHub numeric milestone ID attached to the issue (`payload.issue.milestone.id`). Null when no milestone is set.

issue_milestone_node_id.name: issue_milestone_node_id
issue_milestone_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_milestone_number.name: issue_milestone_number
issue_milestone_number.description: Milestone number within its repository (`payload.issue.milestone.number`). NOT globally unique — `(repo_id, milestone_number)` is the natural key.

issue_milestone_open_issues.name: issue_milestone_open_issues
issue_milestone_open_issues.description: Count of open issues currently tied to this milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.open_issues`). Snapshot at event time.

issue_milestone_closed_issues.name: issue_milestone_closed_issues
issue_milestone_closed_issues.description: Count of closed issues currently tied to this milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.closed_issues`). Snapshot at event time.

issue_milestone_due_on.name: issue_milestone_due_on
issue_milestone_due_on.description: UTC datetime the milestone is due (`payload.issue.milestone.due_on`). Nullable.

issue_milestone_created_at.name: issue_milestone_created_at
issue_milestone_created_at.description: UTC datetime the milestone was created (`payload.issue.milestone.created_at`).

issue_milestone_updated_at.name: issue_milestone_updated_at
issue_milestone_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the milestone was last updated (`payload.issue.milestone.updated_at`).

issue_milestone_closed_at.name: issue_milestone_closed_at
issue_milestone_closed_at.description: UTC datetime the milestone was most recently closed (`payload.issue.milestone.closed_at`). Null while open.

issue_milestone_object_url.name: issue_milestone_object_url
issue_milestone_object_url.description: REST API URL for the milestone object (`payload.issue.milestone.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_html_url.name: issue_milestone_html_url
issue_milestone_html_url.description: Web URL for the milestone page (`payload.issue.milestone.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_labels_url.name: issue_milestone_labels_url
issue_milestone_labels_url.description: REST API URL for the milestone's associated labels (`payload.issue.milestone.labels_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_creator_id.name: issue_milestone_creator_id
issue_milestone_creator_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the user who created the milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.id`).

issue_milestone_creator_node_id.name: issue_milestone_creator_node_id
issue_milestone_creator_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.node_id`, case-sensitive).

issue_milestone_creator_name.name: issue_milestone_creator_name
issue_milestone_creator_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.login`, lowered).

issue_milestone_creator_type.name: issue_milestone_creator_type
issue_milestone_creator_type.description: Lowercased account type of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.type`).

issue_milestone_creator_site_admin.name: issue_milestone_creator_site_admin
issue_milestone_creator_site_admin.description: Whether the milestone creator is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.site_admin`). Boolean.

issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url.name: issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url
issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.avatar_url`, case-sensitive).

issue_milestone_creator_object_url.name: issue_milestone_creator_object_url
issue_milestone_creator_object_url.description: REST API URL for the milestone creator user object (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_milestone_creator_html_url.name: issue_milestone_creator_html_url
issue_milestone_creator_html_url.description: Web URL for the milestone creator's profile page (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_object_url.name: issue_pull_request_object_url
issue_pull_request_object_url.description:
REST API URL of the linked pull request (`payload.issue.pull_request.url`, case-sensitive).
**Presence (non-null) of this field is the canonical discriminator that the issue row actually represents a pull request** — GitHub models PRs as a specialization of issues.

issue_pull_request_html_url.name: issue_pull_request_html_url
issue_pull_request_html_url.description: Web URL of the linked pull request (`payload.issue.pull_request.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_diff_url.name: issue_pull_request_diff_url
issue_pull_request_diff_url.description: URL to the PR's diff view (`payload.issue.pull_request.diff_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_patch_url.name: issue_pull_request_patch_url
issue_pull_request_patch_url.description: URL to the PR's patch file (`payload.issue.pull_request.patch_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

issue_pull_request_merged_at.name: issue_pull_request_merged_at
issue_pull_request_merged_at.description:
UTC datetime the linked pull request was merged (`payload.issue.pull_request.merged_at`).
Null while the PR is unmerged. Only populated on PR-style issues.

issue_reactions_total_count.name: issue_reactions_total_count
issue_reactions_total_count.description:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

issue_reactions_plus_one_count.name: issue_reactions_plus_one_count
issue_reactions_plus_one_count.description: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_minus_one_count.name: issue_reactions_minus_one_count
issue_reactions_minus_one_count.description: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_laugh_count.name: issue_reactions_laugh_count
issue_reactions_laugh_count.description: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_confused_count.name: issue_reactions_confused_count
issue_reactions_confused_count.description: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_heart_count.name: issue_reactions_heart_count
issue_reactions_heart_count.description: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_hooray_count.name: issue_reactions_hooray_count
issue_reactions_hooray_count.description: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_rocket_count.name: issue_reactions_rocket_count
issue_reactions_rocket_count.description: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

issue_reactions_eyes_count.name: issue_reactions_eyes_count
issue_reactions_eyes_count.description: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

assignee_id.name: assignee_id
assignee_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the assignee (`payload.assignee.id`).
Stable across login renames. Populated only when `action in ('assigned', 'unassigned')`.

assignee_node_id.name: assignee_node_id
assignee_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the assignee user (`payload.assignee.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

assignee_name.name: assignee_name
assignee_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the assignee (`payload.assignee.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

assignee_type.name: assignee_type
assignee_type.description:
Lowercased account type of the assignee (`payload.assignee.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`, `organization`, `mannequin`.

assignee_user_view_type.name: assignee_user_view_type
assignee_user_view_type.description:
Lowercased visibility flag for the assignee account
(`payload.assignee.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

assignee_site_admin.name: assignee_site_admin
assignee_site_admin.description: Whether the assignee is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.assignee.site_admin`). Boolean.

assignee_avatar_url.name: assignee_avatar_url
assignee_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the assignee (`payload.assignee.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

assignee_object_url.name: assignee_object_url
assignee_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the assignee user object (`payload.assignee.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

assignee_html_url.name: assignee_html_url
assignee_html_url.description:
GitHub web URL for the assignee profile page (`payload.assignee.html_url`).
Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

label_id.name: label_id
label_id.description:
GitHub numeric label ID (`payload.label.id`). Stable across renames.
Populated only when `action in ('labeled', 'unlabeled')`; null otherwise.

label_node_id.name: label_node_id
label_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the label (`payload.label.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

label_name.name: label_name
label_name.description:
Display name of the label (`payload.label.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `bug`, `good first issue`).
This is what users see in the GitHub UI. Use `label_id` as the durable join key.

label_description.name: label_description
label_description.description: Free-text description of the label (`payload.label.description`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

label_color.name: label_color
label_color.description:
6-character hex color of the label without the leading `#` (`payload.label.color`, e.g. `d73a4a`).
Case-sensitive but conventionally lowercased.

label_default.name: label_default
label_default.description:
Whether this label is one of GitHub's default labels for new repositories
(`payload.label.default`). Boolean.

label_object_url.name: label_object_url
label_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the label object (`payload.label.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/labels/<name>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

changes_title_from.name: changes_title_from
changes_title_from.description:
Previous title text when `action = 'edited'` and the title changed (`payload.changes.title.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event` and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

changes_body_from.name: changes_body_from
changes_body_from.description:
Previous body markdown when `action = 'edited'` and the body changed (`payload.changes.body.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event`, `issue_comment_event`, and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

model.dw.core_fact__issues_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- issue
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.id')), '') as int64) as issue_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.node_id')), '') as issue_node_id,                                                       -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.number')), '') as int64) as issue_number,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.title')), '') as issue_title,                                                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.body')), '') as issue_body,                                                             -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.state')), '')) as issue_state,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.state_reason')), '')) as issue_state_reason,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.author_association')), '')) as issue_author_association,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.active_lock_reason')), '')) as issue_active_lock_reason,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.locked')), '') as bool) as issue_locked,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.draft')), '') as bool) as issue_draft,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.comments')), '') as int64) as issue_comments,
    json_query_array(payload, '$.issue.labels') as issue_labels,
    json_query_array(payload, '$.issue.assignees') as issue_assignees,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.created_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_created_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.updated_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_updated_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.closed_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_closed_at,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.url')), '') as issue_object_url,                                                        -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.html_url')), '') as issue_html_url,                                                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.repository_url')), '') as issue_repository_url,                                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.labels_url')), '') as issue_labels_url,                                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.comments_url')), '') as issue_comments_url,                                             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.events_url')), '') as issue_events_url,                                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.timeline_url')), '') as issue_timeline_url,                                             -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.user
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.id')), '') as int64) as issue_user_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.node_id')), '') as issue_user_node_id,                                             -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.login')), '')) as issue_user_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.type')), '')) as issue_user_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.user_view_type')), '')) as issue_user_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.gravatar_id')), '') as issue_user_gravatar_id,                                     -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.site_admin')), '') as bool) as issue_user_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.avatar_url')), '') as issue_user_avatar_url,                                        -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.url')), '') as issue_user_object_url,                                              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.html_url')), '') as issue_user_html_url,                                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.events_url')), '') as issue_user_events_url,                                       -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.followers_url')), '') as issue_user_followers_url,                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.following_url')), '') as issue_user_following_url,                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.gists_url')), '') as issue_user_gists_url,                                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.organizations_url')), '') as issue_user_organizations_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.received_events_url')), '') as issue_user_received_events_url,                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.repos_url')), '') as issue_user_repos_url,                                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.starred_url')), '') as issue_user_starred_url,                                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.user.subscriptions_url')), '') as issue_user_subscriptions_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.assignee
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.id')), '') as int64) as issue_assignee_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.node_id')), '') as issue_assignee_node_id,                                     -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.login')), '')) as issue_assignee_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.type')), '')) as issue_assignee_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.user_view_type')), '')) as issue_assignee_user_view_type,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.site_admin')), '') as bool) as issue_assignee_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.avatar_url')), '') as issue_assignee_avatar_url,                                -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.url')), '') as issue_assignee_object_url,                                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.assignee.html_url')), '') as issue_assignee_html_url,                                   -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.milestone
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.id')), '') as int64) as issue_milestone_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.node_id')), '') as issue_milestone_node_id,                                   -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.number')), '') as int64) as issue_milestone_number,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.title')), '') as issue_milestone_title,                                       -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.description')), '') as issue_milestone_description,                           -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.state')), '')) as issue_milestone_state,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.open_issues')), '') as int64) as issue_milestone_open_issues,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.closed_issues')), '') as int64) as issue_milestone_closed_issues,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.due_on')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_milestone_due_on,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.created_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_milestone_created_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.updated_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_milestone_updated_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.closed_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_milestone_closed_at,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.url')), '') as issue_milestone_object_url,                                    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.html_url')), '') as issue_milestone_html_url,                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.labels_url')), '') as issue_milestone_labels_url,                             -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.milestone.creator
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.id')), '') as int64) as issue_milestone_creator_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.node_id')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_node_id,                   -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.login')), '')) as issue_milestone_creator_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.type')), '')) as issue_milestone_creator_type,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.site_admin')), '') as bool) as issue_milestone_creator_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.avatar_url')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_avatar_url,              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.url')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_object_url,                    -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.milestone.creator.html_url')), '') as issue_milestone_creator_html_url,                 -- case-sensitive
    -- issue.pull_request
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.url')), '') as issue_pull_request_object_url,                              -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.html_url')), '') as issue_pull_request_html_url,                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.diff_url')), '') as issue_pull_request_diff_url,                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.patch_url')), '') as issue_pull_request_patch_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.pull_request.merged_at')), '') as timestamp)) as issue_pull_request_merged_at,
    -- issue.reactions
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.total_count')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_total_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions."+1"')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_plus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions."-1"')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_minus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.laugh')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_laugh_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.confused')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_confused_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.heart')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_heart_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.hooray')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_hooray_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.rocket')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_rocket_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.issue.reactions.eyes')), '') as int64) as issue_reactions_eyes_count,
    -- assignee (present on action = 'assigned' / 'unassigned')
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.id')), '') as int64) as assignee_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.node_id')), '') as assignee_node_id,                                                 -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.login')), '')) as assignee_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.type')), '')) as assignee_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.user_view_type')), '')) as assignee_user_view_type,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.site_admin')), '') as bool) as assignee_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.avatar_url')), '') as assignee_avatar_url,                                            -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.url')), '') as assignee_object_url,                                                  -- case-sensitive

model.dw.core_fact__fork_events.name: core_fact__fork_events
model.dw.core_fact__fork_events.description:
One row per `ForkEvent` — a repository was forked.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#forkevent)

- Grain: one event (one fork).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'fork_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

`repo_*` (event-level) refers to the **source repository** that was forked.
`forkee_*` columns describe the **newly created fork** repo. All `forkee_*` numeric counts
(stargazers, watchers, forks, open_issues, size) are snapshots at fork time and will be
`0` for typical brand-new forks.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

forkee_id.name: forkee_id
forkee_id.description:
GitHub numeric ID of the newly created fork repository (`payload.forkee.id`).
Differs from event-level `repo_id` (the source repo).

forkee_node_id.name: forkee_node_id
forkee_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the fork (`payload.forkee.node_id`, case-sensitive).

forkee_name.name: forkee_name
forkee_name.description: Short repo name of the fork (`payload.forkee.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `claude-code`).

forkee_visibility.name: forkee_visibility
forkee_visibility.description: Lowercased visibility of the fork (`payload.forkee.visibility`). Observed: `public`, `private`.

forkee_description.name: forkee_description
forkee_description.description:
Fork repository description text (`payload.forkee.description`, case-sensitive).
Inherited from source repo at fork time; nullable.

forkee_homepage.name: forkee_homepage
forkee_homepage.description: Homepage URL set on the fork (`payload.forkee.homepage`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

forkee_language.name: forkee_language
forkee_language.description: Lowercased primary programming language of the fork (`payload.forkee.language`).

forkee_default_branch.name: forkee_default_branch
forkee_default_branch.description: Default branch name of the fork (`payload.forkee.default_branch`, case-sensitive — typically `main`).

forkee_private.name: forkee_private
forkee_private.description: Whether the fork is private (`payload.forkee.private`). Boolean.

forkee_fork.name: forkee_fork
forkee_fork.description: Whether the fork is itself a fork (`payload.forkee.fork`). Always `true` for `ForkEvent` rows.

forkee_archived.name: forkee_archived
forkee_archived.description: Whether the fork is archived (`payload.forkee.archived`). Boolean. Almost always `false` at fork time.

forkee_disabled.name: forkee_disabled
forkee_disabled.description: Whether the fork is disabled (`payload.forkee.disabled`). Boolean. Almost always `false` at fork time.

forkee_is_template.name: forkee_is_template
forkee_is_template.description: Whether the fork is marked as a template repository (`payload.forkee.is_template`). Boolean.

forkee_allow_forking.name: forkee_allow_forking
forkee_allow_forking.description: Whether the fork itself allows further forking (`payload.forkee.allow_forking`). Boolean.

forkee_web_commit_signoff_required.name: forkee_web_commit_signoff_required
forkee_web_commit_signoff_required.description: Whether the fork requires DCO/sign-off on web commits (`payload.forkee.web_commit_signoff_required`). Boolean.

forkee_has_issues.name: forkee_has_issues
forkee_has_issues.description: Whether Issues are enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_issues`). Boolean.

forkee_has_projects.name: forkee_has_projects
forkee_has_projects.description: Whether Projects are enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_projects`). Boolean.

forkee_has_wiki.name: forkee_has_wiki
forkee_has_wiki.description: Whether the Wiki is enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_wiki`). Boolean.

forkee_has_pages.name: forkee_has_pages
forkee_has_pages.description: Whether GitHub Pages is enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_pages`). Boolean.

forkee_has_downloads.name: forkee_has_downloads
forkee_has_downloads.description: Whether the legacy Downloads feature is enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_downloads`). Boolean.

forkee_has_discussions.name: forkee_has_discussions
forkee_has_discussions.description: Whether Discussions are enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_discussions`). Boolean.

forkee_has_pull_requests.name: forkee_has_pull_requests
forkee_has_pull_requests.description: Whether PRs are accepted on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_pull_requests`). Boolean.

forkee_stargazers_count.name: forkee_stargazers_count
forkee_stargazers_count.description: Star count on the fork (`payload.forkee.stargazers_count`). Snapshot at fork time — typically 0 for fresh forks.

forkee_watchers_count.name: forkee_watchers_count
forkee_watchers_count.description: Watcher count on the fork (`payload.forkee.watchers_count`). In modern GitHub API this equals `stargazers_count`.

forkee_watchers.name: forkee_watchers
forkee_watchers.description: Alias of `forkee_watchers_count` kept by the API for backwards compatibility (`payload.forkee.watchers`).

forkee_forks_count.name: forkee_forks_count
forkee_forks_count.description: Fork-of-fork count on the new fork (`payload.forkee.forks_count`). Typically 0 at fork time.

forkee_open_issues_count.name: forkee_open_issues_count
forkee_open_issues_count.description: Open-issue count on the fork (`payload.forkee.open_issues_count`). Typically 0 at fork time.

forkee_topics.name: forkee_topics
forkee_topics.description: String array of topic tags on the fork (`payload.forkee.topics`, `ARRAY<STRING>`). Inherited from source repo at fork time.

forkee_created_at.name: forkee_created_at
forkee_created_at.description: UTC datetime the fork repo was created (`payload.forkee.created_at`). Equals or precedes the event `created_at`.

forkee_updated_at.name: forkee_updated_at
forkee_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the fork repo was last updated (`payload.forkee.updated_at`).

forkee_pushed_at.name: forkee_pushed_at
forkee_pushed_at.description: UTC datetime of the last push to the fork (`payload.forkee.pushed_at`).

forkee_object_url.name: forkee_object_url
forkee_object_url.description: REST API URL for the fork repo (`payload.forkee.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_html_url.name: forkee_html_url
forkee_html_url.description: Web URL for the fork repo (`payload.forkee.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_git_url.name: forkee_git_url
forkee_git_url.description: Git protocol URL for cloning the fork (`payload.forkee.git_url`). Case-sensitive.

forkee_ssh_url.name: forkee_ssh_url
forkee_ssh_url.description: SSH URL for cloning the fork (`payload.forkee.ssh_url`). Case-sensitive.

forkee_clone_url.name: forkee_clone_url
forkee_clone_url.description: HTTPS clone URL for the fork (`payload.forkee.clone_url`). Case-sensitive.

forkee_svn_url.name: forkee_svn_url
forkee_svn_url.description: Legacy SVN-bridge URL for the fork (`payload.forkee.svn_url`). Case-sensitive.

forkee_mirror_url.name: forkee_mirror_url
forkee_mirror_url.description: Upstream mirror URL if the fork is configured as a mirror (`payload.forkee.mirror_url`). Case-sensitive. Usually null.

forkee_archive_url.name: forkee_archive_url
forkee_archive_url.description: REST API URL template for downloading an archive of the fork at a ref. Case-sensitive.

forkee_assignees_url.name: forkee_assignees_url
forkee_assignees_url.description: REST API URL template for assignees of issues/PRs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_blobs_url.name: forkee_blobs_url
forkee_blobs_url.description: REST API URL template for git blobs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_branches_url.name: forkee_branches_url
forkee_branches_url.description: REST API URL template for branches in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_collaborators_url.name: forkee_collaborators_url
forkee_collaborators_url.description: REST API URL template for collaborators of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_comments_url.name: forkee_comments_url
forkee_comments_url.description: REST API URL template for issue/PR comments in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_commits_url.name: forkee_commits_url
forkee_commits_url.description: REST API URL template for commits in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_compare_url.name: forkee_compare_url
forkee_compare_url.description: REST API URL template for comparing refs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_contents_url.name: forkee_contents_url
forkee_contents_url.description: REST API URL template for file contents in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_contributors_url.name: forkee_contributors_url
forkee_contributors_url.description: REST API URL for contributors of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_deployments_url.name: forkee_deployments_url
forkee_deployments_url.description: REST API URL for deployments of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_downloads_url.name: forkee_downloads_url
forkee_downloads_url.description: REST API URL for the legacy Downloads of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_events_url.name: forkee_events_url
forkee_events_url.description: REST API URL for events in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_forks_url.name: forkee_forks_url
forkee_forks_url.description: REST API URL for forks of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_git_commits_url.name: forkee_git_commits_url
forkee_git_commits_url.description: REST API URL template for git commit objects in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_git_refs_url.name: forkee_git_refs_url
forkee_git_refs_url.description: REST API URL template for git refs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_git_tags_url.name: forkee_git_tags_url
forkee_git_tags_url.description: REST API URL template for git tag objects in the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_hooks_url.name: forkee_hooks_url
forkee_hooks_url.description: REST API URL for webhooks of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_issue_comment_url.name: forkee_issue_comment_url
forkee_issue_comment_url.description: REST API URL template for issue comments of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_issue_events_url.name: forkee_issue_events_url
forkee_issue_events_url.description: REST API URL template for issue events of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_issues_url.name: forkee_issues_url
forkee_issues_url.description: REST API URL template for issues of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_keys_url.name: forkee_keys_url
forkee_keys_url.description: REST API URL template for deploy keys of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_labels_url.name: forkee_labels_url
forkee_labels_url.description: REST API URL template for labels of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_languages_url.name: forkee_languages_url
forkee_languages_url.description: REST API URL for language stats of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_merges_url.name: forkee_merges_url
forkee_merges_url.description: REST API URL for merges performed on the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_milestones_url.name: forkee_milestones_url
forkee_milestones_url.description: REST API URL template for milestones of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_notifications_url.name: forkee_notifications_url
forkee_notifications_url.description: REST API URL for notifications of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_pulls_url.name: forkee_pulls_url
forkee_pulls_url.description: REST API URL template for pull requests of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_releases_url.name: forkee_releases_url
forkee_releases_url.description: REST API URL template for releases of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_stargazers_url.name: forkee_stargazers_url
forkee_stargazers_url.description: REST API URL for stargazers of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_statuses_url.name: forkee_statuses_url
forkee_statuses_url.description: REST API URL template for commit statuses of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_subscribers_url.name: forkee_subscribers_url
forkee_subscribers_url.description: REST API URL for subscribers (watchers) of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_subscription_url.name: forkee_subscription_url
forkee_subscription_url.description: REST API URL for the actor's subscription to the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_tags_url.name: forkee_tags_url
forkee_tags_url.description: REST API URL for tags of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_teams_url.name: forkee_teams_url
forkee_teams_url.description: REST API URL for teams with access to the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_trees_url.name: forkee_trees_url
forkee_trees_url.description: REST API URL template for git trees of the fork. Case-sensitive.

forkee_owner_id.name: forkee_owner_id
forkee_owner_id.description:
GitHub numeric user/org ID that owns the fork (`payload.forkee.owner.id`).
Typically equals the event-level `user_id` (the forker).

forkee_owner_node_id.name: forkee_owner_node_id
forkee_owner_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.node_id`, case-sensitive).

forkee_owner_name.name: forkee_owner_name
forkee_owner_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.login`, lowered).

forkee_owner_type.name: forkee_owner_type
forkee_owner_type.description: Lowercased account type of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.type`) — e.g. `user`, `organization`.

forkee_owner_user_view_type.name: forkee_owner_user_view_type
forkee_owner_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.user_view_type`).

forkee_owner_gravatar_id.name: forkee_owner_gravatar_id
forkee_owner_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the fork owner (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

forkee_owner_site_admin.name: forkee_owner_site_admin
forkee_owner_site_admin.description: Whether the fork owner is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.forkee.owner.site_admin`). Boolean.

forkee_owner_avatar_url.name: forkee_owner_avatar_url
forkee_owner_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the fork owner (case-sensitive).

forkee_owner_object_url.name: forkee_owner_object_url
forkee_owner_object_url.description: REST API URL for the fork owner user object (`payload.forkee.owner.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_html_url.name: forkee_owner_html_url
forkee_owner_html_url.description: Web URL for the fork owner's profile (`payload.forkee.owner.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_events_url.name: forkee_owner_events_url
forkee_owner_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the fork owner. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_followers_url.name: forkee_owner_followers_url
forkee_owner_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the fork owner's followers. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_following_url.name: forkee_owner_following_url
forkee_owner_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the fork owner is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_gists_url.name: forkee_owner_gists_url
forkee_owner_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the fork owner's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_organizations_url.name: forkee_owner_organizations_url
forkee_owner_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the fork owner belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_received_events_url.name: forkee_owner_received_events_url
forkee_owner_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the fork owner. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_repos_url.name: forkee_owner_repos_url
forkee_owner_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the fork owner's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_starred_url.name: forkee_owner_starred_url
forkee_owner_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the fork owner. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_owner_subscriptions_url.name: forkee_owner_subscriptions_url
forkee_owner_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the fork owner's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

forkee_license_key.name: forkee_license_key
forkee_license_key.description:
Lowercased SPDX-like short key of the source repo's license (`payload.forkee.license.key` — e.g. `mit`, `apache-2.0`).
Null when the source repo has no detected license.

forkee_license_name.name: forkee_license_name
forkee_license_name.description: Human-readable license name (`payload.forkee.license.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `MIT License`). Nullable.

forkee_license_node_id.name: forkee_license_node_id
forkee_license_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the license (`payload.forkee.license.node_id`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

forkee_license_spdx_id.name: forkee_license_spdx_id
forkee_license_spdx_id.description: Lowercased official SPDX identifier of the license (`payload.forkee.license.spdx_id` — e.g. `mit`, `gpl-3.0`). Nullable.

forkee_license_object_url.name: forkee_license_object_url
forkee_license_object_url.description: REST API URL for the license object (`payload.forkee.license.url`). Case-sensitive reference link. Nullable.

model.dw.core_fact__fork_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- forkee
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.id')), '') as int64) as forkee_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.node_id')), '') as forkee_node_id,                                                             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.name')), '') as forkee_name,                                                                   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.full_name')), '') as forkee_full_name,                                                         -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.visibility')), '')) as forkee_visibility,

    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.homepage')), '') as forkee_homepage,                                                           -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.language')), '')) as forkee_language,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.default_branch')), '') as forkee_default_branch,                                               -- case-sensitive

    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.forkee.watchers_count')), '') as int64) as forkee_watchers_count,
