model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_review_.name: core_fact__pull_request_review_events
model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_review_.description:
One row per `PullRequestReviewEvent` — a review was submitted (or updated/dismissed) on a pull request.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#pullrequestreviewevent)

- Grain: one event = one review submission.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'pull_request_review_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

`review_state` is the analytical headline column (`approved` / `commented` / `changes_requested` / `dismissed`).
For the actual line-level comments inside a review, see `core_fact__pull_request_review_comment_events`
— review **submissions** and review **comments** are separate event types.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

review_id.name: review_id
review_id.description: GitHub numeric review ID (`payload.review.id`). Globally unique, stable.

review_node_id.name: review_node_id
review_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the review (`payload.review.node_id`, case-sensitive).

review_state.name: review_state
review_state.description:
Lowercased state of the review submission (`payload.review.state`).
Observed values: `approved`, `commented`, `changes_requested`, `dismissed`.
The analytical headline — `approved` and `changes_requested` are the typical "decision" reviews;
`commented` reviews carry feedback without an explicit verdict.

review_commit_id.name: review_commit_id
review_commit_id.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA the review targets (`payload.review.commit_id`).
Pin point in PR history for the review.

review_body.name: review_body
review_body.description:
Review summary markdown body (`payload.review.body`, case-sensitive).
Per-line comments live separately in `pull_request_review_comment_event`.

review_submitted_at.name: review_submitted_at
review_submitted_at.description: UTC datetime the review was submitted (`payload.review.submitted_at`). The canonical "review time" for analytics.

review_updated_at.name: review_updated_at
review_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the review was last updated (`payload.review.updated_at`).

review_html_url.name: review_html_url
review_html_url.description: Web URL to the review on the PR page (`payload.review.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

review_pull_request_url.name: review_pull_request_url
review_pull_request_url.description: REST API URL of the PR the review targets (`payload.review.pull_request_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

review_html_link_url.name: review_html_link_url
review_html_link_url.description: Web URL of the review (`payload.review._links.html.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

review_pull_request_link_url.name: review_pull_request_link_url
review_pull_request_link_url.description: REST API URL of the linked PR (`payload.review._links.pull_request.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

review_user_id.name: review_user_id
review_user_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.id`). Should equal event-level `user_id`.

review_user_node_id.name: review_user_node_id
review_user_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

review_user_name.name: review_user_name
review_user_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.login`, lowered).

review_user_type.name: review_user_type
review_user_type.description: Lowercased account type of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

review_user_user_view_type.name: review_user_user_view_type
review_user_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.user_view_type`).

review_user_gravatar_id.name: review_user_gravatar_id
review_user_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the reviewer (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

review_user_site_admin.name: review_user_site_admin
review_user_site_admin.description: Whether the reviewer is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.review.user.site_admin`). Boolean.

review_user_avatar_url.name: review_user_avatar_url
review_user_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the reviewer (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

review_user_object_url.name: review_user_object_url
review_user_object_url.description: REST API URL for the reviewer user object (`payload.review.user.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

review_user_html_url.name: review_user_html_url
review_user_html_url.description: Web URL for the reviewer's profile (`payload.review.user.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

pull_request_id.name: pull_request_id
pull_request_id.description:
GitHub numeric pull request ID (`payload.pull_request.id`).
Globally unique, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into PR-level dimensions.

pull_request_number.name: pull_request_number
pull_request_number.description:
Pull request number within its repository (`payload.pull_request.number`).
NOT globally unique — only unique per repo. Use `(repo_id, pull_request_number)` together as a natural key, or `pull_request_id` alone for global uniqueness.

pull_request_object_url.name: pull_request_object_url
pull_request_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the pull request (`payload.pull_request.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

pull_request_head_ref.name: pull_request_head_ref
pull_request_head_ref.description:
Branch name on the head (source) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.head.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch that contains the changes being proposed.

pull_request_head_sha.name: pull_request_head_sha
pull_request_head_sha.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the head branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.head.sha`). Lowercased for case-insensitive join.

pull_request_head_repo_id.name: pull_request_head_repo_id
pull_request_head_repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.id`).
Differs from `repo_id` (the PR's base repo) on cross-fork PRs.
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

pull_request_head_repo_name.name: pull_request_head_repo_name
pull_request_head_repo_name.description:
`"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.name`, case-sensitive — preserved as-emitted).
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

pull_request_head_repo_object_url.name: pull_request_head_repo_object_url
pull_request_head_repo_object_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

pull_request_base_ref.name: pull_request_base_ref
pull_request_base_ref.description:
Branch name on the base (target) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.base.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch the PR is targeting for merge.

pull_request_base_sha.name: pull_request_base_sha
pull_request_base_sha.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the base branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.base.sha`).

pull_request_base_repo_id.name: pull_request_base_repo_id
pull_request_base_repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the base (target) repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.id`).
Typically equals the event-level `repo_id`.

pull_request_base_repo_name.name: pull_request_base_repo_name
pull_request_base_repo_name.description: `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.name`, case-sensitive).

pull_request_base_repo_object_url.name: pull_request_base_repo_object_url
pull_request_base_repo_object_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_review_.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- review
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.id')), '') as int64) as review_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.node_id')), '') as review_node_id,                                     -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.state')), '')) as review_state,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.commit_id')), '')) as review_commit_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.body')), '') as review_body,                                           -- case-sensitive
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.submitted_at')), '') as timestamp)) as review_submitted_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.updated_at')), '') as timestamp)) as review_updated_at,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.html_url')), '') as review_html_url,                                   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.pull_request_url')), '') as review_pull_request_url,                   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review._links.html.href')), '') as review_html_link_url,                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review._links.pull_request.href')), '') as review_pull_request_link_url,      -- case-sensitive
    -- review.user
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.id')), '') as int64) as review_user_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.node_id')), '') as review_user_node_id,                           -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.login')), '')) as review_user_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.type')), '')) as review_user_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.user_view_type')), '')) as review_user_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.gravatar_id')), '') as review_user_gravatar_id,                   -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.site_admin')), '') as bool) as review_user_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.avatar_url')), '') as review_user_avatar_url,                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.url')), '') as review_user_object_url,                            -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.review.user.html_url')), '') as review_user_html_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    -- pull_request
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_id,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.number')), '') as int64) as pull_request_number,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.url')), '') as pull_request_object_url,                          -- case-sensitive
    -- pull_request.head
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.ref')), '') as pull_request_head_ref,                       -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.sha')), '')) as pull_request_head_sha,
    -- pull_request.head.repo
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.repo.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_head_repo_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.repo.name')), '') as pull_request_head_repo_name,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.repo.url')), '') as pull_request_head_repo_object_url,      -- case-sensitive
    -- pull_request.base
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.ref')), '') as pull_request_base_ref,                       -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.sha')), '')) as pull_request_base_sha,
    -- pull_request.base.repo
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.repo.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_base_repo_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.repo.name')), '') as pull_request_base_repo_name,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.repo.url')), '') as pull_request_base_repo_object_url,      -- case-sensitive
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'pull_request_review_event'

model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_events.name: core_fact__pull_request_events
model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_events.description:
One row per `PullRequestEvent` — an action was taken on a pull request.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#pullrequestevent)

- Grain: one event (one action on one PR).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'pull_request_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

`action` is the discriminator for the kind of activity. Note that **`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action**
— the upstream GitHub webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`; GH Archive collapses this into
a dedicated `merged` action for analytical convenience.

`label_*` columns are populated only on `labeled` / `unlabeled` actions; `assignee_*` only on `assigned` / `unassigned`.
`pull_request.head` / `pull_request.base` describe the PR's source and target sides — for cross-fork PRs
`head_repo_id` differs from the event-level `repo_id` (which always equals the base repo).

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

pull_request_id.name: pull_request_id
pull_request_id.description:
GitHub numeric pull request ID (`payload.pull_request.id`).
Globally unique, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into PR-level dimensions.

pull_request_number.name: pull_request_number
pull_request_number.description:
Pull request number within its repository (`payload.pull_request.number`).
NOT globally unique — only unique per repo. Use `(repo_id, pull_request_number)` together as a natural key, or `pull_request_id` alone for global uniqueness.

pull_request_object_url.name: pull_request_object_url
pull_request_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the pull request (`payload.pull_request.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

pull_request_head_ref.name: pull_request_head_ref
pull_request_head_ref.description:
Branch name on the head (source) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.head.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch that contains the changes being proposed.

pull_request_head_sha.name: pull_request_head_sha
pull_request_head_sha.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the head branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.head.sha`). Lowercased for case-insensitive join.

pull_request_head_repo_id.name: pull_request_head_repo_id
pull_request_head_repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.id`).
Differs from `repo_id` (the PR's base repo) on cross-fork PRs.
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

pull_request_head_repo_name.name: pull_request_head_repo_name
pull_request_head_repo_name.description:
`"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.name`, case-sensitive — preserved as-emitted).
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

pull_request_head_repo_object_url.name: pull_request_head_repo_object_url
pull_request_head_repo_object_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

pull_request_base_ref.name: pull_request_base_ref
pull_request_base_ref.description:
Branch name on the base (target) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.base.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch the PR is targeting for merge.

pull_request_base_sha.name: pull_request_base_sha
pull_request_base_sha.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the base branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.base.sha`).

pull_request_base_repo_id.name: pull_request_base_repo_id
pull_request_base_repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the base (target) repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.id`).
Typically equals the event-level `repo_id`.

pull_request_base_repo_name.name: pull_request_base_repo_name
pull_request_base_repo_name.description: `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.name`, case-sensitive).

pull_request_base_repo_object_url.name: pull_request_base_repo_object_url
pull_request_base_repo_object_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

label_id.name: label_id
label_id.description:
GitHub numeric label ID (`payload.label.id`). Stable across renames.
Populated only when `action in ('labeled', 'unlabeled')`; null otherwise.

label_node_id.name: label_node_id
label_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the label (`payload.label.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

label_name.name: label_name
label_name.description:
Display name of the label (`payload.label.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `bug`, `good first issue`).
This is what users see in the GitHub UI. Use `label_id` as the durable join key.

label_color.name: label_color
label_color.description:
6-character hex color of the label without the leading `#` (`payload.label.color`, e.g. `d73a4a`).
Case-sensitive but conventionally lowercased.

label_description.name: label_description
label_description.description: Free-text description of the label (`payload.label.description`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

label_default.name: label_default
label_default.description:
Whether this label is one of GitHub's default labels for new repositories
(`payload.label.default`). Boolean.

label_object_url.name: label_object_url
label_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the label object (`payload.label.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/labels/<name>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

labels.name: labels
labels.description:
JSON array of label objects currently attached to the parent issue / PR
(`payload.labels`, populated on `labeled` / `unlabeled` actions).
Each element follows the same shape as the `label_*` columns. Stored as `array<json>` — unnest with `unnest(labels)` and extract per-element fields via `json_value`.

assignee_id.name: assignee_id
assignee_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the assignee (`payload.assignee.id`).
Stable across login renames. Populated only when `action in ('assigned', 'unassigned')`.

assignee_node_id.name: assignee_node_id
assignee_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the assignee user (`payload.assignee.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

assignee_name.name: assignee_name
assignee_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the assignee (`payload.assignee.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

assignee_type.name: assignee_type
assignee_type.description:
Lowercased account type of the assignee (`payload.assignee.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`, `organization`, `mannequin`.

assignee_user_view_type.name: assignee_user_view_type
assignee_user_view_type.description:
Lowercased visibility flag for the assignee account
(`payload.assignee.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

assignee_gravatar_id.name: assignee_gravatar_id
assignee_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier (`payload.assignee.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty for modern accounts.

assignee_site_admin.name: assignee_site_admin
assignee_site_admin.description: Whether the assignee is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.assignee.site_admin`). Boolean.

assignee_avatar_url.name: assignee_avatar_url
assignee_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the assignee (`payload.assignee.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

assignee_object_url.name: assignee_object_url
assignee_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the assignee user object (`payload.assignee.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

assignee_html_url.name: assignee_html_url
assignee_html_url.description:
GitHub web URL for the assignee profile page (`payload.assignee.html_url`).
Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

assignee_events_url.name: assignee_events_url
assignee_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the assignee user. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_followers_url.name: assignee_followers_url
assignee_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the assignee's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_following_url.name: assignee_following_url
assignee_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the assignee is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_gists_url.name: assignee_gists_url
assignee_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the assignee's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_organizations_url.name: assignee_organizations_url
assignee_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the assignee belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_received_events_url.name: assignee_received_events_url
assignee_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the assignee. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_repos_url.name: assignee_repos_url
assignee_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the assignee's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_starred_url.name: assignee_starred_url
assignee_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the assignee. Case-sensitive reference link.

assignee_subscriptions_url.name: assignee_subscriptions_url
assignee_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the assignee's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- pull_request
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_id,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.number')), '') as int64) as pull_request_number,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.url')), '') as pull_request_object_url,                          -- case-sensitive
    -- pull_request.head
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.ref')), '') as pull_request_head_ref,                       -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.sha')), '')) as pull_request_head_sha,
    -- pull_request.head.repo
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.repo.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_head_repo_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.repo.name')), '') as pull_request_head_repo_name,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.repo.url')), '') as pull_request_head_repo_object_url,      -- case-sensitive
    -- pull_request.base
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.ref')), '') as pull_request_base_ref,                       -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.sha')), '')) as pull_request_base_sha,
    -- pull_request.base.repo
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.repo.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_base_repo_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.repo.name')), '') as pull_request_base_repo_name,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.base.repo.url')), '') as pull_request_base_repo_object_url,      -- case-sensitive
    -- label (present on action in ('labeled', 'unlabeled'))
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.id')), '') as int64) as label_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.node_id')), '') as label_node_id,                                       -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.name')), '') as label_name,                                             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.color')), '') as label_color,                                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.description')), '') as label_description,                               -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.default')), '') as bool) as label_default,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.label.url')), '') as label_object_url,                                        -- case-sensitive
    -- labels (present on action in ('labeled', 'unlabeled'))
    json_query_array(payload, '$.labels') as labels,
    -- assignee (present on action in ('assigned', 'unassigned'))
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.id')), '') as int64) as assignee_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.node_id')), '') as assignee_node_id,                                 -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.login')), '')) as assignee_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.type')), '')) as assignee_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.user_view_type')), '')) as assignee_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.gravatar_id')), '') as assignee_gravatar_id,                         -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.site_admin')), '') as bool) as assignee_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.avatar_url')), '') as assignee_avatar_url,                            -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.url')), '') as assignee_object_url,                                  -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.html_url')), '') as assignee_html_url,                               -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.events_url')), '') as assignee_events_url,                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.followers_url')), '') as assignee_followers_url,                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.following_url')), '') as assignee_following_url,                     -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.gists_url')), '') as assignee_gists_url,                             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.organizations_url')), '') as assignee_organizations_url,             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.received_events_url')), '') as assignee_received_events_url,         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.repos_url')), '') as assignee_repos_url,                             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.starred_url')), '') as assignee_starred_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.assignee.subscriptions_url')), '') as assignee_subscriptions_url,             -- case-sensitive
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'pull_request_event'

model.dw.core_fact__gollum_events.name: core_fact__gollum_events
model.dw.core_fact__gollum_events.description:
One row per `GollumEvent` — one or more wiki pages were created or edited in a repository.
("Gollum" is the name of the wiki rendering engine GitHub uses.)
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#gollumevent)

- Grain: one event (which may bundle multiple page changes).
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'gollum_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

**Repeated nested column**: a single event can describe N page changes. Rather than fanning out and breaking
the `event_id` grain, the per-page records are kept as a `pages` ARRAY<STRUCT<...>>. Each element has its own
`action` (e.g. `created`, `edited`), so the discriminator lives inside the array, not at the event root.
To analyze per-page, `cross join unnest(pages)`.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

pages.name: pages
pages.description:
Repeated nested column — one element per wiki page changed by this event (`payload.pages`, `ARRAY<STRUCT<...>>`).
Kept as a repeated struct so the `event_id` grain stays intact (one push can touch many pages).
Analyze per-page with `cross join unnest(pages) as p`.

pages.page_name.name: pages.page_name
pages.page_name.description: URL slug of the wiki page (`payload.pages[].page_name`, case-sensitive).

pages.title.name: pages.title
pages.title.description: Display title of the wiki page (`payload.pages[].title`, case-sensitive).

pages.summary.name: pages.summary
pages.summary.description: Optional commit-message-like summary of the page change (`payload.pages[].summary`, case-sensitive). Often null.

pages.action.name: pages.action
pages.action.description:
Lowercased per-page action — what happened to this specific page. Observed values: `created`, `edited`.
Note: lives inside the array element, not at the event root (the `gollum_event` itself has no top-level `action`).

pages.sha.name: pages.sha
pages.sha.description: Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA of the page revision (`payload.pages[].sha`).

pages.html_url.name: pages.html_url
pages.html_url.description: Web URL for the wiki page on GitHub (`payload.pages[].html_url`, case-sensitive). Case-sensitive reference link.

model.dw.core_fact__gollum_events.raw_code:
select
    e.* except (payload),
    -- pages (repeated nested struct; preserves event_id grain)
    array(
        select as struct
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.page_name')), '') as page_name,    -- case-sensitive
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.title')), '') as title,            -- case-sensitive
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.summary')), '') as summary,        -- case-sensitive
            lower(nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.action')), '')) as action,
            lower(nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.sha')), '')) as sha,
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.html_url')), '') as html_url,      -- case-sensitive
        from unnest(json_query_array(e.payload, '$.pages')) p
    ) as pages,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }} e
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'gollum_event'
model.dw.core_fact__gollum_events.compiled_code:
select
    e.* except (payload),
    -- pages (repeated nested struct; preserves event_id grain)
    array(
        select as struct
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.page_name')), '') as page_name,    -- case-sensitive
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.title')), '') as title,            -- case-sensitive
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.summary')), '') as summary,        -- case-sensitive
            lower(nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.action')), '')) as action,
            lower(nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.sha')), '')) as sha,
            nullif(trim(json_value(p, '$.html_url')), '') as html_url,      -- case-sensitive
        from unnest(json_query_array(e.payload, '$.pages')) p
    ) as pages,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events` e
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'gollum_event'

model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_review_.name: core_fact__pull_request_review_comment_events
model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_review_.description:
One row per `PullRequestReviewCommentEvent` — a line-level comment was made on a pull request diff
(typically as part of a review).
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#pullrequestreviewcommentevent)

- Grain: one event = one diff comment created.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'pull_request_review_comment_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

Each comment is tied back to the review submission via `pull_request_review_id` and to a specific
diff location via `comment__path` + `comment_position` (current) and `comment_original_position` (when first written).
Comments can target a single line (`comment_subject_type = 'line'`) or a whole file (`comment_subject_type = 'file'`).

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

comment_id.name: comment_id
comment_id.description: GitHub numeric ID of the comment (`payload.comment.id`). Globally unique across all comments of this kind.

comment_node_id.name: comment_node_id
comment_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the comment (`payload.comment.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

pull_request_review_id.name: pull_request_review_id
pull_request_review_id.description:
Numeric ID of the parent review submission this comment belongs to (`payload.comment.pull_request_review_id`).
Joins back to `core_fact__pull_request_review_events.review_id`.

in_reply_to_id.name: in_reply_to_id
in_reply_to_id.description:
When the comment is a reply, the `comment_id` of the parent comment in the thread (`payload.comment.in_reply_to_id`).
Null for top-level comments.

commit_id.name: commit_id
commit_id.description:
Lowercased 40-char SHA the comment is associated with (`payload.comment.commit_id`).
Lowercased for case-insensitive join. The join key into commit-level data.
For `commit_comment_event`: SHA of the commit being commented on (stable).
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: SHA the comment was rebased onto in current state —
the original SHA the comment was first written against is in `original_commit_id`.

original_commit_id.name: original_commit_id
original_commit_id.description:
Lowercased 40-char SHA the comment was originally written against (`payload.comment.original_commit_id`).
Stable across PR head moves, unlike `commit_id` (which tracks the current head).

comment_body.name: comment_body
comment_body.description: Comment markdown body (`payload.comment.body`, case-sensitive). Can be long.

comment_diff_hunk.name: comment_diff_hunk
comment_diff_hunk.description:
Snippet of the diff hunk the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.diff_hunk`, case-sensitive).
Useful for showing context without re-fetching the diff.

comment__path.name: comment__path
comment__path.description:
File path the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.path`, case-sensitive).
For `commit_comment_event`: null when the comment is on the commit as a whole (not a specific file).
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: always populated — the file in the PR diff the comment targets.
Note the double-underscore in the column name distinguishes it from the comment object itself.

comment_position.name: comment_position
comment_position.description:
Diff-relative position the comment is anchored to (`payload.comment.position`).
For `commit_comment_event`: position in the commit's diff.
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: current position in the PR diff — updated when the PR head moves;
null if the comment is now outdated (the original anchor is in `comment_original_position`).

comment_original_position.name: comment_original_position
comment_original_position.description:
Diff-relative position the comment was originally placed at (`payload.comment.original_position`).
Stable across PR head moves, unlike `comment_position`.

comment_subject_type.name: comment_subject_type
comment_subject_type.description:
Lowercased granularity of the comment target (`payload.comment.subject_type`).
Observed: `line` (most common), `file` (whole-file comment).

comment_created_at.name: comment_created_at
comment_created_at.description: UTC datetime the comment was created (`payload.comment.created_at`).

comment_updated_at.name: comment_updated_at
comment_updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the comment was last updated (`payload.comment.updated_at`).
Refreshed on edits and on reaction count changes.

comment_object_url.name: comment_object_url
comment_object_url.description: REST API URL for the comment object (`payload.comment.url`). Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

comment_html_url.name: comment_html_url
comment_html_url.description: Web URL for the comment in the GitHub UI (`payload.comment.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_pull_request_url.name: comment_pull_request_url
comment_pull_request_url.description: REST API URL of the parent PR (`payload.comment.pull_request_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_self_link_url.name: comment_self_link_url
comment_self_link_url.description: Self-link to the comment (`payload.comment._links.self.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_html_link_url.name: comment_html_link_url
comment_html_link_url.description: HTML link to the comment (`payload.comment._links.html.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_pull_request_link_url.name: comment_pull_request_link_url
comment_pull_request_link_url.description: Link to the parent PR (`payload.comment._links.pull_request.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_user_id.name: comment_user_id
comment_user_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.id`). Should equal event-level `user_id`.

comment_user_node_id.name: comment_user_node_id
comment_user_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

comment_user_name.name: comment_user_name
comment_user_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.login`, lowered).

comment_user_type.name: comment_user_type
comment_user_type.description: Lowercased account type of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

comment_user_user_view_type.name: comment_user_user_view_type
comment_user_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

comment_user_gravatar_id.name: comment_user_gravatar_id
comment_user_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the comment author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

comment_user_site_admin.name: comment_user_site_admin
comment_user_site_admin.description: Whether the comment author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

comment_user_avatar_url.name: comment_user_avatar_url
comment_user_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the comment author (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

comment_user_object_url.name: comment_user_object_url
comment_user_object_url.description: REST API URL for the comment author user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

comment_user_html_url.name: comment_user_html_url
comment_user_html_url.description: Web URL for the comment author's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

reactions_total_count.name: reactions_total_count
reactions_total_count.description:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

reactions_plus_one_count.name: reactions_plus_one_count
reactions_plus_one_count.description: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_minus_one_count.name: reactions_minus_one_count
reactions_minus_one_count.description: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_laugh_count.name: reactions_laugh_count
reactions_laugh_count.description: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_confused_count.name: reactions_confused_count
reactions_confused_count.description: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_heart_count.name: reactions_heart_count
reactions_heart_count.description: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_hooray_count.name: reactions_hooray_count
reactions_hooray_count.description: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_rocket_count.name: reactions_rocket_count
reactions_rocket_count.description: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

reactions_eyes_count.name: reactions_eyes_count
reactions_eyes_count.description: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

pull_request_id.name: pull_request_id
pull_request_id.description:
GitHub numeric pull request ID (`payload.pull_request.id`).
Globally unique, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into PR-level dimensions.

pull_request_number.name: pull_request_number
pull_request_number.description:
Pull request number within its repository (`payload.pull_request.number`).
NOT globally unique — only unique per repo. Use `(repo_id, pull_request_number)` together as a natural key, or `pull_request_id` alone for global uniqueness.

pull_request_object_url.name: pull_request_object_url
pull_request_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the pull request (`payload.pull_request.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

pull_request_head_ref.name: pull_request_head_ref
pull_request_head_ref.description:
Branch name on the head (source) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.head.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch that contains the changes being proposed.

pull_request_head_sha.name: pull_request_head_sha
pull_request_head_sha.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the head branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.head.sha`). Lowercased for case-insensitive join.

pull_request_head_repo_id.name: pull_request_head_repo_id
pull_request_head_repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.id`).
Differs from `repo_id` (the PR's base repo) on cross-fork PRs.
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

pull_request_head_repo_name.name: pull_request_head_repo_name
pull_request_head_repo_name.description:
`"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.name`, case-sensitive — preserved as-emitted).
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

pull_request_head_repo_object_url.name: pull_request_head_repo_object_url
pull_request_head_repo_object_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

pull_request_base_ref.name: pull_request_base_ref
pull_request_base_ref.description:
Branch name on the base (target) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.base.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch the PR is targeting for merge.

pull_request_base_sha.name: pull_request_base_sha
pull_request_base_sha.description:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the base branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.base.sha`).

pull_request_base_repo_id.name: pull_request_base_repo_id
pull_request_base_repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the base (target) repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.id`).
Typically equals the event-level `repo_id`.

pull_request_base_repo_name.name: pull_request_base_repo_name
pull_request_base_repo_name.description: `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.name`, case-sensitive).

pull_request_base_repo_object_url.name: pull_request_base_repo_object_url
pull_request_base_repo_object_url.description: GitHub REST API URL for the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

model.dw.core_fact__pull_request_review_.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- comment
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.id')), '') as int64) as comment_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.node_id')), '') as comment_node_id,                                     -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.pull_request_review_id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_review_id,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.in_reply_to_id')), '') as int64) as in_reply_to_id,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.commit_id')), '')) as commit_id,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.original_commit_id')), '')) as original_commit_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.body')), '') as comment_body,                                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.diff_hunk')), '') as comment_diff_hunk,                                 -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.path')), '') as comment__path,                                          -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.position')), '') as int64) as comment_position,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.original_position')), '') as int64) as comment_original_position,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.subject_type')), '')) as comment_subject_type,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.created_at')), '') as timestamp)) as comment_created_at,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.updated_at')), '') as timestamp)) as comment_updated_at,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.url')), '') as comment_object_url,                                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.html_url')), '') as comment_html_url,                                   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.pull_request_url')), '') as comment_pull_request_url,                   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment._links.self.href')), '') as comment_self_link_url,                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment._links.html.href')), '') as comment_html_link_url,                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment._links.pull_request.href')), '') as comment_pull_request_link_url,      -- case-sensitive
    -- comment.user
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.id')), '') as int64) as comment_user_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.node_id')), '') as comment_user_node_id,                           -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.login')), '')) as comment_user_name,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.type')), '')) as comment_user_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.user_view_type')), '')) as comment_user_user_view_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.gravatar_id')), '') as comment_user_gravatar_id,                   -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.site_admin')), '') as bool) as comment_user_site_admin,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.avatar_url')), '') as comment_user_avatar_url,                      -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.url')), '') as comment_user_object_url,                            -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.user.html_url')), '') as comment_user_html_url,                         -- case-sensitive
    -- comment.reactions
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.total_count')), '') as int64) as reactions_total_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."+1"')), '') as int64) as reactions_plus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions."-1"')), '') as int64) as reactions_minus_one_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.laugh')), '') as int64) as reactions_laugh_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.confused')), '') as int64) as reactions_confused_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.heart')), '') as int64) as reactions_heart_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.hooray')), '') as int64) as reactions_hooray_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.rocket')), '') as int64) as reactions_rocket_count,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.comment.reactions.eyes')), '') as int64) as reactions_eyes_count,
    -- pull_request
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.id')), '') as int64) as pull_request_id,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.number')), '') as int64) as pull_request_number,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.url')), '') as pull_request_object_url,                            -- case-sensitive
    -- pull_request.head
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.ref')), '') as pull_request_head_ref,                         -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pull_request.head.sha')), '')) as pull_request_head_sha,
    -- pull_request.head.repo
