model.dw.core_fact__delete_events.name: core_fact__delete_events
model.dw.core_fact__delete_events.description:
One row per `DeleteEvent` — a git branch or tag was removed from a repository.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#deleteevent)

- Grain: one event.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'delete_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

`ref_type` distinguishes between branch and tag deletion. Repository deletion is NOT emitted as a `DeleteEvent`
— when a repo is deleted, GitHub simply stops emitting any events for it.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

ref.name: ref
ref.description:
Short git ref involved in the event (case-sensitive).
  - `create_event` / `delete_event`: the branch or tag name (e.g. `feat/login`, `v1.2.0`) — without the `refs/heads/` or `refs/tags/` prefix.
  - `push_event`: the full ref path `refs/heads/<branch>` or `refs/tags/<tag>` — note the prefix is present here, unlike create/delete.

Nullable on `create_event` when `ref_type = 'repository'` (the repo itself was created and no specific ref applies).

full_ref.name: full_ref
full_ref.description:
Fully qualified git ref path including the `refs/heads/` or `refs/tags/` prefix (case-sensitive).
Populated on `create_event` and `delete_event`; for the analogous field on `push_event` see `ref`.
Nullable when not provided by the upstream payload.

ref_type.name: ref_type
ref_type.description:
Lowercased kind of git ref this event acts on. Not null on `create_event` / `delete_event`.

Per the GitHub Events API:
  - `create_event` — `branch`, `tag`, or `repository`
  - `delete_event` — `branch` or `tag`

Observed in the GH Archive sample: `branch` (dominant), `tag`. `repository` is documented but very rare in the public feed (was deprecated for create events in 2018).

pusher_type.name: pusher_type
pusher_type.description:
Lowercased actor category that performed the push / create / delete (`payload.pusher_type`).
Effectively always `user` in the GH Archive public feed — included for parity with the upstream API schema.

model.dw.core_fact__delete_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref')), '') as ref,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.full_ref')), '') as full_ref, -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref_type')), '')) as ref_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pusher_type')), '')) as pusher_type,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'delete_event'
model.dw.core_fact__delete_events.compiled_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref')), '') as ref,           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.full_ref')), '') as full_ref, -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref_type')), '')) as ref_type,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pusher_type')), '')) as pusher_type,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'delete_event'

model.dw.core_fact__create_events.name: core_fact__create_events
model.dw.core_fact__create_events.description:
One row per `CreateEvent` — a git branch, tag, or repository was created.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#createevent)

- Primary key: `event_id`.

`ref_type` is the discriminator for what was created. In the current GH Archive public feed `branch`
dominates by volume; `tag` and `repository` are documented by the API but rare/absent in the recent sample.
When `ref_type = 'repository'`, the `ref` / `full_ref` fields are null.

master_branch.name: master_branch
master_branch.description:
Repository's default branch name at the time the create event fired (`payload.master_branch`, case-sensitive).
Populated on `create_event` regardless of `ref_type`.
Note: the field name predates GitHub's rename to "default branch" — content can be `main`, `master`, or any branch name.

The yml column is literally named `description` — this doc block is renamed to `description__repo` to avoid colliding with the more common usage of the word.

pusher_type.name: pusher_type
pusher_type.description:
Lowercased actor category that performed the push / create / delete (`payload.pusher_type`).
Effectively always `user` in the GH Archive public feed — included for parity with the upstream API schema.

model.dw.core_fact__create_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref')), '') as ref,                       -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.full_ref')), '') as full_ref,             -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref_type')), '')) as ref_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.master_branch')), '') as master_branch,   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.description')), '') as description,       -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pusher_type')), '')) as pusher_type,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'create_event'
model.dw.core_fact__create_events.compiled_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref')), '') as ref,                       -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.full_ref')), '') as full_ref,             -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.ref_type')), '')) as ref_type,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.master_branch')), '') as master_branch,   -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.description')), '') as description,       -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.pusher_type')), '')) as pusher_type,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and event_name = 'create_event'

model.dw.core_fact__discussion_events.name: core_fact__discussion_events
model.dw.core_fact__discussion_events.description:
One row per `DiscussionEvent` — a GitHub Discussions thread was created (or otherwise acted upon).
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#discussionevent)

- Grain: one event.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'discussion_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

Low volume in the public feed (hundreds per day vs. millions for `push_event`).
The `created` action dominates; the upstream webhook supports other actions (`edited`, `deleted`,
`pinned`, `unpinned`, `locked`, `unlocked`, `transferred`, `category_changed`, `answered`, `unanswered`,
`labeled`, `unlabeled`) but they're rare in the public stream.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

action.name: action
action.description:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

discussion_id.name: discussion_id
discussion_id.description: GitHub numeric discussion ID (`payload.discussion.id`). Globally unique, stable.

discussion_node_id.name: discussion_node_id
discussion_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the discussion (`payload.discussion.node_id`, case-sensitive).

discussion_number.name: discussion_number
discussion_number.description:
Discussion number within its repository (`payload.discussion.number`).
NOT globally unique — use `(repo_id, discussion_number)`.

discussion_body.name: discussion_body
discussion_body.description: Discussion body markdown (`payload.discussion.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.

discussion_state.name: discussion_state
discussion_state.description: Lowercased state of the discussion (`payload.discussion.state`). Observed: `open`, `closed`.

discussion_state_reason.name: discussion_state_reason
discussion_state_reason.description:
Lowercased reason for the current state (`payload.discussion.state_reason`).
Observed: `resolved`, `reopened`. Null when not applicable.

discussion_author_association.name: discussion_author_association
discussion_author_association.description:
Lowercased author-vs-repo relationship (`payload.discussion.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`,
`first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`. Often null in the public feed.

discussion_active_lock_reason.name: discussion_active_lock_reason
discussion_active_lock_reason.description: Lowercased reason the discussion is locked (`payload.discussion.active_lock_reason`). Null when not locked.

discussion_locked.name: discussion_locked
discussion_locked.description: Whether the discussion is currently locked (`payload.discussion.locked`). Boolean.

discussion_comments.name: discussion_comments
discussion_comments.description: Count of comments on the discussion (`payload.discussion.comments`). Snapshot at event time.

discussion_labels.name: discussion_labels
discussion_labels.description:
JSON array of label objects on the discussion (`payload.discussion.labels`, `ARRAY<JSON>`).
Each element follows the label-object shape.

discussion_created_at.name: discussion_created_at
discussion_created_at.description: UTC datetime the discussion was created (`payload.discussion.created_at`).

discussion_updated_at.name: discussion_updated_at
discussion_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the discussion was last updated (`payload.discussion.updated_at`).

discussion_answer_chosen_at.name: discussion_answer_chosen_at
discussion_answer_chosen_at.description:
UTC datetime an answer was marked on the discussion (`payload.discussion.answer_chosen_at`).
Null when no answer is chosen, or when the category is not answerable.

discussion_answer_html_url.name: discussion_answer_html_url
discussion_answer_html_url.description:
Web URL of the chosen answer comment (`payload.discussion.answer_html_url`). Case-sensitive.
Null when no answer is chosen.

discussion_html_url.name: discussion_html_url
discussion_html_url.description: Web URL of the discussion page (`payload.discussion.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_repository_url.name: discussion_repository_url
discussion_repository_url.description: REST API URL for the parent repository (`payload.discussion.repository_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_timeline_url.name: discussion_timeline_url
discussion_timeline_url.description: REST API URL for the discussion timeline (`payload.discussion.timeline_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_id.name: discussion_user_id
discussion_user_id.description: GitHub numeric user ID of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.id`).

discussion_user_node_id.name: discussion_user_node_id
discussion_user_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

discussion_user_name.name: discussion_user_name
discussion_user_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.login`, lowered).

discussion_user_type.name: discussion_user_type
discussion_user_type.description: Lowercased account type of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.type`).

discussion_user_user_view_type.name: discussion_user_user_view_type
discussion_user_user_view_type.description: Lowercased account visibility of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.user_view_type`).

discussion_user_gravatar_id.name: discussion_user_gravatar_id
discussion_user_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the discussion author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

discussion_user_site_admin.name: discussion_user_site_admin
discussion_user_site_admin.description: Whether the discussion author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

discussion_user_avatar_url.name: discussion_user_avatar_url
discussion_user_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the discussion author (case-sensitive).

discussion_user_object_url.name: discussion_user_object_url
discussion_user_object_url.description: REST API URL for the discussion author user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_html_url.name: discussion_user_html_url
discussion_user_html_url.description: Web URL for the discussion author's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_events_url.name: discussion_user_events_url
discussion_user_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the discussion author. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_followers_url.name: discussion_user_followers_url
discussion_user_followers_url.description: REST API URL for the discussion author's followers. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_following_url.name: discussion_user_following_url
discussion_user_following_url.description: REST API URL template for users the discussion author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_gists_url.name: discussion_user_gists_url
discussion_user_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the discussion author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_organizations_url.name: discussion_user_organizations_url
discussion_user_organizations_url.description: REST API URL for the orgs the discussion author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_received_events_url.name: discussion_user_received_events_url
discussion_user_received_events_url.description: REST API URL for events received by the discussion author. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_repos_url.name: discussion_user_repos_url
discussion_user_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the discussion author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_starred_url.name: discussion_user_starred_url
discussion_user_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the discussion author. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_user_subscriptions_url.name: discussion_user_subscriptions_url
discussion_user_subscriptions_url.description: REST API URL for the discussion author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_category_id.name: discussion_category_id
discussion_category_id.description: GitHub numeric ID of the discussion category (`payload.discussion.category.id`). Categories are per-repo.

discussion_category_node_id.name: discussion_category_node_id
discussion_category_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the discussion category (`payload.discussion.category.node_id`, case-sensitive).

discussion_category_name.name: discussion_category_name
discussion_category_name.description: Display name of the category (`payload.discussion.category.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `Q&A`, `Ideas`).

discussion_category_emoji.name: discussion_category_emoji
discussion_category_emoji.description: Emoji shortcode associated with the category (`payload.discussion.category.emoji`, case-sensitive — e.g. `:bulb:`).

discussion_category_description.name: discussion_category_description
discussion_category_description.description: Free-text description of the category (`payload.discussion.category.description`, case-sensitive).

discussion_category_slug.name: discussion_category_slug
discussion_category_slug.description: Lowercased URL slug of the category (`payload.discussion.category.slug` — e.g. `q-a`, `ideas`).

discussion_category_is_answerable.name: discussion_category_is_answerable
discussion_category_is_answerable.description: Whether discussions in this category can be marked with an answer (`payload.discussion.category.is_answerable`). Boolean.

discussion_category_repository_id.name: discussion_category_repository_id
discussion_category_repository_id.description: Numeric repo ID the category belongs to (`payload.discussion.category.repository_id`). Equals event-level `repo_id`.

discussion_category_created_at.name: discussion_category_created_at
discussion_category_created_at.description: UTC datetime the category was created (`payload.discussion.category.created_at`).

discussion_category_updated_at.name: discussion_category_updated_at
discussion_category_updated_at.description: UTC datetime the category was last updated (`payload.discussion.category.updated_at`).

discussion_answer_chosen_by_id.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_id
discussion_answer_chosen_by_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the user who marked the chosen answer (`payload.discussion.answer_chosen_by.id`).
Null when no answer is chosen.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_node_id.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_node_id
discussion_answer_chosen_by_node_id.description: GraphQL global node ID of the answer-chooser user (case-sensitive).

discussion_answer_chosen_by_name.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_name
discussion_answer_chosen_by_name.description: Lowercased GitHub login of the answer-chooser (`payload.discussion.answer_chosen_by.login`, lowered).

discussion_answer_chosen_by_type.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_type
discussion_answer_chosen_by_type.description: Lowercased account type of the answer-chooser.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_user_view_ty.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_user_view_type
discussion_answer_chosen_by_user_view_ty.description: Lowercased account visibility of the answer-chooser.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_gravatar_id.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_gravatar_id
discussion_answer_chosen_by_gravatar_id.description: Legacy Gravatar identifier (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_site_admin.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_site_admin
discussion_answer_chosen_by_site_admin.description: Whether the answer-chooser is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_avatar_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_avatar_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_avatar_url.description: Avatar image URL for the answer-chooser (case-sensitive).

discussion_answer_chosen_by_object_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_object_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_object_url.description: REST API URL for the answer-chooser user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_html_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_html_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_html_url.description: Web URL for the answer-chooser's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_events_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_events_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_events_url.description: REST API URL template for events involving the answer-chooser. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_followers_ur.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_followers_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_followers_ur.description: REST API URL for the answer-chooser's followers. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_following_ur.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_following_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_following_ur.description: REST API URL template for users the answer-chooser is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_gists_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_gists_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_gists_url.description: REST API URL template for the answer-chooser's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_organization.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_organizations_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_organization.description: REST API URL for the orgs the answer-chooser belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_received_eve.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_received_events_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_received_eve.description: REST API URL for events received by the answer-chooser. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_repos_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_repos_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_repos_url.description: REST API URL for the answer-chooser's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_starred_url.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_starred_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_starred_url.description: REST API URL template for repos starred by the answer-chooser. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_answer_chosen_by_subscription.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_subscriptions_url
discussion_answer_chosen_by_subscription.description: REST API URL for the answer-chooser's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

discussion_reactions_total_count.name: discussion_reactions_total_count
discussion_reactions_total_count.description:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

discussion_reactions_plus_one_count.name: discussion_reactions_plus_one_count
discussion_reactions_plus_one_count.description: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_minus_one_count.name: discussion_reactions_minus_one_count
discussion_reactions_minus_one_count.description: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_laugh_count.name: discussion_reactions_laugh_count
discussion_reactions_laugh_count.description: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_confused_count.name: discussion_reactions_confused_count
discussion_reactions_confused_count.description: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_heart_count.name: discussion_reactions_heart_count
discussion_reactions_heart_count.description: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_hooray_count.name: discussion_reactions_hooray_count
discussion_reactions_hooray_count.description: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_rocket_count.name: discussion_reactions_rocket_count
discussion_reactions_rocket_count.description: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_eyes_count.name: discussion_reactions_eyes_count
discussion_reactions_eyes_count.description: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

discussion_reactions_object_url.name: discussion_reactions_object_url
discussion_reactions_object_url.description: REST API URL for the discussion's reactions collection (`payload.discussion.reactions.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

changes_title_from.name: changes_title_from
changes_title_from.description:
Previous title text when `action = 'edited'` and the title changed (`payload.changes.title.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event` and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

changes_body_from.name: changes_body_from
changes_body_from.description:
Previous body markdown when `action = 'edited'` and the body changed (`payload.changes.body.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event`, `issue_comment_event`, and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

changes_category_from_name.name: changes_category_from_name
changes_category_from_name.description:
Previous category display name when `action = 'category_changed'` on a `discussion_event`
(`payload.changes.category.from.name`, case-sensitive). Null otherwise.

changes_category_from_slug.name: changes_category_from_slug
changes_category_from_slug.description:
Previous category slug (lowercased) when `action = 'category_changed'` on a `discussion_event`
(`payload.changes.category.from.slug`). Null otherwise.

model.dw.core_fact__discussion_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload),
    -- action
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.action')), '')) as action,
    -- discussion
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.id')), '') as int64) as discussion_id,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.node_id')), '') as discussion_node_id,                                                       -- case-sensitive
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.number')), '') as int64) as discussion_number,
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.title')), '') as discussion_title,                                                           -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.body')), '') as discussion_body,                                                             -- case-sensitive
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.state')), '')) as discussion_state,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.state_reason')), '')) as discussion_state_reason,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.author_association')), '')) as discussion_author_association,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.active_lock_reason')), '')) as discussion_active_lock_reason,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.locked')), '') as bool) as discussion_locked,
    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.comments')), '') as int64) as discussion_comments,
    json_query_array(payload, '$.discussion.labels') as discussion_labels,
    datetime(safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(payload, '$.discussion.created_at')), '') as timestamp)) as discussion_created_at,

model.dw.core_fact__public_events.name: core_fact__public_events
model.dw.core_fact__public_events.description:
One row per `PublicEvent` — a previously private repository was flipped to public visibility.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types#publicevent)

- Grain: one event.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` filtered to `event_name = 'public_event'`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`repo_id`, `user_id`)

The upstream payload is intentionally empty for this event type, so this model surfaces the base event columns only
(actor / repo / org / timestamps) — the event itself signals the visibility flip; the new state is "public".

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

model.dw.core_fact__public_events.raw_code:
select
    * except (payload)
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and event_name = 'public_event'

model.dw.core_snp_fact__monthly_active_u.name: core_snp_fact__monthly_active_users
model.dw.core_snp_fact__monthly_active_u.description:
Activated Users (AU) periodic snapshot fact at the monthly grain — one row
per `(user_id, date)` where the user was active on at least one day
between the first day of the calendar month (`date`) and the current
`batch_date`.

- Grain: one (user, calendar month).
- Primary key: (`user_id`, `date`).
- Source: `core_snp_fact__daily_active_users`, rolled up by month.
- Partitioned by: `date` (month-granularity — native BigQuery monthly
  partitioning).
- Clustered by: `user_id`

`events_count` is the sum of daily `events_count` over the month so far.
`active_days` is the sum of daily `active_days` over the month so far,
i.e. the number of distinct days within the month the user was active
(range 1–31, depending on the calendar month).

Incremental strategy: `insert_overwrite` with dynamic partition discovery.
`batch_filter(interval='month')` snaps the **lower bound** of the batch
window back to the 1st of the enclosing month so each batch reads from
the start of every affected month through `batch_date`. The monthly row
is therefore a **month-to-date** snapshot — partial mid-month, full once
the month closes. Each batch re-writes the affected month partitions
with the latest MTD value, so late-arriving daily events propagate
cleanly.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

date.name: date
date.description:
Calendar date (UTC). Not null.

Usage across tables:
  - `core_snp_fact__daily_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the calendar day
    the entity was active on.
  - `core_snp_fact__weekly_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the **Sunday that
    starts the week** the row reports on (`DATE_TRUNC(..., WEEK(SUNDAY))`).
  - `core_snp_fact__monthly_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the **first day
    of the calendar month** the row reports on (`DATE_TRUNC(..., MONTH)`).

In the weekly / monthly AU rollups the row's measures (`events_count`,
`active_days`) are **period-to-date** from `date` through the current
`batch_date` — partial mid-period, full once the period closes.

events_count.name: events_count
events_count.description:
Total number of GitHub events triggered by this entity during the snapshot period
(day / week / month, depending on the table's grain). Always `> 0` — periods with
zero events do not produce a row (the AU table is a sparse activation snapshot,
not a dense calendar cross-join).

In rollup tables (weekly / monthly) this is the sum of the underlying daily
`events_count` values across the period.

active_days.name: active_days
active_days.description:
Number of distinct calendar days within the snapshot period the entity was
active (triggered at least one event). Range:
  - daily tables:   always 1 (kept so daily / weekly / monthly share a column shape).
  - weekly tables:  1–7
  - monthly tables: 1–31 (28–31 depending on the calendar month)

Always `≥ 1` — periods with zero active days do not produce a row.

In rollup tables (weekly / monthly) this is the sum of the underlying daily
`active_days` values across the period, which — because the daily table is
unique on `(<entity>_id, date)` — equals the count of distinct active days
in that period.

model.dw.core_snp_fact__monthly_active_u.raw_code:
{% set interval = 'month' %}

select
    user_id,
    date_trunc(date, {{ interval }}) as date,
    sum(events_count) as events_count,
    sum(active_days) as active_days,
from
    {{ ref('core_snp_fact__daily_active_users') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='date', interval=interval) }}
group by
    all
model.dw.core_snp_fact__monthly_active_u.compiled_code:
select
    user_id,
    date_trunc(date, month) as date,
    sum(events_count) as events_count,
    sum(active_days) as active_days,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`core_snp_fact__daily_active_users`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', month) and date '2026-06-29'


group by
    all

model.dw.core_snp_fact__weekly_active_or.name: core_snp_fact__weekly_active_orgs
model.dw.core_snp_fact__weekly_active_or.description:
Activated Orgs (AU pattern applied at the org grain) periodic snapshot
fact at the weekly grain — one row per `(org_id, date)` where the
organization received at least one org-scoped event between the Sunday
that starts the week (`date`) and the current `batch_date`.

- Grain: one (org, calendar week, Sunday-based).
- Primary key: (`org_id`, `date`).
- Source: `core_snp_fact__daily_active_orgs`, rolled up by week.
- Partitioned by: `date` (day-granularity)
- Clustered by: `org_id`

Inherits the org-scoping caveat from `core_snp_fact__daily_active_orgs`
— personal-repo activity is excluded by design. Week convention is
`WEEK(SUNDAY)`. `events_count` is the sum of daily `events_count` over
the week so far.
`active_days` is the sum of daily `active_days` over the week so far,
i.e. the number of distinct days within the week the org was active
(range 1–7).

Incremental strategy: `insert_overwrite` with dynamic partition discovery.
`batch_filter(interval='week(sunday)')` snaps the **lower bound** of the
batch window back to the enclosing Sunday so each batch reads from the
start of every affected week through `batch_date`. The weekly row is
therefore a **week-to-date** snapshot — partial mid-week, full once the
week closes. Each batch re-writes the affected week partitions with the
latest WTD value, so late-arriving daily events propagate cleanly.

model.dw.core_snp_fact__daily_active_use.name: core_snp_fact__daily_active_users
model.dw.core_snp_fact__daily_active_use.description:
Activated Users (AU) periodic snapshot fact at the daily grain — one row
per `(user_id, date)` where the user triggered at least one event on that
UTC calendar day.

- Grain: one (user, day).
- Primary key: (`user_id`, `date`).
- Source: `stg_fact__events`, filtered to `user_id is not null`.
- Partitioned by: `date` (day)
- Clustered by: `user_id`

Activation is binary — the row's existence encodes it. `events_count`
records the total event volume contribution that day; `active_days = 1`
for every daily row, kept so daily / weekly / monthly grains share the
same column shape (and weekly / monthly can roll it up with `SUM`).
The AU pattern intentionally compresses event-level detail away, so
per-event-type splits live in a future "AU by Events" table, not here.

No `is_public` filter applied — `stg_fact__events` is already the public
feed; the rare non-public rows (~1 in 100k) still represent activity and
we keep parity with the transactional fact tables which also don't filter.

Incremental strategy: `insert_overwrite` with dynamic partition discovery
— dbt overwrites exactly the `date` partitions present in the model's
output, which is scoped by `batch_filter` to the active batch window.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

date.name: date
date.description:
Calendar date (UTC). Not null.

Usage across tables:
  - `core_snp_fact__daily_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the calendar day
    the entity was active on.
  - `core_snp_fact__weekly_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the **Sunday that
    starts the week** the row reports on (`DATE_TRUNC(..., WEEK(SUNDAY))`).
  - `core_snp_fact__monthly_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the **first day
    of the calendar month** the row reports on (`DATE_TRUNC(..., MONTH)`).

In the weekly / monthly AU rollups the row's measures (`events_count`,
`active_days`) are **period-to-date** from `date` through the current
`batch_date` — partial mid-period, full once the period closes.

events_count.name: events_count
events_count.description:
Total number of GitHub events triggered by this entity during the snapshot period
(day / week / month, depending on the table's grain). Always `> 0` — periods with
zero events do not produce a row (the AU table is a sparse activation snapshot,
not a dense calendar cross-join).

In rollup tables (weekly / monthly) this is the sum of the underlying daily
`events_count` values across the period.

active_days.name: active_days
active_days.description:
Number of distinct calendar days within the snapshot period the entity was
active (triggered at least one event). Range:
  - daily tables:   always 1 (kept so daily / weekly / monthly share a column shape).
  - weekly tables:  1–7
  - monthly tables: 1–31 (28–31 depending on the calendar month)

Always `≥ 1` — periods with zero active days do not produce a row.

In rollup tables (weekly / monthly) this is the sum of the underlying daily
`active_days` values across the period, which — because the daily table is
unique on `(<entity>_id, date)` — equals the count of distinct active days
in that period.

model.dw.core_snp_fact__daily_active_use.raw_code:
select
    user_id,
    created_date as date,
    count(1) as events_count,
    1 as active_days,
from
    {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
    and user_id is not null
    and event_name is not null
group by
    all
model.dw.core_snp_fact__daily_active_use.compiled_code:
select
    user_id,
    created_date as date,
    count(1) as events_count,
    1 as active_days,
from
    `joshua-data`.`dw`.`stg_fact__events`
where true
    and 

    
        
        
        
        
            
            
        
    

    created_date between date_trunc(date '2026-06-28', day) and date '2026-06-29'


    and user_id is not null
    and event_name is not null
group by
    all

model.dw.core_snp_fact__weekly_active_re.name: core_snp_fact__weekly_active_repos
model.dw.core_snp_fact__weekly_active_re.description:
Activated Repos (AU pattern applied at the repo grain) periodic snapshot
fact at the weekly grain — one row per `(repo_id, date)` where the repo
received at least one event between the Sunday that starts the week
(`date`) and the current `batch_date`.

- Grain: one (repo, calendar week, Sunday-based).
- Primary key: (`repo_id`, `date`).
- Source: `core_snp_fact__daily_active_repos`, rolled up by week.
- Partitioned by: `date` (day-granularity)
- Clustered by: `repo_id`

Week convention is `WEEK(SUNDAY)`. `events_count` is the sum of daily
`events_count` over the week so far.
`active_days` is the sum of daily `active_days` over the week so far,
i.e. the number of distinct days within the week the repo was active
(range 1–7).

Incremental strategy: `insert_overwrite` with dynamic partition discovery.
`batch_filter(interval='week(sunday)')` snaps the **lower bound** of the
batch window back to the enclosing Sunday so each batch reads from the
start of every affected week through `batch_date`. The weekly row is
therefore a **week-to-date** snapshot — partial mid-week, full once the
week closes. Each batch re-writes the affected week partitions with the
latest WTD value, so late-arriving daily events propagate cleanly.

model.dw.core_scd1__orgs.name: core_scd1__orgs
model.dw.core_scd1__orgs.description:
Conformed organization dimension — one row per GitHub organization observed in any event we have ingested.
(SCD Type 1: only the latest attribute values are kept; no history.)

- Grain: one organization.
- Primary key: `org_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` (top-level org columns), restricted to rows with non-null `org_id`
  (only ~11% of events are org-scoped; the remainder are personal-repo events with no org).
- Clustered by: `org_id`

`created_at` records first sighting and is immutable; `updated_at` advances only when `org_name`,
`org_object_url`, or `org_image_url` actually changes between batches.
Re-observation with identical attributes is a no-op for `updated_at`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime the entity was first observed in any GH Archive event we have ingested.
Set on first insert into this dimension and **never updated** thereafter.
(distinct from the event-level `created_at`, which is the timestamp of a specific event row.)
Not null.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the most recent time **any tracked attribute on this row actually changed**.
Equals `created_at` on first insert; advances only when this batch's observed attributes differ from
the values previously stored in this dimension. Stays put when an entity is re-observed with identical
attributes — i.e. it is a true change marker, not a last-seen timestamp.
Not null.

model.dw.core_scd1__orgs.raw_code:
with

dim__latest_orgs as (
    select
        org_id,
        org_name,
        org_object_url,
        org_image_url,
        created_at,
    from
        {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
    where true
        and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
        and org_id is not null
    qualify
        row_number() over (partition by org_id order by created_at desc, event_id desc) = 1
)

select
    latest.org_id,
    latest.org_name,
    latest.org_object_url,
    latest.org_image_url,
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    coalesce(existing.created_at, latest.created_at) as created_at,
    case
        when existing.org_id is null then latest.created_at
        when coalesce(existing.org_name, '')         != coalesce(latest.org_name, '')
            or coalesce(existing.org_object_url, '') != coalesce(latest.org_object_url, '')
            or coalesce(existing.org_image_url, '')  != coalesce(latest.org_image_url, '')
            then latest.created_at
        else existing.updated_at
    end as updated_at,
    {% else %}
    latest.created_at,
    latest.created_at as updated_at,
    {% endif %}
from
    dim__latest_orgs as latest
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    left join {{ this }} as existing
        using (org_id)
    {% endif %}

    dim__latest_repos as latest
    
    left join `joshua-data`.`dw`.`core_scd1__repos` as existing
        using (repo_id)

model.dw.core_scd1__users.name: core_scd1__users
model.dw.core_scd1__users.description:
Conformed user dimension — one row per GitHub user observed in any event we have ingested.
(SCD Type 1: only the latest attribute values are kept; no history.)

- Grain: one user.
- Primary key: `user_id`.
- Source: `stg_fact__events` (top-level actor columns).
- Clustered by: `user_id`

`created_at` records first sighting and is immutable; `updated_at` advances only when at least one of
`user_name`, `user_display_name`, `user_object_url`, `user_image_url` actually changes between batches.
Re-observation with identical attributes is a no-op for `updated_at`.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime the entity was first observed in any GH Archive event we have ingested.
Set on first insert into this dimension and **never updated** thereafter.
(distinct from the event-level `created_at`, which is the timestamp of a specific event row.)
Not null.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.description:
UTC datetime the most recent time **any tracked attribute on this row actually changed**.
Equals `created_at` on first insert; advances only when this batch's observed attributes differ from
the values previously stored in this dimension. Stays put when an entity is re-observed with identical
attributes — i.e. it is a true change marker, not a last-seen timestamp.
Not null.

model.dw.core_scd1__users.raw_code:
with

dim__latest_users as (
    select
        user_id,
        user_name,
        user_display_name,
        user_object_url,
        user_image_url,
        created_at,
    from
        {{ ref('stg_fact__events') }}
    where true
        and {{ batch_filter(date_col='created_date') }}
        and user_id is not null
    qualify
        row_number() over (partition by user_id order by created_at desc, event_id desc) = 1
)

select
    latest.user_id,
    latest.user_name,
    latest.user_display_name,
    latest.user_object_url,
    latest.user_image_url,
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    coalesce(existing.created_at, latest.created_at) as created_at,
    case
        when existing.user_id is null then latest.created_at
        when coalesce(existing.user_name, '')           != coalesce(latest.user_name, '')
            or coalesce(existing.user_display_name, '') != coalesce(latest.user_display_name, '')
            or coalesce(existing.user_object_url, '')   != coalesce(latest.user_object_url, '')
            or coalesce(existing.user_image_url, '')    != coalesce(latest.user_image_url, '')
            then latest.created_at
        else existing.updated_at
    end as updated_at,
    {% else %}
    latest.created_at,
    latest.created_at as updated_at,
    {% endif %}
from
    dim__latest_users as latest
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    left join {{ this }} as existing
        using (user_id)
    {% endif %}

model.dw.stg_fact__events.name: stg_fact__events
model.dw.stg_fact__events.description:
One row per GitHub public event sourced from the GH Archive feed (https://www.gharchive.org/), itself a mirror of the GitHub Events REST API.
(https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types)

- Grain: one event.
- Primary key: `event_id`.
- Partitioned by: `created_date` (day)
- Clustered by: (`event_name`, `repo_id`)

Deduplication across re-ingests is enforced in the model via `row_number() over (partition by id order by ingested_at desc) = 1`,
so the latest ingested copy of any given event wins.

This is the first staging layer on top of `raw__gharchive.ext__events` (a BigQuery external table over Hive-partitioned Parquet in GCS).
It flattens the actor / repo / org JSON blobs into typed scalar columns and parses `payload` into a native JSON type.

The shape of `payload` itself varies by `event_name` (see GitHub Events API spec)
and is intentionally left unflattened here — downstream marts pick the fields they need per event type.

Naming conventions:
  - `*_id` columns are GitHub's numeric IDs (stable across renames).
  - `*_name` columns are lowercased canonical handles, safe to join on.
  - `*_display_name` columns preserve the original casing for display.
  - `*_object_url` columns are GitHub REST API URLs (api.github.com/...).
  - `*_image_url` columns are avatar image URLs.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

created_date.name: created_date
created_date.description:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

ingested_date.name: ingested_date
ingested_date.description:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

ingested_at.name: ingested_at
ingested_at.description:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

user_id.name: user_id
user_id.description:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

user_name.name: user_name
user_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.description:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

user_object_url.name: user_object_url
user_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

user_image_url.name: user_image_url
user_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

repo_id.name: repo_id
repo_id.description:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

repo_name.name: repo_name
repo_name.description:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
repo_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

org_id.name: org_id
org_id.description:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

org_name.name: org_name
org_name.description:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

org_object_url.name: org_object_url
org_object_url.description:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

org_image_url.name: org_image_url
org_image_url.description:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

payload.name: payload
payload.description:
Event-type-specific data as a native BigQuery JSON value.
(parsed via `SAFE.PARSE_JSON` from the source string column.)

The schema of this object varies by `event_name`
— see the GitHub Events API reference for the per-type contract: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types

Examples:
  - `push_event`         -> `{ ref, head, before, commits[], ... }`
  - `pull_request_event` -> `{ action, number, pull_request{...} }`
  - `issues_event`       -> `{ action, issue{...} }`
  - `release_event`      -> `{ action, release{...} }`

Downstream marts should switch on `event_name` and project the fields they need.
Not null at the row level, but inner paths are best read with `SAFE.` / `JSON_VALUE` / `JSON_QUERY`.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.description:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

model.dw.stg_fact__events.raw_code:
select
    id as event_id,
    lower(regexp_replace(nullif(trim(type), ''), r'([a-z])([A-Z])', r'\1_\2')) as event_name,

    dt as created_date,
    datetime(safe_cast(created_at as timestamp)) as created_at,
    date(safe_cast(ingested_at as timestamp)) as ingested_date,
    datetime(safe_cast(ingested_at as timestamp)) as ingested_at,

    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(actor, '$.id')), '') as int64) as user_id,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(actor, '$.login')), '')) as user_name,
    nullif(trim(json_value(actor, '$.display_login')), '') as user_display_name, -- case-sensitive
    -- actor.gravatar_id (deprecated)
    nullif(trim(json_value(actor, '$.url')), '') as user_object_url,             -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(actor, '$.avatar_url')), '') as user_image_url,       -- case-sensitive

    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(repo, '$.id')), '') as int64) as repo_id,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(repo, '$.name')), '')) as repo_name,
    nullif(trim(json_value(repo, '$.url')), '') as repo_object_url,              -- case-sensitive

    safe_cast(nullif(trim(json_value(org, '$.id')), '') as int64) as org_id,
    lower(nullif(trim(json_value(org, '$.login')), '')) as org_name,
    -- org.gravatar_id (deprecated)
    nullif(trim(json_value(org, '$.url')), '') as org_object_url,                -- case-sensitive
    nullif(trim(json_value(org, '$.avatar_url')), '') as org_image_url,          -- case-sensitive

    safe.parse_json(payload) as payload,
    public as is_public,
from
    {{ source('gharchive', 'events') }}
where true
    and {{ batch_filter(date_col='dt') }}
qualify
    row_number() over (partition by id order by ingested_at desc) = 1

macro.dw.batch_filter.name: batch_filter
macro.dw.batch_filter.description:
Renders a date-range predicate for the `where` clause of batch / incremental models.

Window resolution order:
① args (`start_date` + `end_date`)
→ ② vars (`batch_start_date` + `batch_end_date`)
→ ③ var (`batch_date`, with a 1-day lookback).

`interval` snaps only the lower bound back to the start of the
enclosing period; the upper bound is left as-is. This widens the scan
to cover period-to-date windows (WTD / MTD / QTD) without pulling in
future dates that haven't materialised yet.

Raises a compile error if no window resolves, to prevent full scans.

arguments.name: date_col
arguments.description: Name of the `DATE` column to filter on. Defaults to `dt` (the Hive partition column on `raw__gharchive.ext__events`).

arguments.name: start_date
arguments.description: Explicit start date (`YYYY-MM-DD`, inclusive). Takes precedence over vars only when paired with `end_date`.

arguments.name: end_date
arguments.description: Explicit end date (`YYYY-MM-DD`, inclusive). Only honored when paired with `start_date`.

arguments.name: interval
arguments.description:
Period to snap the lower bound back to — passed straight through to
BigQuery `DATE_TRUNC`, so any unit `DATE_TRUNC` accepts works:
`day` (default, no-op), `week`, `week(<weekday>)`,
`month`, `quarter`, `year`.

macro.dw.get_where_subquery.name: get_where_subquery
macro.dw.get_where_subquery.description:
Overrides dbt's built-in `get_where_subquery` to rewrite `__batch_start_date__` / `__batch_end_date__` placeholders
in a test's `where` config into `date 'YYYY-MM-DD'` literals at compile time.

Lets test yml scope assertions to the batch window without embedding `{{ var('...') }}`.
Falls back to the built-in behavior when no placeholder is present.

arguments.name: relation
arguments.description: Target `Relation` passed in automatically by dbt. Not called directly.

macro.dw.replace_batch_dates.name: replace_batch_dates
macro.dw.replace_batch_dates.description:
Helper used by `get_where_subquery`. Replaces `__batch_start_date__` / `__batch_end_date__` tokens in a string with `date '...'` literals.

Values resolve from `batch_start_date` / `batch_end_date` vars, falling back to `batch_date`.
Unlike `batch_filter`, no 1-day lookback is applied (the model layer already handles it).
