macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.name: _bigquery__get_table_shards_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_table_shards_sql(information_schema) %}
    select
        tables.project_id as table_catalog,
        tables.dataset_id as table_schema,
        coalesce(REGEXP_EXTRACT(tables.table_id, '^(.+)[0-9]{8}$'), tables.table_id) as table_name,
        tables.table_id as shard_name,
        REGEXP_EXTRACT(tables.table_id, '^.+([0-9]{8})$') as shard_index,
        REGEXP_CONTAINS(tables.table_id, '^.+[0-9]{8}$') and tables.type = 1 as is_date_shard,
        case
            when materialized_views.table_name is not null then 'materialized view'
            when tables.type = 1 then 'table'
            when tables.type = 2 then 'view'
            else 'external'
        end as table_type,
        tables.type = 1 as is_table,
        JSON_VALUE(table_description.option_value) as table_comment,
        tables.size_bytes,
        tables.row_count
    from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='__TABLES__') }} tables
    left join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='MATERIALIZED_VIEWS') }} materialized_views
        on materialized_views.table_catalog = tables.project_id
        and materialized_views.table_schema = tables.dataset_id
        and materialized_views.table_name = tables.table_id
    left join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='TABLE_OPTIONS') }} table_description
        on table_description.table_catalog = tables.project_id
        and table_description.table_schema = tables.dataset_id
        and table_description.table_name = tables.table_id
        and table_description.option_name = 'description'
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_tables.name: _bigquery__get_tables_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_tables.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_tables_sql() %}
    select distinct
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        table_name,
        is_date_shard,
        table_type,
        is_table,
        table_comment
    from table_shards
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.name: _bigquery__get_table_stats_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_table_.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_table_stats_sql() %}
    select
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        table_name,
        max(shard_name) as latest_shard_name,
        min(shard_index) as shard_min,
        max(shard_index) as shard_max,
        count(shard_index) as shard_count,
        sum(size_bytes) as size_bytes,
        sum(row_count) as row_count
    from table_shards
    group by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.name: _bigquery__get_columns_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_columns_sql(information_schema) %}
    select
        columns.table_catalog,
        columns.table_schema,
        columns.table_name as shard_name,
        coalesce(paths.field_path, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        -- invent a row number to account for nested fields
        -- BQ does not treat these nested properties as independent fields
        row_number() over (
            partition by
                columns.table_catalog,
                columns.table_schema,
                columns.table_name
            order by
                columns.ordinal_position,
                paths.field_path
        ) as column_index,
        coalesce(paths.data_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        paths.description as column_comment,
        case when columns.is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then 1 else 0 end as is_partitioning_column,
        case when columns.is_partitioning_column = 'YES' then paths.field_path end as partition_column,
        case when columns.clustering_ordinal_position is not null then 1 else 0 end as is_clustering_column,
        case when columns.clustering_ordinal_position is not null then paths.field_path end as cluster_column,
        columns.clustering_ordinal_position
    from {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMNS') }} columns
    join {{ information_schema.replace(information_schema_view='COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS') }} paths
        on paths.table_catalog = columns.table_catalog
        and paths.table_schema = columns.table_schema
        and paths.table_name = columns.table_name
        and paths.column_name = columns.column_name
    where columns.ordinal_position is not null
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.name: _bigquery__get_column_stats_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_column.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_column_stats_sql() %}
    select
        table_catalog,
        table_schema,
        shard_name,
        max(is_partitioning_column) = 1 as is_partitioned,
        max(partition_column) as partition_column,
        max(is_clustering_column) = 1 as is_clustered,
        array_to_string(
            array_agg(
                cluster_column ignore nulls
                order by clustering_ordinal_position
            ), ', '
        ) as clustering_columns
    from columns
    group by 1, 2, 3
{% endmacro %}

macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_extend.name: _bigquery__get_extended_catalog_sql
macro.dbt_bigquery._bigquery__get_extend.macro_sql:
{% macro _bigquery__get_extended_catalog_sql() %}
    select
        tables.table_catalog as table_database,
        tables.table_schema,
        case
            when tables.is_date_shard then concat(tables.table_name, '*')
            else tables.table_name
        end as table_name,
        tables.table_type,
        tables.table_comment,
        -- coalesce column metadata fields to ensure they are non-null for catalog generation
        -- external table columns are not present in COLUMN_FIELD_PATHS
        coalesce(columns.column_name, '<unknown>') as column_name,
        coalesce(columns.column_index, 1) as column_index,
        coalesce(columns.column_type, '<unknown>') as column_type,
        coalesce(columns.column_comment, '') as column_comment,

        'Shard count' as `stats__date_shards__label`,
        table_stats.shard_count as `stats__date_shards__value`,
        'The number of date shards in this table' as `stats__date_shards__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shards__include`,

        'Shard (min)' as `stats__date_shard_min__label`,
        table_stats.shard_min as `stats__date_shard_min__value`,
        'The first date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_min__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_min__include`,

        'Shard (max)' as `stats__date_shard_max__label`,
        table_stats.shard_max as `stats__date_shard_max__value`,
        'The last date shard in this table' as `stats__date_shard_max__description`,
        tables.is_date_shard as `stats__date_shard_max__include`,

        '# Rows' as `stats__num_rows__label`,
        table_stats.row_count as `stats__num_rows__value`,
        'Approximate count of rows in this table' as `stats__num_rows__description`,
        tables.is_table as `stats__num_rows__include`,

        'Approximate Size' as `stats__num_bytes__label`,
        table_stats.size_bytes as `stats__num_bytes__value`,
        'Approximate size of table as reported by BigQuery' as `stats__num_bytes__description`,
        tables.is_table as `stats__num_bytes__include`,

        'Partitioned By' as `stats__partitioning_type__label`,
        column_stats.partition_column as `stats__partitioning_type__value`,
        'The partitioning column for this table' as `stats__partitioning_type__description`,
        column_stats.is_partitioned as `stats__partitioning_type__include`,

        'Clustered By' as `stats__clustering_fields__label`,
        column_stats.clustering_columns as `stats__clustering_fields__value`,
        'The clustering columns for this table' as `stats__clustering_fields__description`,
        column_stats.is_clustered as `stats__clustering_fields__include`

    from tables
    join table_stats
        on table_stats.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and table_stats.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and table_stats.table_name = tables.table_name
    left join column_stats
        on column_stats.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and column_stats.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and column_stats.shard_name = table_stats.latest_shard_name
    left join columns
        on columns.table_catalog = tables.table_catalog
        and columns.table_schema = tables.table_schema
        and columns.shard_name = table_stats.latest_shard_name
{% endmacro %}

doc.dw.__overview__.name: __overview__
doc.dw.__overview__.block_contents:
# gharchive ELT — dbt project overview

This dbt project turns the public **[GitHub Archive](https://www.gharchive.org/)**
event stream into curated facts and dimensions on BigQuery. Hourly `.json.gz`
files are landed as Hive-partitioned Parquet in GCS by an upstream ingest job
(outside dbt) and exposed via a BigQuery external table. dbt reads from that
external table and writes into the curated `dw` dataset.

---

## What dbt owns here

Two layers:

- **Staging** — a single fact model that reads the external table, flattens the
  per-event JSON envelope (actor / repo / org) into typed scalar columns, keeps
  the polymorphic `payload` as native `JSON`, and dedupes by event id across
  re-ingests.
- **Core** — built on staging:
  - **Transactional facts** — one per GitHub event type, each projecting the
    typed payload fields it cares about.
  - **SCD-1 dimensions** — conformed user / repo / org dimensions, latest
    attributes only (no history).
  - **Periodic-snapshot facts** — Activated Users (AU) rollups at
    daily / weekly / monthly grains per entity.

Incremental everywhere, partitioned by the relevant date column, clustered for
the common filter shape.

## How the daily batch is scoped

Every model calls the `batch_filter` macro, which requires one of two variables
and fails the compile otherwise (so `require_partition_filter` tables never get
a full scan by accident):

- `batch_date` → window is **`[batch_date − 1 day, batch_date]`**. The 1-day
  lookback mirrors the ingest job's catchup window so a late hour doesn't get
  stranded. Production runs nightly with `batch_date = yesterday UTC`.
- `batch_start_date` + `batch_end_date` → an explicit closed range, used for
  backfills.

Periodic-snapshot models pass an `interval` (`'week(sunday)'`, `'month'`, …) to
snap the lower bound back to the enclosing period start — each batch rewrites
the active period as a period-to-date (WTD / MTD) snapshot, and late-arriving
events propagate cleanly on the next run.

## Where to start

Use the **Models tree on the left** — staging is the entry point, then follow
the lineage into core for whichever event type, dimension, or snapshot grain
you care about. Each model's page documents its grain, partition / cluster
keys, and column semantics.

> **Note on data quality.** The data contract (test severities, freshness
> thresholds, schema-drift triage) is still being finalized. Until then, treat
> any tests you see on individual models as work-in-progress rather than
> promises.

doc.dw.forkee_id.name: forkee_id
doc.dw.forkee_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric ID of the newly created fork repository (`payload.forkee.id`).
Differs from event-level `repo_id` (the source repo).

doc.dw.forkee_node_id.name: forkee_node_id
doc.dw.forkee_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the fork (`payload.forkee.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.forkee_name.name: forkee_name
doc.dw.forkee_name.block_contents: Short repo name of the fork (`payload.forkee.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `claude-code`).

doc.dw.forkee_visibility.name: forkee_visibility
doc.dw.forkee_visibility.block_contents: Lowercased visibility of the fork (`payload.forkee.visibility`). Observed: `public`, `private`.

doc.dw.forkee_description.name: forkee_description
doc.dw.forkee_description.block_contents:
Fork repository description text (`payload.forkee.description`, case-sensitive).
Inherited from source repo at fork time; nullable.

doc.dw.forkee_homepage.name: forkee_homepage
doc.dw.forkee_homepage.block_contents: Homepage URL set on the fork (`payload.forkee.homepage`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

doc.dw.forkee_language.name: forkee_language
doc.dw.forkee_language.block_contents: Lowercased primary programming language of the fork (`payload.forkee.language`).

doc.dw.forkee_default_branch.name: forkee_default_branch
doc.dw.forkee_default_branch.block_contents: Default branch name of the fork (`payload.forkee.default_branch`, case-sensitive — typically `main`).

doc.dw.forkee_private.name: forkee_private
doc.dw.forkee_private.block_contents: Whether the fork is private (`payload.forkee.private`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_fork.name: forkee_fork
doc.dw.forkee_fork.block_contents: Whether the fork is itself a fork (`payload.forkee.fork`). Always `true` for `ForkEvent` rows.

doc.dw.forkee_archived.name: forkee_archived
doc.dw.forkee_archived.block_contents: Whether the fork is archived (`payload.forkee.archived`). Boolean. Almost always `false` at fork time.

doc.dw.forkee_disabled.name: forkee_disabled
doc.dw.forkee_disabled.block_contents: Whether the fork is disabled (`payload.forkee.disabled`). Boolean. Almost always `false` at fork time.

doc.dw.forkee_is_template.name: forkee_is_template
doc.dw.forkee_is_template.block_contents: Whether the fork is marked as a template repository (`payload.forkee.is_template`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_allow_forking.name: forkee_allow_forking
doc.dw.forkee_allow_forking.block_contents: Whether the fork itself allows further forking (`payload.forkee.allow_forking`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_web_commit_signoff_require.name: forkee_web_commit_signoff_required
doc.dw.forkee_web_commit_signoff_require.block_contents: Whether the fork requires DCO/sign-off on web commits (`payload.forkee.web_commit_signoff_required`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_issues.name: forkee_has_issues
doc.dw.forkee_has_issues.block_contents: Whether Issues are enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_issues`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_projects.name: forkee_has_projects
doc.dw.forkee_has_projects.block_contents: Whether Projects are enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_projects`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_wiki.name: forkee_has_wiki
doc.dw.forkee_has_wiki.block_contents: Whether the Wiki is enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_wiki`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_pages.name: forkee_has_pages
doc.dw.forkee_has_pages.block_contents: Whether GitHub Pages is enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_pages`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_downloads.name: forkee_has_downloads
doc.dw.forkee_has_downloads.block_contents: Whether the legacy Downloads feature is enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_downloads`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_discussions.name: forkee_has_discussions
doc.dw.forkee_has_discussions.block_contents: Whether Discussions are enabled on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_discussions`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_has_pull_requests.name: forkee_has_pull_requests
doc.dw.forkee_has_pull_requests.block_contents: Whether PRs are accepted on the fork (`payload.forkee.has_pull_requests`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_stargazers_count.name: forkee_stargazers_count
doc.dw.forkee_stargazers_count.block_contents: Star count on the fork (`payload.forkee.stargazers_count`). Snapshot at fork time — typically 0 for fresh forks.

doc.dw.forkee_watchers_count.name: forkee_watchers_count
doc.dw.forkee_watchers_count.block_contents: Watcher count on the fork (`payload.forkee.watchers_count`). In modern GitHub API this equals `stargazers_count`.

doc.dw.forkee_watchers.name: forkee_watchers
doc.dw.forkee_watchers.block_contents: Alias of `forkee_watchers_count` kept by the API for backwards compatibility (`payload.forkee.watchers`).

doc.dw.forkee_forks_count.name: forkee_forks_count
doc.dw.forkee_forks_count.block_contents: Fork-of-fork count on the new fork (`payload.forkee.forks_count`). Typically 0 at fork time.

doc.dw.forkee_open_issues_count.name: forkee_open_issues_count
doc.dw.forkee_open_issues_count.block_contents: Open-issue count on the fork (`payload.forkee.open_issues_count`). Typically 0 at fork time.

doc.dw.forkee_topics.name: forkee_topics
doc.dw.forkee_topics.block_contents: String array of topic tags on the fork (`payload.forkee.topics`, `ARRAY<STRING>`). Inherited from source repo at fork time.

doc.dw.forkee_created_at.name: forkee_created_at
doc.dw.forkee_created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the fork repo was created (`payload.forkee.created_at`). Equals or precedes the event `created_at`.

doc.dw.forkee_updated_at.name: forkee_updated_at
doc.dw.forkee_updated_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the fork repo was last updated (`payload.forkee.updated_at`).

doc.dw.forkee_pushed_at.name: forkee_pushed_at
doc.dw.forkee_pushed_at.block_contents: UTC datetime of the last push to the fork (`payload.forkee.pushed_at`).

doc.dw.forkee_object_url.name: forkee_object_url
doc.dw.forkee_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the fork repo (`payload.forkee.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_html_url.name: forkee_html_url
doc.dw.forkee_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the fork repo (`payload.forkee.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_git_url.name: forkee_git_url
doc.dw.forkee_git_url.block_contents: Git protocol URL for cloning the fork (`payload.forkee.git_url`). Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_ssh_url.name: forkee_ssh_url
doc.dw.forkee_ssh_url.block_contents: SSH URL for cloning the fork (`payload.forkee.ssh_url`). Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_clone_url.name: forkee_clone_url
doc.dw.forkee_clone_url.block_contents: HTTPS clone URL for the fork (`payload.forkee.clone_url`). Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_svn_url.name: forkee_svn_url
doc.dw.forkee_svn_url.block_contents: Legacy SVN-bridge URL for the fork (`payload.forkee.svn_url`). Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_mirror_url.name: forkee_mirror_url
doc.dw.forkee_mirror_url.block_contents: Upstream mirror URL if the fork is configured as a mirror (`payload.forkee.mirror_url`). Case-sensitive. Usually null.

doc.dw.forkee_archive_url.name: forkee_archive_url
doc.dw.forkee_archive_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for downloading an archive of the fork at a ref. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_assignees_url.name: forkee_assignees_url
doc.dw.forkee_assignees_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for assignees of issues/PRs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_blobs_url.name: forkee_blobs_url
doc.dw.forkee_blobs_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for git blobs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_branches_url.name: forkee_branches_url
doc.dw.forkee_branches_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for branches in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_collaborators_url.name: forkee_collaborators_url
doc.dw.forkee_collaborators_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for collaborators of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_comments_url.name: forkee_comments_url
doc.dw.forkee_comments_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for issue/PR comments in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_commits_url.name: forkee_commits_url
doc.dw.forkee_commits_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for commits in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_compare_url.name: forkee_compare_url
doc.dw.forkee_compare_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for comparing refs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_contents_url.name: forkee_contents_url
doc.dw.forkee_contents_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for file contents in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_contributors_url.name: forkee_contributors_url
doc.dw.forkee_contributors_url.block_contents: REST API URL for contributors of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_deployments_url.name: forkee_deployments_url
doc.dw.forkee_deployments_url.block_contents: REST API URL for deployments of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_downloads_url.name: forkee_downloads_url
doc.dw.forkee_downloads_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the legacy Downloads of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_events_url.name: forkee_events_url
doc.dw.forkee_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL for events in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_forks_url.name: forkee_forks_url
doc.dw.forkee_forks_url.block_contents: REST API URL for forks of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_git_commits_url.name: forkee_git_commits_url
doc.dw.forkee_git_commits_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for git commit objects in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_git_refs_url.name: forkee_git_refs_url
doc.dw.forkee_git_refs_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for git refs in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_git_tags_url.name: forkee_git_tags_url
doc.dw.forkee_git_tags_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for git tag objects in the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_hooks_url.name: forkee_hooks_url
doc.dw.forkee_hooks_url.block_contents: REST API URL for webhooks of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_issue_comment_url.name: forkee_issue_comment_url
doc.dw.forkee_issue_comment_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for issue comments of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_issue_events_url.name: forkee_issue_events_url
doc.dw.forkee_issue_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for issue events of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_issues_url.name: forkee_issues_url
doc.dw.forkee_issues_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for issues of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_keys_url.name: forkee_keys_url
doc.dw.forkee_keys_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for deploy keys of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_labels_url.name: forkee_labels_url
doc.dw.forkee_labels_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for labels of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_languages_url.name: forkee_languages_url
doc.dw.forkee_languages_url.block_contents: REST API URL for language stats of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_merges_url.name: forkee_merges_url
doc.dw.forkee_merges_url.block_contents: REST API URL for merges performed on the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_milestones_url.name: forkee_milestones_url
doc.dw.forkee_milestones_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for milestones of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_notifications_url.name: forkee_notifications_url
doc.dw.forkee_notifications_url.block_contents: REST API URL for notifications of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_pulls_url.name: forkee_pulls_url
doc.dw.forkee_pulls_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for pull requests of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_releases_url.name: forkee_releases_url
doc.dw.forkee_releases_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for releases of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_stargazers_url.name: forkee_stargazers_url
doc.dw.forkee_stargazers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for stargazers of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_statuses_url.name: forkee_statuses_url
doc.dw.forkee_statuses_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for commit statuses of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_subscribers_url.name: forkee_subscribers_url
doc.dw.forkee_subscribers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for subscribers (watchers) of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_subscription_url.name: forkee_subscription_url
doc.dw.forkee_subscription_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the actor's subscription to the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_tags_url.name: forkee_tags_url
doc.dw.forkee_tags_url.block_contents: REST API URL for tags of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_teams_url.name: forkee_teams_url
doc.dw.forkee_teams_url.block_contents: REST API URL for teams with access to the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_trees_url.name: forkee_trees_url
doc.dw.forkee_trees_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for git trees of the fork. Case-sensitive.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_id.name: forkee_owner_id
doc.dw.forkee_owner_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric user/org ID that owns the fork (`payload.forkee.owner.id`).
Typically equals the event-level `user_id` (the forker).

doc.dw.forkee_owner_node_id.name: forkee_owner_node_id
doc.dw.forkee_owner_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.forkee_owner_name.name: forkee_owner_name
doc.dw.forkee_owner_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.forkee_owner_type.name: forkee_owner_type
doc.dw.forkee_owner_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.type`) — e.g. `user`, `organization`.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_user_view_type.name: forkee_owner_user_view_type
doc.dw.forkee_owner_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the fork owner (`payload.forkee.owner.user_view_type`).

doc.dw.forkee_owner_gravatar_id.name: forkee_owner_gravatar_id
doc.dw.forkee_owner_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the fork owner (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_site_admin.name: forkee_owner_site_admin
doc.dw.forkee_owner_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the fork owner is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.forkee.owner.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_avatar_url.name: forkee_owner_avatar_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the fork owner (case-sensitive).

doc.dw.forkee_owner_object_url.name: forkee_owner_object_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the fork owner user object (`payload.forkee.owner.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_html_url.name: forkee_owner_html_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the fork owner's profile (`payload.forkee.owner.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_events_url.name: forkee_owner_events_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the fork owner. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_followers_url.name: forkee_owner_followers_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_followers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the fork owner's followers. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_following_url.name: forkee_owner_following_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_following_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the fork owner is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_gists_url.name: forkee_owner_gists_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_gists_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the fork owner's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_organizations_url.name: forkee_owner_organizations_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_organizations_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the fork owner belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_received_events_url.name: forkee_owner_received_events_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_received_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the fork owner. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_repos_url.name: forkee_owner_repos_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_repos_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the fork owner's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_starred_url.name: forkee_owner_starred_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_starred_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the fork owner. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_owner_subscriptions_url.name: forkee_owner_subscriptions_url
doc.dw.forkee_owner_subscriptions_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the fork owner's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.forkee_license_key.name: forkee_license_key
doc.dw.forkee_license_key.block_contents:
Lowercased SPDX-like short key of the source repo's license (`payload.forkee.license.key` — e.g. `mit`, `apache-2.0`).
Null when the source repo has no detected license.

doc.dw.forkee_license_name.name: forkee_license_name
doc.dw.forkee_license_name.block_contents: Human-readable license name (`payload.forkee.license.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `MIT License`). Nullable.

doc.dw.forkee_license_node_id.name: forkee_license_node_id
doc.dw.forkee_license_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the license (`payload.forkee.license.node_id`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

doc.dw.forkee_license_spdx_id.name: forkee_license_spdx_id
doc.dw.forkee_license_spdx_id.block_contents: Lowercased official SPDX identifier of the license (`payload.forkee.license.spdx_id` — e.g. `mit`, `gpl-3.0`). Nullable.

doc.dw.forkee_license_object_url.name: forkee_license_object_url
doc.dw.forkee_license_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the license object (`payload.forkee.license.url`). Case-sensitive reference link. Nullable.

doc.dw.comment_author_association.name: comment_author_association
doc.dw.comment_author_association.block_contents:
Lowercased relationship of the comment author to the repo (`payload.comment.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`,
`first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`. Typically null in the GH Archive public feed.

doc.dw.comment_issue_url.name: comment_issue_url
doc.dw.comment_issue_url.block_contents: REST API URL of the parent issue/PR the comment lives on (`payload.comment.issue_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.ref.name: ref
doc.dw.ref.block_contents:
Short git ref involved in the event (case-sensitive).
  - `create_event` / `delete_event`: the branch or tag name (e.g. `feat/login`, `v1.2.0`) — without the `refs/heads/` or `refs/tags/` prefix.
  - `push_event`: the full ref path `refs/heads/<branch>` or `refs/tags/<tag>` — note the prefix is present here, unlike create/delete.

Nullable on `create_event` when `ref_type = 'repository'` (the repo itself was created and no specific ref applies).

doc.dw.full_ref.name: full_ref
doc.dw.full_ref.block_contents:
Fully qualified git ref path including the `refs/heads/` or `refs/tags/` prefix (case-sensitive).
Populated on `create_event` and `delete_event`; for the analogous field on `push_event` see `ref`.
Nullable when not provided by the upstream payload.

doc.dw.ref_type.name: ref_type
doc.dw.ref_type.block_contents:
Lowercased kind of git ref this event acts on. Not null on `create_event` / `delete_event`.

Per the GitHub Events API:
  - `create_event` — `branch`, `tag`, or `repository`
  - `delete_event` — `branch` or `tag`

Observed in the GH Archive sample: `branch` (dominant), `tag`. `repository` is documented but very rare in the public feed (was deprecated for create events in 2018).

doc.dw.pusher_type.name: pusher_type
doc.dw.pusher_type.block_contents:
Lowercased actor category that performed the push / create / delete (`payload.pusher_type`).
Effectively always `user` in the GH Archive public feed — included for parity with the upstream API schema.

doc.dw.changes_title_from.name: changes_title_from
doc.dw.changes_title_from.block_contents:
Previous title text when `action = 'edited'` and the title changed (`payload.changes.title.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event` and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

doc.dw.changes_body_from.name: changes_body_from
doc.dw.changes_body_from.block_contents:
Previous body markdown when `action = 'edited'` and the body changed (`payload.changes.body.from`, case-sensitive).
Used by `issues_event`, `issue_comment_event`, and `discussion_event`. Null otherwise.

doc.dw.changes_permission_from.name: changes_permission_from
doc.dw.changes_permission_from.block_contents:
Previous permission level when `action = 'edited'` on a `member_event`
(`payload.changes.permission.from`, case-sensitive — e.g. `read`, `write`, `admin`).
Null on `added` and on `edited` rows where permission did not change.

doc.dw.changes_permission_to.name: changes_permission_to
doc.dw.changes_permission_to.block_contents:
New permission level when `action = 'edited'` on a `member_event` (`payload.changes.permission.to`, case-sensitive).
Null on `added` and on `edited` rows where permission did not change.

doc.dw.changes_category_from_name.name: changes_category_from_name
doc.dw.changes_category_from_name.block_contents:
Previous category display name when `action = 'category_changed'` on a `discussion_event`
(`payload.changes.category.from.name`, case-sensitive). Null otherwise.

doc.dw.changes_category_from_slug.name: changes_category_from_slug
doc.dw.changes_category_from_slug.block_contents:
Previous category slug (lowercased) when `action = 'category_changed'` on a `discussion_event`
(`payload.changes.category.from.slug`). Null otherwise.

doc.dw.issue__id.name: issue__id
doc.dw.issue__id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric issue ID (`payload.issue.id`).
Globally unique across all repositories, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into issue-level dimensions.

doc.dw.issue__node_id.name: issue__node_id
doc.dw.issue__node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the issue (`payload.issue.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.issue__number.name: issue__number
doc.dw.issue__number.block_contents:
Issue number within its repository (`payload.issue.number`). NOT globally unique — only unique per repo.
Use `(repo_id, issue_number)` together, or `issue_id` alone for global uniqueness.

doc.dw.issue__body.name: issue__body
doc.dw.issue__body.block_contents:
Issue body markdown (`payload.issue.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.
Nullable when the issue was opened without a description.

doc.dw.issue__state.name: issue__state
doc.dw.issue__state.block_contents:
Lowercased open/closed state of the issue (`payload.issue.state`).
Observed values: `open`, `closed`.

doc.dw.issue__state_reason.name: issue__state_reason
doc.dw.issue__state_reason.block_contents:
Lowercased reason the issue is in its current state (`payload.issue.state_reason`).
Observed values: `completed`, `not_planned`, `reopened`, `duplicate`. Null when not applicable.

doc.dw.issue__author_association.name: issue__author_association
doc.dw.issue__author_association.block_contents:
Lowercased relationship of the issue author to the repository (`payload.issue.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`, `first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`.
Typically null in GH Archive's public feed — included for parity with the upstream schema.

doc.dw.issue__active_lock_reason.name: issue__active_lock_reason
doc.dw.issue__active_lock_reason.block_contents:
Lowercased reason this issue is locked from comments (`payload.issue.active_lock_reason`).
Observed values: `resolved`, `spam`, `off-topic`, `too heated`. Null when the issue is not locked.

doc.dw.issue__locked.name: issue__locked
doc.dw.issue__locked.block_contents: Whether the issue is currently locked from new comments (`payload.issue.locked`). Boolean.

doc.dw.issue__draft.name: issue__draft
doc.dw.issue__draft.block_contents:
Whether the issue is a draft (`payload.issue.draft`). Boolean.
Only meaningful when the row represents a pull-request-style issue
(see `issue_pull_request_object_url` for the discriminator).

doc.dw.issue__comments.name: issue__comments
doc.dw.issue__comments.block_contents: Count of comments currently on the issue (`payload.issue.comments`). Snapshot at event time.

doc.dw.issue__labels.name: issue__labels
doc.dw.issue__labels.block_contents:
JSON array of label objects attached to the issue (`payload.issue.labels`).
Each element follows the label-object shape (`id`, `name`, `color`, `description`, ...).
Stored as `array<json>` — unnest with `unnest(issue_labels)` and project per-element fields via `json_value`.

doc.dw.issue__assignees.name: issue__assignees
doc.dw.issue__assignees.block_contents:
JSON array of assignee user objects on the issue (`payload.issue.assignees`).
Each element follows the user-object shape.
Stored as `array<json>` — unnest and project as needed.

doc.dw.issue__created_at.name: issue__created_at
doc.dw.issue__created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the issue was created (`payload.issue.created_at`). DATETIME, not TIMESTAMP.

doc.dw.issue__updated_at.name: issue__updated_at
doc.dw.issue__updated_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime the issue was last updated (`payload.issue.updated_at`).
Refreshed on any state/labels/comments change.

doc.dw.issue__closed_at.name: issue__closed_at
doc.dw.issue__closed_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime the issue was most recently closed (`payload.issue.closed_at`).
Null when `issue_state = 'open'`.

doc.dw.issue__object_url.name: issue__object_url
doc.dw.issue__object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the issue (`payload.issue.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/issues/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__html_url.name: issue__html_url
doc.dw.issue__html_url.block_contents: GitHub web URL for the issue page (`payload.issue.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__repository_url.name: issue__repository_url
doc.dw.issue__repository_url.block_contents: GitHub REST API URL for the parent repository (`payload.issue.repository_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__labels_url.name: issue__labels_url
doc.dw.issue__labels_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the issue's labels (`payload.issue.labels_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__comments_url.name: issue__comments_url
doc.dw.issue__comments_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue's comments collection (`payload.issue.comments_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__events_url.name: issue__events_url
doc.dw.issue__events_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue's timeline events (`payload.issue.events_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__timeline_url.name: issue__timeline_url
doc.dw.issue__timeline_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue's full timeline (`payload.issue.timeline_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_id.name: issue__user_id
doc.dw.issue__user_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric user ID of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.id`). Stable across login renames.

doc.dw.issue__user_node_id.name: issue__user_node_id
doc.dw.issue__user_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.issue__user_name.name: issue__user_name
doc.dw.issue__user_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

doc.dw.issue__user_type.name: issue__user_type
doc.dw.issue__user_type.block_contents:
Lowercased account type of the issue author (`payload.issue.user.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`, `organization`, `mannequin`.

doc.dw.issue__user_user_view_type.name: issue__user_user_view_type
doc.dw.issue__user_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility for the issue author (`payload.issue.user.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

doc.dw.issue__user_gravatar_id.name: issue__user_gravatar_id
doc.dw.issue__user_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier (`payload.issue.user.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.issue__user_site_admin.name: issue__user_site_admin
doc.dw.issue__user_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the issue author is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.user.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.issue__user_avatar_url.name: issue__user_avatar_url
doc.dw.issue__user_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the issue author (`payload.issue.user.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

doc.dw.issue__user_object_url.name: issue__user_object_url
doc.dw.issue__user_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue author user object (`payload.issue.user.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_html_url.name: issue__user_html_url
doc.dw.issue__user_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the issue author's profile (`payload.issue.user.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_events_url.name: issue__user_events_url
doc.dw.issue__user_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_followers_url.name: issue__user_followers_url
doc.dw.issue__user_followers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue author's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_following_url.name: issue__user_following_url
doc.dw.issue__user_following_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the issue author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_gists_url.name: issue__user_gists_url
doc.dw.issue__user_gists_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the issue author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_organizations_url.name: issue__user_organizations_url
doc.dw.issue__user_organizations_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the issue author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_received_events_url.name: issue__user_received_events_url
doc.dw.issue__user_received_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_repos_url.name: issue__user_repos_url
doc.dw.issue__user_repos_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_starred_url.name: issue__user_starred_url
doc.dw.issue__user_starred_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the issue author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__user_subscriptions_url.name: issue__user_subscriptions_url
doc.dw.issue__user_subscriptions_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the issue author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__assignee_id.name: issue__assignee_id
doc.dw.issue__assignee_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric user ID of the issue's primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.id`).
Null when the issue has no assignee. For the full list of assignees see `issue_assignees`.

doc.dw.issue__assignee_node_id.name: issue__assignee_node_id
doc.dw.issue__assignee_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.issue__assignee_name.name: issue__assignee_name
doc.dw.issue__assignee_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.issue__assignee_type.name: issue__assignee_type
doc.dw.issue__assignee_type.block_contents:
Lowercased account type of the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`.

doc.dw.issue__assignee_user_view_type.name: issue__assignee_user_view_type
doc.dw.issue__assignee_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility for the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.user_view_type`).

doc.dw.issue__assignee_site_admin.name: issue__assignee_site_admin
doc.dw.issue__assignee_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the primary assignee is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.assignee.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.issue__assignee_avatar_url.name: issue__assignee_avatar_url
doc.dw.issue__assignee_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the primary assignee (`payload.issue.assignee.avatar_url`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.issue__assignee_object_url.name: issue__assignee_object_url
doc.dw.issue__assignee_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the primary assignee user object (`payload.issue.assignee.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__assignee_html_url.name: issue__assignee_html_url
doc.dw.issue__assignee_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the primary assignee's profile page (`payload.issue.assignee.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_id.name: issue__milestone_id
doc.dw.issue__milestone_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric milestone ID attached to the issue (`payload.issue.milestone.id`). Null when no milestone is set.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_node_id.name: issue__milestone_node_id
doc.dw.issue__milestone_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_number.name: issue__milestone_number
doc.dw.issue__milestone_number.block_contents: Milestone number within its repository (`payload.issue.milestone.number`). NOT globally unique — `(repo_id, milestone_number)` is the natural key.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_open_issues.name: issue__milestone_open_issues
doc.dw.issue__milestone_open_issues.block_contents: Count of open issues currently tied to this milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.open_issues`). Snapshot at event time.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_closed_issues.name: issue__milestone_closed_issues
doc.dw.issue__milestone_closed_issues.block_contents: Count of closed issues currently tied to this milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.closed_issues`). Snapshot at event time.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_due_on.name: issue__milestone_due_on
doc.dw.issue__milestone_due_on.block_contents: UTC datetime the milestone is due (`payload.issue.milestone.due_on`). Nullable.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_created_at.name: issue__milestone_created_at
doc.dw.issue__milestone_created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the milestone was created (`payload.issue.milestone.created_at`).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_updated_at.name: issue__milestone_updated_at
doc.dw.issue__milestone_updated_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the milestone was last updated (`payload.issue.milestone.updated_at`).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_closed_at.name: issue__milestone_closed_at
doc.dw.issue__milestone_closed_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the milestone was most recently closed (`payload.issue.milestone.closed_at`). Null while open.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_object_url.name: issue__milestone_object_url
doc.dw.issue__milestone_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the milestone object (`payload.issue.milestone.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_html_url.name: issue__milestone_html_url
doc.dw.issue__milestone_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the milestone page (`payload.issue.milestone.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_labels_url.name: issue__milestone_labels_url
doc.dw.issue__milestone_labels_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the milestone's associated labels (`payload.issue.milestone.labels_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_id.name: issue__milestone_creator_id
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric user ID of the user who created the milestone (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.id`).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_node_id.name: issue__milestone_creator_node_id
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_name.name: issue__milestone_creator_name
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_type.name: issue__milestone_creator_type
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.type`).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_site_adm.name: issue__milestone_creator_site_admin
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_site_adm.block_contents: Whether the milestone creator is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_avatar_u.name: issue__milestone_creator_avatar_url
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_avatar_u.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the milestone creator (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.avatar_url`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_object_u.name: issue__milestone_creator_object_url
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_object_u.block_contents: REST API URL for the milestone creator user object (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_html_url.name: issue__milestone_creator_html_url
doc.dw.issue__milestone_creator_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the milestone creator's profile page (`payload.issue.milestone.creator.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__pull_request_object_url.name: issue__pull_request_object_url
doc.dw.issue__pull_request_object_url.block_contents:
REST API URL of the linked pull request (`payload.issue.pull_request.url`, case-sensitive).
**Presence (non-null) of this field is the canonical discriminator that the issue row actually represents a pull request** — GitHub models PRs as a specialization of issues.

doc.dw.issue__pull_request_html_url.name: issue__pull_request_html_url
doc.dw.issue__pull_request_html_url.block_contents: Web URL of the linked pull request (`payload.issue.pull_request.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__pull_request_diff_url.name: issue__pull_request_diff_url
doc.dw.issue__pull_request_diff_url.block_contents: URL to the PR's diff view (`payload.issue.pull_request.diff_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__pull_request_patch_url.name: issue__pull_request_patch_url
doc.dw.issue__pull_request_patch_url.block_contents: URL to the PR's patch file (`payload.issue.pull_request.patch_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.issue__pull_request_merged_at.name: issue__pull_request_merged_at
doc.dw.issue__pull_request_merged_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime the linked pull request was merged (`payload.issue.pull_request.merged_at`).
Null while the PR is unmerged. Only populated on PR-style issues.

doc.dw.master_branch.name: master_branch
doc.dw.master_branch.block_contents:
Repository's default branch name at the time the create event fired (`payload.master_branch`, case-sensitive).
Populated on `create_event` regardless of `ref_type`.
Note: the field name predates GitHub's rename to "default branch" — content can be `main`, `master`, or any branch name.

doc.dw.description__repo.name: description__repo
doc.dw.description__repo.block_contents:
Repository description text as of the event (`payload.description`, case-sensitive).
Populated on `create_event` and primarily meaningful when `ref_type = 'repository'`; nullable otherwise.
The yml column is literally named `description` — this doc block is renamed to `description__repo` to avoid colliding with the more common usage of the word.

doc.dw.reactions__total_count.name: reactions__total_count
doc.dw.reactions__total_count.block_contents:
Total number of emoji reactions on the parent object (issue / discussion / comment).
Sum of the 8 individual reaction-type counts. Zero when the API returned no `reactions` block.

doc.dw.reactions__plus_one_count.name: reactions__plus_one_count
doc.dw.reactions__plus_one_count.block_contents: Count of 👍 (`+1`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__minus_one_count.name: reactions__minus_one_count
doc.dw.reactions__minus_one_count.block_contents: Count of 👎 (`-1`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__laugh_count.name: reactions__laugh_count
doc.dw.reactions__laugh_count.block_contents: Count of 😄 (`laugh`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__confused_count.name: reactions__confused_count
doc.dw.reactions__confused_count.block_contents: Count of 😕 (`confused`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__heart_count.name: reactions__heart_count
doc.dw.reactions__heart_count.block_contents: Count of ❤️ (`heart`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__hooray_count.name: reactions__hooray_count
doc.dw.reactions__hooray_count.block_contents: Count of 🎉 (`hooray`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__rocket_count.name: reactions__rocket_count
doc.dw.reactions__rocket_count.block_contents: Count of 🚀 (`rocket`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.reactions__eyes_count.name: reactions__eyes_count
doc.dw.reactions__eyes_count.block_contents: Count of 👀 (`eyes`) reactions on the parent object.

doc.dw.release_id.name: release_id
doc.dw.release_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric release ID (`payload.release.id`). Globally unique, stable.

doc.dw.release_node_id.name: release_node_id
doc.dw.release_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the release (`payload.release.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.release_tag_name.name: release_tag_name
doc.dw.release_tag_name.block_contents: Git tag name the release is built on (`payload.release.tag_name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `v1.2.0`).

doc.dw.release_target_commitish.name: release_target_commitish
doc.dw.release_target_commitish.block_contents:
Branch or commit SHA the release was cut from (`payload.release.target_commitish`, case-sensitive).
Typically a branch name like `main`; can be a full SHA for releases targeting a specific commit.

doc.dw.release_name.name: release_name
doc.dw.release_name.block_contents:
Display name of the release (`payload.release.name`, case-sensitive).
Often null when the tag name is used as the display label.

doc.dw.release_body.name: release_body
doc.dw.release_body.block_contents: Release notes markdown (`payload.release.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.

doc.dw.release_short_description_html.name: release_short_description_html
doc.dw.release_short_description_html.block_contents:
Pre-rendered HTML snippet of the release notes (`payload.release.short_description_html`, case-sensitive).
Truncated; see `release_is_short_description_html_truncated`.

doc.dw.release_draft.name: release_draft
doc.dw.release_draft.block_contents: Whether the release is a draft (`payload.release.draft`). Boolean — drafts rarely appear in the public feed since `published` is the only action emitted.

doc.dw.release_prerelease.name: release_prerelease
doc.dw.release_prerelease.block_contents: Whether the release is marked as pre-release (`payload.release.prerelease`). Boolean.

doc.dw.release_immutable.name: release_immutable
doc.dw.release_immutable.block_contents: Whether the release is marked immutable (`payload.release.immutable`). Boolean.

doc.dw.release_created_at.name: release_created_at
doc.dw.release_created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the release object was created (`payload.release.created_at`).

doc.dw.release_updated_at.name: release_updated_at
doc.dw.release_updated_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the release was last updated (`payload.release.updated_at`).

doc.dw.release_published_at.name: release_published_at
doc.dw.release_published_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the release was published (`payload.release.published_at`). The canonical "release time" for analytics.

doc.dw.release_object_url.name: release_object_url
doc.dw.release_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the release object (`payload.release.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_html_url.name: release_html_url
doc.dw.release_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the release page (`payload.release.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_assets_url.name: release_assets_url
doc.dw.release_assets_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the release's asset collection (`payload.release.assets_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_upload_url.name: release_upload_url
doc.dw.release_upload_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for uploading new assets to the release (`payload.release.upload_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_tarball_url.name: release_tarball_url
doc.dw.release_tarball_url.block_contents: URL to download the source code tarball at the release tag (`payload.release.tarball_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_zipball_url.name: release_zipball_url
doc.dw.release_zipball_url.block_contents: URL to download the source code zip at the release tag (`payload.release.zipball_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_discussion_url.name: release_discussion_url
doc.dw.release_discussion_url.block_contents:
Web URL for the linked Discussions thread (`payload.release.discussion_url`).
Nullable — only set when discussions are enabled and linked.

doc.dw.release_author_id.name: release_author_id
doc.dw.release_author_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric user ID of the release author (`payload.release.author.id`).

doc.dw.release_author_node_id.name: release_author_node_id
doc.dw.release_author_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the release author (`payload.release.author.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.release_author_name.name: release_author_name
doc.dw.release_author_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the release author (`payload.release.author.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.release_author_type.name: release_author_type
doc.dw.release_author_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the release author (`payload.release.author.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

doc.dw.release_author_user_view_type.name: release_author_user_view_type
doc.dw.release_author_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the release author (`payload.release.author.user_view_type`).

doc.dw.release_author_gravatar_id.name: release_author_gravatar_id
doc.dw.release_author_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the release author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.release_author_site_admin.name: release_author_site_admin
doc.dw.release_author_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the release author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

doc.dw.release_author_avatar_url.name: release_author_avatar_url
doc.dw.release_author_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the release author (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

doc.dw.release_author_object_url.name: release_author_object_url
doc.dw.release_author_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the release author user object (`payload.release.author.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_html_url.name: release_author_html_url
doc.dw.release_author_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the release author's profile (`payload.release.author.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_events_url.name: release_author_events_url
doc.dw.release_author_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the release author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_followers_url.name: release_author_followers_url
doc.dw.release_author_followers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the release author's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_following_url.name: release_author_following_url
doc.dw.release_author_following_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the release author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_gists_url.name: release_author_gists_url
doc.dw.release_author_gists_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the release author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_organizations_url.name: release_author_organizations_url
doc.dw.release_author_organizations_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the release author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_received_events_ur.name: release_author_received_events_url
doc.dw.release_author_received_events_ur.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the release author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_repos_url.name: release_author_repos_url
doc.dw.release_author_repos_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the release author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_starred_url.name: release_author_starred_url
doc.dw.release_author_starred_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the release author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_author_subscriptions_url.name: release_author_subscriptions_url
doc.dw.release_author_subscriptions_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the release author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.release_assets.name: release_assets
doc.dw.release_assets.block_contents:
JSON array of release asset objects (`payload.release.assets`, `ARRAY<JSON>`).
Each element describes a binary attached to the release (name, content type, size, download_count, urls, uploader, ...).
Asset count varies 0 → hundreds per release; kept as `ARRAY<JSON>` to preserve the per-event grain.
Unnest with `unnest(release_assets)` and project per-element fields via `json_value`.

doc.dw.push_id.name: push_id
doc.dw.push_id.block_contents:
GitHub's numeric ID for the push itself (`payload.push_id`). Distinct from `event_id`.
Globally unique. A single push that touches multiple refs may produce multiple events sharing one push context,
though in practice 1:1 with `event_id`.

doc.dw.push_head.name: push_head
doc.dw.push_head.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the branch after the push (`payload.head`).
Lowercased for case-insensitive comparison.

doc.dw.push_before.name: push_before
doc.dw.push_before.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the branch before the push (`payload.before`).
Equals `0000000000000000000000000000000000000000` when the push created a new ref.

doc.dw.push_repository_id.name: push_repository_id
doc.dw.push_repository_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric repo ID echoed inside the push payload (`payload.repository_id`).
Should equal the event-level `repo_id` — surfaced separately for fidelity to the upstream shape.

doc.dw.gollum_pages.name: gollum_pages
doc.dw.gollum_pages.block_contents:
Repeated nested column — one element per wiki page changed by this event (`payload.pages`, `ARRAY<STRUCT<...>>`).
Kept as a repeated struct so the `event_id` grain stays intact (one push can touch many pages).
Analyze per-page with `cross join unnest(pages) as p`.

doc.dw.gollum_pages_page_name.name: gollum_pages_page_name
doc.dw.gollum_pages_page_name.block_contents: URL slug of the wiki page (`payload.pages[].page_name`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.gollum_pages_title.name: gollum_pages_title
doc.dw.gollum_pages_title.block_contents: Display title of the wiki page (`payload.pages[].title`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.gollum_pages_summary.name: gollum_pages_summary
doc.dw.gollum_pages_summary.block_contents: Optional commit-message-like summary of the page change (`payload.pages[].summary`, case-sensitive). Often null.

doc.dw.gollum_pages_action.name: gollum_pages_action
doc.dw.gollum_pages_action.block_contents:
Lowercased per-page action — what happened to this specific page. Observed values: `created`, `edited`.
Note: lives inside the array element, not at the event root (the `gollum_event` itself has no top-level `action`).

doc.dw.gollum_pages_sha.name: gollum_pages_sha
doc.dw.gollum_pages_sha.block_contents: Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA of the page revision (`payload.pages[].sha`).

doc.dw.gollum_pages_html_url.name: gollum_pages_html_url
doc.dw.gollum_pages_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the wiki page on GitHub (`payload.pages[].html_url`, case-sensitive). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment_id.name: comment_id
doc.dw.comment_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric ID of the comment (`payload.comment.id`). Globally unique across all comments of this kind.

doc.dw.comment_node_id.name: comment_node_id
doc.dw.comment_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the comment (`payload.comment.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.comment_body.name: comment_body
doc.dw.comment_body.block_contents: Comment markdown body (`payload.comment.body`, case-sensitive). Can be long.

doc.dw.comment_created_at.name: comment_created_at
doc.dw.comment_created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the comment was created (`payload.comment.created_at`).

doc.dw.comment_updated_at.name: comment_updated_at
doc.dw.comment_updated_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime the comment was last updated (`payload.comment.updated_at`).
Refreshed on edits and on reaction count changes.

doc.dw.comment_object_url.name: comment_object_url
doc.dw.comment_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the comment object (`payload.comment.url`). Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

doc.dw.comment_html_url.name: comment_html_url
doc.dw.comment_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the comment in the GitHub UI (`payload.comment.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment_user_id.name: comment_user_id
doc.dw.comment_user_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric user ID of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.id`). Should equal event-level `user_id`.

doc.dw.comment_user_node_id.name: comment_user_node_id
doc.dw.comment_user_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.comment_user_name.name: comment_user_name
doc.dw.comment_user_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.comment_user_type.name: comment_user_type
doc.dw.comment_user_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

doc.dw.comment_user_user_view_type.name: comment_user_user_view_type
doc.dw.comment_user_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the comment author (`payload.comment.user.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

doc.dw.comment_user_gravatar_id.name: comment_user_gravatar_id
doc.dw.comment_user_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the comment author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.comment_user_site_admin.name: comment_user_site_admin
doc.dw.comment_user_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the comment author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

doc.dw.comment_user_avatar_url.name: comment_user_avatar_url
doc.dw.comment_user_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the comment author (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

doc.dw.comment_user_object_url.name: comment_user_object_url
doc.dw.comment_user_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the comment author user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment_user_html_url.name: comment_user_html_url
doc.dw.comment_user_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the comment author's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment__path.name: comment__path
doc.dw.comment__path.block_contents:
File path the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.path`, case-sensitive).
For `commit_comment_event`: null when the comment is on the commit as a whole (not a specific file).
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: always populated — the file in the PR diff the comment targets.
Note the double-underscore in the column name distinguishes it from the comment object itself.

doc.dw.comment_position.name: comment_position
doc.dw.comment_position.block_contents:
Diff-relative position the comment is anchored to (`payload.comment.position`).
For `commit_comment_event`: position in the commit's diff.
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: current position in the PR diff — updated when the PR head moves;
null if the comment is now outdated (the original anchor is in `comment_original_position`).

doc.dw.commit_id.name: commit_id
doc.dw.commit_id.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char SHA the comment is associated with (`payload.comment.commit_id`).
Lowercased for case-insensitive join. The join key into commit-level data.
For `commit_comment_event`: SHA of the commit being commented on (stable).
For `pull_request_review_comment_event`: SHA the comment was rebased onto in current state —
the original SHA the comment was first written against is in `original_commit_id`.

doc.dw.comment_line.name: comment_line
doc.dw.comment_line.block_contents:
Line number in the file the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.line`).
Used by `commit_comment_event` only. Null when commenting on a non-line target.

doc.dw.action.name: action
doc.dw.action.block_contents:
Lowercased value of `payload.action` — the discriminator for what kind of activity an event represents inside its event type.
Not null on every event type that carries this field
(i.e. all event types except `create_event`, `delete_event`, `public_event`, `push_event` — and `gollum_event`, which carries `action` inside each element of its `pages` array instead of at the event root).

The accepted value set is event-type-specific. Values observed in the GH Archive public feed (consistent with the GitHub Events API reference):

  - `issues_event`                        — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled`
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — `created` (GH Archive's public feed effectively never emits `edited` / `deleted`)
  - `pull_request_event`                  — `opened` / `closed` / `reopened` / `assigned` / `unassigned` / `labeled` / `unlabeled` / `merged`
                                            (`merged` is a GH-Archive-normalized action; the upstream webhook fires `closed` with `pull_request.merged = true`.)
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — `created` / `updated` / `dismissed`
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — `created`
  - `commit_comment_event`                — `created`
  - `release_event`                       — `published`
  - `watch_event`                         — `started` (the only action — despite the legacy event name, this represents a star)
  - `fork_event`                          — `forked`
  - `member_event`                        — `added` (collaborator accepted invitation)
  - `discussion_event`                    — `created` (most common in the public feed; other discussion actions are rare)

doc.dw.assignee__id.name: assignee__id
doc.dw.assignee__id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric user ID of the assignee (`payload.assignee.id`).
Stable across login renames. Populated only when `action in ('assigned', 'unassigned')`.

doc.dw.assignee__node_id.name: assignee__node_id
doc.dw.assignee__node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the assignee user (`payload.assignee.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.assignee__name.name: assignee__name
doc.dw.assignee__name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the assignee (`payload.assignee.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

doc.dw.assignee__type.name: assignee__type
doc.dw.assignee__type.block_contents:
Lowercased account type of the assignee (`payload.assignee.type`).
Observed: `user`, `bot`, `organization`, `mannequin`.

doc.dw.assignee__user_view_type.name: assignee__user_view_type
doc.dw.assignee__user_view_type.block_contents:
Lowercased visibility flag for the assignee account
(`payload.assignee.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

doc.dw.assignee__gravatar_id.name: assignee__gravatar_id
doc.dw.assignee__gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier (`payload.assignee.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty for modern accounts.

doc.dw.assignee__site_admin.name: assignee__site_admin
doc.dw.assignee__site_admin.block_contents: Whether the assignee is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.assignee.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.assignee__avatar_url.name: assignee__avatar_url
doc.dw.assignee__avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the assignee (`payload.assignee.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

doc.dw.assignee__object_url.name: assignee__object_url
doc.dw.assignee__object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the assignee user object (`payload.assignee.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.assignee__html_url.name: assignee__html_url
doc.dw.assignee__html_url.block_contents:
GitHub web URL for the assignee profile page (`payload.assignee.html_url`).
Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.assignee__events_url.name: assignee__events_url
doc.dw.assignee__events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the assignee user. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__followers_url.name: assignee__followers_url
doc.dw.assignee__followers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the assignee's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__following_url.name: assignee__following_url
doc.dw.assignee__following_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the assignee is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__gists_url.name: assignee__gists_url
doc.dw.assignee__gists_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the assignee's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__organizations_url.name: assignee__organizations_url
doc.dw.assignee__organizations_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the assignee belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__received_events_url.name: assignee__received_events_url
doc.dw.assignee__received_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the assignee. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__repos_url.name: assignee__repos_url
doc.dw.assignee__repos_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the assignee's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__starred_url.name: assignee__starred_url
doc.dw.assignee__starred_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the assignee. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.assignee__subscriptions_url.name: assignee__subscriptions_url
doc.dw.assignee__subscriptions_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the assignee's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.created_at__scd1.name: created_at__scd1
doc.dw.created_at__scd1.block_contents:
UTC datetime the entity was first observed in any GH Archive event we have ingested.
Set on first insert into this dimension and **never updated** thereafter.
(distinct from the event-level `created_at`, which is the timestamp of a specific event row.)
Not null.

doc.dw.updated_at__scd1.name: updated_at__scd1
doc.dw.updated_at__scd1.block_contents:
UTC datetime the most recent time **any tracked attribute on this row actually changed**.
Equals `created_at` on first insert; advances only when this batch's observed attributes differ from
the values previously stored in this dimension. Stays put when an entity is re-observed with identical
attributes — i.e. it is a true change marker, not a last-seen timestamp.
Not null.

doc.dw.events_count.name: events_count
doc.dw.events_count.block_contents:
Total number of GitHub events triggered by this entity during the snapshot period
(day / week / month, depending on the table's grain). Always `> 0` — periods with
zero events do not produce a row (the AU table is a sparse activation snapshot,
not a dense calendar cross-join).

In rollup tables (weekly / monthly) this is the sum of the underlying daily
`events_count` values across the period.

doc.dw.active_days.name: active_days
doc.dw.active_days.block_contents:
Number of distinct calendar days within the snapshot period the entity was
active (triggered at least one event). Range:
  - daily tables:   always 1 (kept so daily / weekly / monthly share a column shape).
  - weekly tables:  1–7
  - monthly tables: 1–31 (28–31 depending on the calendar month)

Always `≥ 1` — periods with zero active days do not produce a row.

In rollup tables (weekly / monthly) this is the sum of the underlying daily
`active_days` values across the period, which — because the daily table is
unique on `(<entity>_id, date)` — equals the count of distinct active days
in that period.

doc.dw.pull_request_review_id.name: pull_request_review_id
doc.dw.pull_request_review_id.block_contents:
Numeric ID of the parent review submission this comment belongs to (`payload.comment.pull_request_review_id`).
Joins back to `core_fact__pull_request_review_events.review_id`.

doc.dw.in_reply_to_id.name: in_reply_to_id
doc.dw.in_reply_to_id.block_contents:
When the comment is a reply, the `comment_id` of the parent comment in the thread (`payload.comment.in_reply_to_id`).
Null for top-level comments.

doc.dw.original_commit_id.name: original_commit_id
doc.dw.original_commit_id.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char SHA the comment was originally written against (`payload.comment.original_commit_id`).
Stable across PR head moves, unlike `commit_id` (which tracks the current head).

doc.dw.comment_diff_hunk.name: comment_diff_hunk
doc.dw.comment_diff_hunk.block_contents:
Snippet of the diff hunk the comment is attached to (`payload.comment.diff_hunk`, case-sensitive).
Useful for showing context without re-fetching the diff.

doc.dw.comment_original_position.name: comment_original_position
doc.dw.comment_original_position.block_contents:
Diff-relative position the comment was originally placed at (`payload.comment.original_position`).
Stable across PR head moves, unlike `comment_position`.

doc.dw.comment_subject_type.name: comment_subject_type
doc.dw.comment_subject_type.block_contents:
Lowercased granularity of the comment target (`payload.comment.subject_type`).
Observed: `line` (most common), `file` (whole-file comment).

doc.dw.comment_pull_request_url.name: comment_pull_request_url
doc.dw.comment_pull_request_url.block_contents: REST API URL of the parent PR (`payload.comment.pull_request_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment_self_link_url.name: comment_self_link_url
doc.dw.comment_self_link_url.block_contents: Self-link to the comment (`payload.comment._links.self.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment_html_link_url.name: comment_html_link_url
doc.dw.comment_html_link_url.block_contents: HTML link to the comment (`payload.comment._links.html.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.comment_pull_request_link_url.name: comment_pull_request_link_url
doc.dw.comment_pull_request_link_url.block_contents: Link to the parent PR (`payload.comment._links.pull_request.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.event_id.name: event_id
doc.dw.event_id.block_contents:
GitHub's globally unique numeric event identifier (`id` field of the Events API).
Stable, never reused. Not null.

doc.dw.event_name.name: event_name
doc.dw.event_name.block_contents:
Lowercased snake_case form of GitHub's PascalCase event `type`.
(e.g. `PushEvent` -> `push_event`)
Acts as the discriminator for the shape of `payload`.
Not null.

Observed values (GitHub Events API, public feed):
  - `push_event`                          — commits pushed to a branch
  - `create_event`                        — branch / tag / repo created
  - `delete_event`                        — branch or tag deleted
  - `pull_request_event`                  — PR opened / closed / edited / etc.
  - `pull_request_review_event`           — PR review submitted / dismissed
  - `pull_request_review_comment_event`   — comment on a PR diff
  - `issues_event`                        — issue opened / closed / labeled / etc.
  - `issue_comment_event`                 — comment on an issue or PR
  - `commit_comment_event`                — comment on a commit
  - `watch_event`                         — user starred a repo
  - `fork_event`                          — repo was forked
  - `release_event`                       — release published
  - `member_event`                        — collaborator added / removed
  - `public_event`                        — private repo flipped to public
