  - `gollum_event`                        — wiki page created / updated
  - `discussion_event`                    — repo discussion activity

doc.dw.created_date.name: created_date
doc.dw.created_date.block_contents:
UTC date that it occurred.
Equals `DATE(created_at)`. Not null.

doc.dw.created_at.name: created_at
doc.dw.created_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime time it occurred.
Not null.

doc.dw.ingested_date.name: ingested_date
doc.dw.ingested_date.block_contents:
UTC date the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Equals `DATE(ingested_at)`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_date`.)
Not null.

doc.dw.ingested_at.name: ingested_at
doc.dw.ingested_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime the upstream loader wrote this row to `raw__gharchive.ext__events`.
Use this to debug pipeline lag, not for analytics.
(analytics should use `created_at`.)
Not null.

doc.dw.user_id.name: user_id
doc.dw.user_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric user ID of the actor that triggered the event (`actor.id`).
Stable across login renames.
(prefer this over `user_name` as a join key into user-level dimensions.)
Not null.

doc.dw.user_name.name: user_name
doc.dw.user_name.block_contents:
Lowercased GitHub login of the actor (`actor.login`, lowered).
Canonical, case-insensitive join key for matching against other tables that store user handles.
Pairs 1:1 with `user_id` within a single point in time, but can change if a user renames.
(`user_id` is the more durable key.)
Not null.

doc.dw.user_display_name.name: user_display_name
doc.dw.user_display_name.block_contents:
Display form of the actor's login (`actor.display_login`), case preserved.
Use for rendering in UI / reports.
Do NOT join on this — use `user_id` or `user_name`.
Not null.

doc.dw.user_object_url.name: user_object_url
doc.dw.user_object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the actor user object (`actor.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/users/<login>`).
Case-sensitive. Reference link, not a join key. Not null.

doc.dw.user_image_url.name: user_image_url
doc.dw.user_image_url.block_contents:
Avatar image URL for the actor (`actor.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive. Not null.

doc.dw.repo_id.name: repo_id
doc.dw.repo_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric repository ID (`repo.id`).
Stable across repo renames / transfers.
(preferred join key into repo-level dimensions.)

Nullable in rare edge cases (~6 in 1,000,000) where the upstream Events API emitted an empty `repo` object
— observed on `fork_event` rows whose source repo became unavailable.

doc.dw.repo_name.name: repo_name
doc.dw.repo_name.block_contents:
Lowercased `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug (`repo.name`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for matching against tables that store repo full names.
Can change on rename — `repo_id` is the durable key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

doc.dw.repo_object_url.name: repo_object_url
doc.dw.repo_object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the repo object (`repo.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.
Nullable in the same edge cases as `repo_id`.

doc.dw.org_id.name: org_id
doc.dw.org_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric organization ID (`org.id`).
Populated only when the event is scoped to an organization.
(e.g. activity on a repo owned by an org.)
Null for events on personal repos.

When present, stable across org renames
— preferred join key into org-level dimensions.

doc.dw.org_name.name: org_name
doc.dw.org_name.block_contents:
Lowercased GitHub login of the organization (`org.login`, lowered).
Case-insensitive join key for org dimensions.

Null whenever `org_id` is null (i.e. non-org-scoped events).

doc.dw.org_object_url.name: org_object_url
doc.dw.org_object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the org object
(`org.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/orgs/<login>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

Null whenever `org_id` is null.

doc.dw.org_image_url.name: org_image_url
doc.dw.org_image_url.block_contents:
Avatar image URL for the organization (`org.avatar_url`).
Useful for UI rendering. Case-sensitive.
Null whenever `org_id` is null.

doc.dw.payload.name: payload
doc.dw.payload.block_contents:
Event-type-specific data as a native BigQuery JSON value.
(parsed via `SAFE.PARSE_JSON` from the source string column.)

The schema of this object varies by `event_name`
— see the GitHub Events API reference for the per-type contract: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/using-the-rest-api/github-event-types

Examples:
  - `push_event`         -> `{ ref, head, before, commits[], ... }`
  - `pull_request_event` -> `{ action, number, pull_request{...} }`
  - `issues_event`       -> `{ action, issue{...} }`
  - `release_event`      -> `{ action, release{...} }`

Downstream marts should switch on `event_name` and project the fields they need.
Not null at the row level, but inner paths are best read with `SAFE.` / `JSON_VALUE` / `JSON_QUERY`.

doc.dw.is_public.name: is_public
doc.dw.is_public.block_contents:
Whether the event is publicly visible (`public` field of the Events API).
Effectively always true in the GH Archive public feed; observed FALSE in ~1 in 100,000 rows (legacy / edge
cases).
Not null.

doc.dw.discussion_id.name: discussion_id
doc.dw.discussion_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric discussion ID (`payload.discussion.id`). Globally unique, stable.

doc.dw.discussion_node_id.name: discussion_node_id
doc.dw.discussion_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the discussion (`payload.discussion.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_number.name: discussion_number
doc.dw.discussion_number.block_contents:
Discussion number within its repository (`payload.discussion.number`).
NOT globally unique — use `(repo_id, discussion_number)`.

doc.dw.discussion_body.name: discussion_body
doc.dw.discussion_body.block_contents: Discussion body markdown (`payload.discussion.body`, case-sensitive). Can be very long.

doc.dw.discussion_state.name: discussion_state
doc.dw.discussion_state.block_contents: Lowercased state of the discussion (`payload.discussion.state`). Observed: `open`, `closed`.

doc.dw.discussion_state_reason.name: discussion_state_reason
doc.dw.discussion_state_reason.block_contents:
Lowercased reason for the current state (`payload.discussion.state_reason`).
Observed: `resolved`, `reopened`. Null when not applicable.

doc.dw.discussion_author_association.name: discussion_author_association
doc.dw.discussion_author_association.block_contents:
Lowercased author-vs-repo relationship (`payload.discussion.author_association`).
Defined values per GitHub spec: `owner`, `member`, `collaborator`, `contributor`, `first_time_contributor`,
`first_timer`, `mannequin`, `none`. Often null in the public feed.

doc.dw.discussion_active_lock_reason.name: discussion_active_lock_reason
doc.dw.discussion_active_lock_reason.block_contents: Lowercased reason the discussion is locked (`payload.discussion.active_lock_reason`). Null when not locked.

doc.dw.discussion_locked.name: discussion_locked
doc.dw.discussion_locked.block_contents: Whether the discussion is currently locked (`payload.discussion.locked`). Boolean.

doc.dw.discussion_comments.name: discussion_comments
doc.dw.discussion_comments.block_contents: Count of comments on the discussion (`payload.discussion.comments`). Snapshot at event time.

doc.dw.discussion_labels.name: discussion_labels
doc.dw.discussion_labels.block_contents:
JSON array of label objects on the discussion (`payload.discussion.labels`, `ARRAY<JSON>`).
Each element follows the label-object shape.

doc.dw.discussion_created_at.name: discussion_created_at
doc.dw.discussion_created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the discussion was created (`payload.discussion.created_at`).

doc.dw.discussion_updated_at.name: discussion_updated_at
doc.dw.discussion_updated_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the discussion was last updated (`payload.discussion.updated_at`).

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_at.name: discussion_answer_chosen_at
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_at.block_contents:
UTC datetime an answer was marked on the discussion (`payload.discussion.answer_chosen_at`).
Null when no answer is chosen, or when the category is not answerable.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_html_url.name: discussion_answer_html_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_html_url.block_contents:
Web URL of the chosen answer comment (`payload.discussion.answer_html_url`). Case-sensitive.
Null when no answer is chosen.

doc.dw.discussion_html_url.name: discussion_html_url
doc.dw.discussion_html_url.block_contents: Web URL of the discussion page (`payload.discussion.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_repository_url.name: discussion_repository_url
doc.dw.discussion_repository_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the parent repository (`payload.discussion.repository_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_timeline_url.name: discussion_timeline_url
doc.dw.discussion_timeline_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the discussion timeline (`payload.discussion.timeline_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_id.name: discussion_user_id
doc.dw.discussion_user_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric user ID of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.id`).

doc.dw.discussion_user_node_id.name: discussion_user_node_id
doc.dw.discussion_user_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_user_name.name: discussion_user_name
doc.dw.discussion_user_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.discussion_user_type.name: discussion_user_type
doc.dw.discussion_user_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.type`).

doc.dw.discussion_user_user_view_type.name: discussion_user_user_view_type
doc.dw.discussion_user_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the discussion author (`payload.discussion.user.user_view_type`).

doc.dw.discussion_user_gravatar_id.name: discussion_user_gravatar_id
doc.dw.discussion_user_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the discussion author (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.discussion_user_site_admin.name: discussion_user_site_admin
doc.dw.discussion_user_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the discussion author is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

doc.dw.discussion_user_avatar_url.name: discussion_user_avatar_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the discussion author (case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_user_object_url.name: discussion_user_object_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the discussion author user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_html_url.name: discussion_user_html_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the discussion author's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_events_url.name: discussion_user_events_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the discussion author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_followers_url.name: discussion_user_followers_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_followers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the discussion author's followers. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_following_url.name: discussion_user_following_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_following_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the discussion author is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_gists_url.name: discussion_user_gists_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_gists_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the discussion author's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_organizations_url.name: discussion_user_organizations_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_organizations_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the discussion author belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_received_events_u.name: discussion_user_received_events_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_received_events_u.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the discussion author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_repos_url.name: discussion_user_repos_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_repos_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the discussion author's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_starred_url.name: discussion_user_starred_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_starred_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the discussion author. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_user_subscriptions_url.name: discussion_user_subscriptions_url
doc.dw.discussion_user_subscriptions_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the discussion author's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_category_id.name: discussion_category_id
doc.dw.discussion_category_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric ID of the discussion category (`payload.discussion.category.id`). Categories are per-repo.

doc.dw.discussion_category_node_id.name: discussion_category_node_id
doc.dw.discussion_category_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the discussion category (`payload.discussion.category.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_category_name.name: discussion_category_name
doc.dw.discussion_category_name.block_contents: Display name of the category (`payload.discussion.category.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `Q&A`, `Ideas`).

doc.dw.discussion_category_emoji.name: discussion_category_emoji
doc.dw.discussion_category_emoji.block_contents: Emoji shortcode associated with the category (`payload.discussion.category.emoji`, case-sensitive — e.g. `:bulb:`).

doc.dw.discussion_category_description.name: discussion_category_description
doc.dw.discussion_category_description.block_contents: Free-text description of the category (`payload.discussion.category.description`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_category_slug.name: discussion_category_slug
doc.dw.discussion_category_slug.block_contents: Lowercased URL slug of the category (`payload.discussion.category.slug` — e.g. `q-a`, `ideas`).

doc.dw.discussion_category_is_answerable.name: discussion_category_is_answerable
doc.dw.discussion_category_is_answerable.block_contents: Whether discussions in this category can be marked with an answer (`payload.discussion.category.is_answerable`). Boolean.

doc.dw.discussion_category_repository_id.name: discussion_category_repository_id
doc.dw.discussion_category_repository_id.block_contents: Numeric repo ID the category belongs to (`payload.discussion.category.repository_id`). Equals event-level `repo_id`.

doc.dw.discussion_category_created_at.name: discussion_category_created_at
doc.dw.discussion_category_created_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the category was created (`payload.discussion.category.created_at`).

doc.dw.discussion_category_updated_at.name: discussion_category_updated_at
doc.dw.discussion_category_updated_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the category was last updated (`payload.discussion.category.updated_at`).

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_id.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_id
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric user ID of the user who marked the chosen answer (`payload.discussion.answer_chosen_by.id`).
Null when no answer is chosen.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_node_.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_node_id
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_node_.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the answer-chooser user (case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_name.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_name
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the answer-chooser (`payload.discussion.answer_chosen_by.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_type.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_type
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the answer-chooser.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_user_.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_user_view_type
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_user_.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the answer-chooser.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_grava.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_gravatar_id
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_grava.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_site_.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_site_admin
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_site_.block_contents: Whether the answer-chooser is a GitHub staff/site admin. Boolean.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_avata.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_avatar_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_avata.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the answer-chooser (case-sensitive).

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_objec.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_object_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_objec.block_contents: REST API URL for the answer-chooser user object. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_html_.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_html_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_html_.block_contents: Web URL for the answer-chooser's profile. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_event.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_events_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_event.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the answer-chooser. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_follo.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_followers_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_follo.block_contents: REST API URL for the answer-chooser's followers. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_follo.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_following_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_follo.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the answer-chooser is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_gists.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_gists_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_gists.block_contents: REST API URL template for the answer-chooser's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_organ.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_organizations_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_organ.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the answer-chooser belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_recei.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_received_events_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_recei.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the answer-chooser. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_repos.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_repos_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_repos.block_contents: REST API URL for the answer-chooser's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_starr.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_starred_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_starr.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the answer-chooser. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_subsc.name: discussion_answer_chosen_by_subscriptions_url
doc.dw.discussion_answer_chosen_by_subsc.block_contents: REST API URL for the answer-chooser's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.discussion_reactions_object_url.name: discussion_reactions_object_url
doc.dw.discussion_reactions_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the discussion's reactions collection (`payload.discussion.reactions.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.pr__id.name: pr__id
doc.dw.pr__id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric pull request ID (`payload.pull_request.id`).
Globally unique, stable across renames/transfers — preferred join key into PR-level dimensions.

doc.dw.pr__number.name: pr__number
doc.dw.pr__number.block_contents:
Pull request number within its repository (`payload.pull_request.number`).
NOT globally unique — only unique per repo. Use `(repo_id, pull_request_number)` together as a natural key, or `pull_request_id` alone for global uniqueness.

doc.dw.pr__object_url.name: pr__object_url
doc.dw.pr__object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the pull request (`payload.pull_request.url`,
e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/pulls/<number>`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.pr__head_ref.name: pr__head_ref
doc.dw.pr__head_ref.block_contents:
Branch name on the head (source) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.head.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch that contains the changes being proposed.

doc.dw.pr__head_sha.name: pr__head_sha
doc.dw.pr__head_sha.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the head branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.head.sha`). Lowercased for case-insensitive join.

doc.dw.pr__head_repo_id.name: pr__head_repo_id
doc.dw.pr__head_repo_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.id`).
Differs from `repo_id` (the PR's base repo) on cross-fork PRs.
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

doc.dw.pr__head_repo_name.name: pr__head_repo_name
doc.dw.pr__head_repo_name.block_contents:
`"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.name`, case-sensitive — preserved as-emitted).
Nullable when the head fork has been deleted.

doc.dw.pr__head_repo_object_url.name: pr__head_repo_object_url
doc.dw.pr__head_repo_object_url.block_contents: GitHub REST API URL for the head repository (`payload.pull_request.head.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.pr__base_ref.name: pr__base_ref
doc.dw.pr__base_ref.block_contents:
Branch name on the base (target) side of the PR (`payload.pull_request.base.ref`, case-sensitive).
The branch the PR is targeting for merge.

doc.dw.pr__base_sha.name: pr__base_sha
doc.dw.pr__base_sha.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA at the tip of the base branch when the event fired
(`payload.pull_request.base.sha`).

doc.dw.pr__base_repo_id.name: pr__base_repo_id
doc.dw.pr__base_repo_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric repo ID of the base (target) repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.id`).
Typically equals the event-level `repo_id`.

doc.dw.pr__base_repo_name.name: pr__base_repo_name
doc.dw.pr__base_repo_name.block_contents: `"{owner}/{repo}"` slug of the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.name`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.pr__base_repo_object_url.name: pr__base_repo_object_url
doc.dw.pr__base_repo_object_url.block_contents: GitHub REST API URL for the base repository (`payload.pull_request.base.repo.url`). Case-sensitive reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.review_id.name: review_id
doc.dw.review_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric review ID (`payload.review.id`). Globally unique, stable.

doc.dw.review_node_id.name: review_node_id
doc.dw.review_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the review (`payload.review.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.review_state.name: review_state
doc.dw.review_state.block_contents:
Lowercased state of the review submission (`payload.review.state`).
Observed values: `approved`, `commented`, `changes_requested`, `dismissed`.
The analytical headline — `approved` and `changes_requested` are the typical "decision" reviews;
`commented` reviews carry feedback without an explicit verdict.

doc.dw.review_commit_id.name: review_commit_id
doc.dw.review_commit_id.block_contents:
Lowercased 40-char git commit SHA the review targets (`payload.review.commit_id`).
Pin point in PR history for the review.

doc.dw.review_body.name: review_body
doc.dw.review_body.block_contents:
Review summary markdown body (`payload.review.body`, case-sensitive).
Per-line comments live separately in `pull_request_review_comment_event`.

doc.dw.review_submitted_at.name: review_submitted_at
doc.dw.review_submitted_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the review was submitted (`payload.review.submitted_at`). The canonical "review time" for analytics.

doc.dw.review_updated_at.name: review_updated_at
doc.dw.review_updated_at.block_contents: UTC datetime the review was last updated (`payload.review.updated_at`).

doc.dw.review_html_url.name: review_html_url
doc.dw.review_html_url.block_contents: Web URL to the review on the PR page (`payload.review.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.review_pull_request_url.name: review_pull_request_url
doc.dw.review_pull_request_url.block_contents: REST API URL of the PR the review targets (`payload.review.pull_request_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.review_html_link_url.name: review_html_link_url
doc.dw.review_html_link_url.block_contents: Web URL of the review (`payload.review._links.html.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.review_pull_request_link_url.name: review_pull_request_link_url
doc.dw.review_pull_request_link_url.block_contents: REST API URL of the linked PR (`payload.review._links.pull_request.href`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.review_user_id.name: review_user_id
doc.dw.review_user_id.block_contents: GitHub numeric user ID of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.id`). Should equal event-level `user_id`.

doc.dw.review_user_node_id.name: review_user_node_id
doc.dw.review_user_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.node_id`, case-sensitive).

doc.dw.review_user_name.name: review_user_name
doc.dw.review_user_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.login`, lowered).

doc.dw.review_user_type.name: review_user_type
doc.dw.review_user_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.type`) — e.g. `user`, `bot`.

doc.dw.review_user_user_view_type.name: review_user_user_view_type
doc.dw.review_user_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the reviewer (`payload.review.user.user_view_type`).

doc.dw.review_user_gravatar_id.name: review_user_gravatar_id
doc.dw.review_user_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the reviewer (case-sensitive). Effectively always empty.

doc.dw.review_user_site_admin.name: review_user_site_admin
doc.dw.review_user_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the reviewer is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.review.user.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.review_user_avatar_url.name: review_user_avatar_url
doc.dw.review_user_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the reviewer (case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

doc.dw.review_user_object_url.name: review_user_object_url
doc.dw.review_user_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the reviewer user object (`payload.review.user.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.review_user_html_url.name: review_user_html_url
doc.dw.review_user_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the reviewer's profile (`payload.review.user.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_id.name: member_id
doc.dw.member_id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric user ID of the **target collaborator** (`payload.member.id`).
The user being added to / modified on the repo — distinct from the actor in `user_id` (who performed the change).

doc.dw.member_node_id.name: member_node_id
doc.dw.member_node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the target collaborator (`payload.member.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.member_name.name: member_name
doc.dw.member_name.block_contents: Lowercased GitHub login of the target collaborator (`payload.member.login`, lowered). Case-insensitive join key.

doc.dw.member_type.name: member_type
doc.dw.member_type.block_contents: Lowercased account type of the target collaborator (`payload.member.type`) — typically `user`.

doc.dw.member_user_view_type.name: member_user_view_type
doc.dw.member_user_view_type.block_contents: Lowercased account visibility of the target collaborator (`payload.member.user_view_type` — typically `public`).

doc.dw.member_gravatar_id.name: member_gravatar_id
doc.dw.member_gravatar_id.block_contents: Legacy Gravatar identifier of the target collaborator (`payload.member.gravatar_id`, case-sensitive). Effectively always empty for modern accounts.

doc.dw.member_site_admin.name: member_site_admin
doc.dw.member_site_admin.block_contents: Whether the target collaborator is a GitHub staff/site admin (`payload.member.site_admin`). Boolean.

doc.dw.member_avatar_url.name: member_avatar_url
doc.dw.member_avatar_url.block_contents: Avatar image URL for the target collaborator (`payload.member.avatar_url`, case-sensitive). UI rendering only.

doc.dw.member_object_url.name: member_object_url
doc.dw.member_object_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the target collaborator user object (`payload.member.url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_html_url.name: member_html_url
doc.dw.member_html_url.block_contents: Web URL for the target collaborator profile (`payload.member.html_url`). Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_events_url.name: member_events_url
doc.dw.member_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for events involving the target collaborator. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_followers_url.name: member_followers_url
doc.dw.member_followers_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the target collaborator's followers list. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_following_url.name: member_following_url
doc.dw.member_following_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for users the target collaborator is following. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_gists_url.name: member_gists_url
doc.dw.member_gists_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for the target collaborator's gists. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_organizations_url.name: member_organizations_url
doc.dw.member_organizations_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the orgs the target collaborator belongs to. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_received_events_url.name: member_received_events_url
doc.dw.member_received_events_url.block_contents: REST API URL for events received by the target collaborator. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_repos_url.name: member_repos_url
doc.dw.member_repos_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the target collaborator's repositories. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_starred_url.name: member_starred_url
doc.dw.member_starred_url.block_contents: REST API URL template for repos starred by the target collaborator. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.member_subscriptions_url.name: member_subscriptions_url
doc.dw.member_subscriptions_url.block_contents: REST API URL for the target collaborator's repo subscriptions. Case-sensitive reference link.

doc.dw.date.name: date
doc.dw.date.block_contents:
Calendar date (UTC). Not null.

Usage across tables:
  - `core_snp_fact__daily_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the calendar day
    the entity was active on.
  - `core_snp_fact__weekly_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the **Sunday that
    starts the week** the row reports on (`DATE_TRUNC(..., WEEK(SUNDAY))`).
  - `core_snp_fact__monthly_active_{users,repos,orgs}.date` — the **first day
    of the calendar month** the row reports on (`DATE_TRUNC(..., MONTH)`).

In the weekly / monthly AU rollups the row's measures (`events_count`,
`active_days`) are **period-to-date** from `date` through the current
`batch_date` — partial mid-period, full once the period closes.

doc.dw.label__id.name: label__id
doc.dw.label__id.block_contents:
GitHub numeric label ID (`payload.label.id`). Stable across renames.
Populated only when `action in ('labeled', 'unlabeled')`; null otherwise.

doc.dw.label__node_id.name: label__node_id
doc.dw.label__node_id.block_contents: GraphQL global node ID of the label (`payload.label.node_id`, case-sensitive). Reference, not a join key.

doc.dw.label__name.name: label__name
doc.dw.label__name.block_contents:
Display name of the label (`payload.label.name`, case-sensitive — e.g. `bug`, `good first issue`).
This is what users see in the GitHub UI. Use `label_id` as the durable join key.

doc.dw.label__color.name: label__color
doc.dw.label__color.block_contents:
6-character hex color of the label without the leading `#` (`payload.label.color`, e.g. `d73a4a`).
Case-sensitive but conventionally lowercased.

doc.dw.label__description.name: label__description
doc.dw.label__description.block_contents: Free-text description of the label (`payload.label.description`, case-sensitive). Nullable.

doc.dw.label__default.name: label__default
doc.dw.label__default.block_contents:
Whether this label is one of GitHub's default labels for new repositories
(`payload.label.default`). Boolean.

doc.dw.label__object_url.name: label__object_url
doc.dw.label__object_url.block_contents:
GitHub REST API URL for the label object (`payload.label.url`, e.g. `https://api.github.com/repos/<owner>/<repo>/labels/<name>`).
Case-sensitive reference link, not a join key.

doc.dw.labels__array.name: labels__array
doc.dw.labels__array.block_contents:
JSON array of label objects currently attached to the parent issue / PR
(`payload.labels`, populated on `labeled` / `unlabeled` actions).
Each element follows the same shape as the `label_*` columns. Stored as `array<json>` — unnest with `unnest(labels)` and extract per-element fields via `json_value`.

doc.dbt.__overview__.name: __overview__
doc.dbt.__overview__.block_contents:
### Welcome!

Welcome to the auto-generated documentation for your dbt project!

### Navigation

You can use the `Project` and `Database` navigation tabs on the left side of the window to explore the models
in your project.

#### Project Tab
The `Project` tab mirrors the directory structure of your dbt project. In this tab, you can see all of the
models defined in your dbt project, as well as models imported from dbt packages.

#### Database Tab
The `Database` tab also exposes your models, but in a format that looks more like a database explorer. This view
shows relations (tables and views) grouped into database schemas. Note that ephemeral models are _not_ shown
in this interface, as they do not exist in the database.

### Graph Exploration
You can click the blue icon on the bottom-right corner of the page to view the lineage graph of your models.

On model pages, you'll see the immediate parents and children of the model you're exploring. By clicking the `Expand`
button at the top-right of this lineage pane, you'll be able to see all of the models that are used to build,
or are built from, the model you're exploring.

Once expanded, you'll be able to use the `--select` and `--exclude` model selection syntax to filter the
models in the graph. For more information on model selection, check out the [dbt docs](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/model-selection-syntax).

Note that you can also right-click on models to interactively filter and explore the graph.

---

### More information

- [What is dbt](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/introduction)?
- Read the [dbt viewpoint](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/viewpoint)
- [Installation](https://docs.getdbt.com/docs/installation)
- Join the [dbt Community](https://www.getdbt.com/community/) for questions and discussion
